title: Quest duration contract: separate authored duration from expiry windows
description:
Why
Quest templates currently use `timeLimitDays` as expiry, but authored copy often implies execution duration. This causes multi-day investigations and escort/survival contracts to resolve in a single slot while the fiction promises longer work.

What
Introduce an explicit authored duration contract for quests so runtime can distinguish deadline, execution duration, and required beats. Cover investigation and local on-site contracts first.

Why now
Player-reported bug: `Three Days on Assessor Vorn` and `Safe Passage Through the Pale` can both complete on Day 1 evening, breaking trust in the work-board and activity log. This is a core player-facing contract failure.

Player impact
Quest text, pacing, and outcome timing align. A player no longer sees a three-day tail completed in one afternoon.

System impact
Quest templates, quest runtime progression, investigation flow, on-site contract flow, selectors, and tests.

Does not include
No new quest content. No board reskin. No combat redesign.

Acceptance
1. Multi-day authored quests cannot resolve before their declared execution duration has elapsed.
2. Deadlines and execution duration are distinct concepts in code and UI.
3. Regression tests cover same-day double-completion scenarios.

Finding coverage
F1: authored multi-day investigation resolves same day.
F2: escort/survival contract resolves in one watch despite longer implied work.
F3: two accepted quests can both settle in the same evening without clear duration gating.
notes:
Steps to reproduce
1. Start a fresh save on Day 1.
2. Discover and accept "Three Days on Assessor Vorn" and "Safe Passage Through the Pale".
3. Execute the survival contract on-site and run the investigation flow the same day.
4. Observe both contracts can settle on Day 1 evening despite authored text implying longer work.

Design direction
Separate deadline from execution duration. Runtime and UI should be able to represent both expiry and required execution time or beats without overloading timeLimitDays.

title: Make investigation quests consume time and honor authored surveillance duration
description:
Why
Investigation runtime currently resolves from approach selection to payout in a single resolve action. Authored surveillance quests like `Three Days on Assessor Vorn` promise multi-day tracking that the system does not enforce.

What
Add investigation duration beats and time consumption so surveillance-style investigations require multiple watches or days before settlement.

Why now
This is the clearest live trust break in the current quest set and the user hit it immediately on Day 1.

Player impact
Investigations feel like actual operations, not instant dice rolls.

System impact
Investigation reducers, runtime progress, time-slot advancement, investigation UI, quest templates, and tests.

Does not include
No rewrite of investigation skill math. No new clue content beyond what pacing requires.

Acceptance
1. `quest-compact-watch` cannot settle on the same afternoon it is accepted.
2. Investigation steps visibly advance across multiple watches/days when authored duration requires it.
3. Activity log and quest journal explain progress and remaining work.

Finding coverage
F1: `Three Days on Assessor Vorn` resolves same day despite three-day briefing.

title: Add event budget and first-day pacing guardrails to endDay
description:
## Why
A fresh-save `End Day` can surface roughly 23 pending events at once. The current queue covers event integrity and expiry cleanup, but not first-day pacing, event-budgeting, or queue-governance. This overwhelms the opening and turns authored world beats into noise.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start a fresh save on day 1.
2. Trigger `End Day` once from the dashboard.
3. Open the event modal / inspect pending events.
4. Observe that a large burst of pending events is queued immediately, overwhelming the opening flow.

## What
Add an end-of-day event scheduling policy with first-hour pacing limits, event-budget caps, and progressive disclosure so a single tick cannot flood the player with low-priority pending events.

## Why now
This is a first-session comprehension failure and a systemic quality hole. If `End Day` explodes the queue on day 1, the opening fantasy and every subsequent event chain lose clarity.

## Player impact
Ending day 1 produces a readable, prioritized set of events instead of an overwhelming dump. Important opening beats are surfaced first and minor world churn is deferred.

## System impact
Touches `endDay`, event evaluation/scheduling, possible event metadata for priority/deferability, and playthrough verification.

## Does not include
- no procedural quest generation expansion
- no broad rewrite of every authored event text

## Evidence
User report on 2026-05-17. Code evidence: `endDay.ts` chains many event-producing systems in one tick; `evaluateEvents.ts` only checks conditions/cooldown, not a day-budget. Existing first-hour playthrough does not assert event-volume limits after `End Day`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. **Player-visible:** A fresh-save day-1 `End Day` does not dump an overwhelming stack of pending events; opening events are paced and prioritized.
2. **System / technical:** The event pipeline enforces a documented budget/governance rule (caps, deferment, or priority queue) for first-hour and generic day ticks.
3. **Tests / verification:** Add regression coverage for fresh-save `End Day` event volume and priority behavior; `pnpm test:run`, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm build` must pass.

## Finding coverage
F3 — Day-1 `End Day` creates ~23 pending events.

title: Define canonical house political footing and fix day-one faction seat truth
description:
## Why
`/Users/andre.dittrich/privat/projects/project_destiny/src/ui/screens/FactionsScreen.tsx` currently computes `restoredWardSeats` by summing all `councilSeats`, which surfaces `Restored ward seats 7` on day 1. That contradicts the opening fiction that House Valdris begins stripped of power.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start a fresh save on day 1.
2. Open the Factions screen.
3. Read the `House Political Footing` section.
4. Observe that `Restored ward seats` is shown as `7` even though the opening fiction says the house lost its seats.

## What
Introduce a canonical political-footing selector contract that distinguishes city/faction chamber composition from House-controlled restored seats and sponsor channels, then update the Factions screen to use the correct values and wording.

## Why now
This is a day-one fiction contradiction on a core overview screen. It breaks the core fantasy of rebuilding from ruin and makes the world state untrustworthy.

## Player impact
The factions screen truthfully communicates how weak or connected the house actually is at the start, and only shows restored seats once they are genuinely earned.

## System impact
Touches political/renown selectors, the Factions screen, initial state assumptions, and screen/selector tests.

## Does not include
- no full politics rebalance
- no new council-vote content

## Evidence
User report on 2026-05-17. Code evidence: `restoredWardSeats = Object.values(councilSeats).reduce(...)` in `src/ui/screens/FactionsScreen.tsx`; initial state in `data/runtime/initial-game-state.json` seeds other factions with seats. Existing `OverviewScreens.test.tsx` does not assert day-1 restored-seat truth.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. **Player-visible:** On day 1 the Factions screen does not claim House Valdris has restored ward seats it has not earned; sponsor access and literal seats are clearly distinct.
2. **System / technical:** A selector or equivalent application contract exposes separate values for house-controlled restored seats, sponsor channels, and faction chamber composition.
3. **Tests / verification:** Add selector/screen regression tests for fresh-save political footing; `pnpm test:run`, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm build` must pass.

## Finding coverage
F2 — `Restored ward seats 7` on day 1.

## Fiction contract
House Valdris begins politically diminished. The UI must not imply restored institutional power before the player earns it.

title: Fix router-safe dashboard directive navigation and session continuity
description:
## Why
The dashboard still uses raw `<a href>` directive links in `/Users/andre.dittrich/privat/projects/project_destiny/src/ui/screens/DashboardScreen.tsx` for the Work Board and recommended quest actions. In SPA play this can trigger a hard navigation/bootstrap path that feels like the game restarted instead of preserving the current session.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start from a fresh save on the dashboard.
2. Ensure there is no active quest and the first-run Work Board CTA is visible.
3. Click `→ Check the Work Board`.
4. Observe whether the app hard-navigates / reloads and appears to restart rather than preserving the current in-memory session.

## What
Replace raw directive anchors on dashboard/work-board-facing surfaces with router-safe navigation and add an explicit route-continuity contract for fresh-save and mid-session flows.

## Why now
This is a live player-facing trust break in the first hour. If the player thinks a primary CTA restarts the game, all other quest and house improvements lose credibility.

## Player impact
Clicking `Check the Work Board` or the recommended quest action preserves the current session and takes the player to the expected surface without feeling like a reset.

## System impact
Touches `DashboardScreen`, any similar directive-link surfaces, route wiring/tests in React Router screens, and possibly shared CTA components.

## Does not include
- no quest-content rewrite
- no broader dashboard visual redesign

## Evidence
User report on 2026-05-17. Code evidence: raw `href` links in `src/ui/screens/DashboardScreen.tsx`. Current tests only assert presence/copy in `src/ui/screens/DashboardScreen.test.tsx`, not route continuity.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. **Player-visible:** From a fresh save and from an in-progress save, dashboard quest/work-board CTAs navigate in-app without resetting opening state or feeling like a restart.
2. **System / technical:** Dashboard directive links use router-safe navigation rather than raw anchor reload semantics.
3. **Tests / verification:** Add route-level regression tests for dashboard -> work board and dashboard -> recommended quest route; `pnpm test:run`, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm build` must pass.

## Finding coverage
F4 — Dashboard Work Board quick link appears to restart the game.

title: Add missing quest-gilded-court-favor and fix district-pale-warrens ID
description: Why: two broken content references: (1) event-gilded-court-approach references questId quest-gilded-court-favor which does not exist - quest lead silently fails to create; (2) an event uses sourceDistrictId district-pale-warrens which does not exist (valid IDs are district-the-pale and district-the-warrens). These are silent failures the player cannot diagnose. What: add quest-gilded-court-favor to quests.json OR remove the questId reference. Fix district ID to district-the-pale. Acceptance: no events.json or quests.json reference references an ID that does not exist in the catalog.

title: Standardise house name across all content — Valdric or Valdris
description: narrative.md uses 'House Valdric' (10 references, canonical founding history). data/definitions/npcs.json uses 'House Valdris' (12 references). data/definitions/events.json uses 'House Valdris' (1 reference). pursuePlayerLegacy.ts:129 uses 'House Valdris'. Decision: align all sources to one spelling. Recommendation: if narrative.md is the lore bible, use Valdric everywhere. Update: npcs.json (12 entries), events.json (1 entry), pursuePlayerLegacy.ts (heir originStory). Add a grep CI check that fails if both spellings appear simultaneously. Acceptance: only one spelling exists in the entire codebase and data directory.

title: Author item effects for item-medkit-field and 14 other items with no effects
description:
**Why:** 15 items in data/definitions/items.json have no effects authored:
- item-medkit-field (Field Medkit) — the most common healing consumable has no heal effect
- 5 trade goods (iron ingots, pre-breach mechanism, tallow bricks, salt block, coal sack) — economically inert
- 5 documents (bureau ledger, ledger removal chit, margin note, sealed envelope, lane watch sketch)
- 2 materials (scrap cloth, activated charcoal bricks)
- 1 gift (unfamiliar crest ring)
- 1 stimulant-style item is likely close to done but medkit is the critical gap

**What:**
1. Author effect for item-medkit-field: restoreHealth +25 or +30.
2. Author effects for remaining consumables (dressing kit, tincture, antitoxin, salve, poultice, tonic — check each).
3. For documents: author quest-flag or reveal effects where appropriate.
4. For trade goods: mark as sell-only (no use effect — they are economy goods, not consumables).
5. For the gift: author a relationship boost effect for gifting (once gifting system exists — flag as pending that system).

**Why now:** Players trying to use a medkit in combat or post-mission get no benefit. The medkit is probably the most commonly purchased item.

**Player impact:** Healing items work. Consumables have real value. Players understand what documents do.
**System impact:** items.json content only. No schema changes needed if effects field already exists.

**Non-goals:** Do not build the gifting mechanic here. Do not build the trading mechanic here.

**Acceptance:** All 15 items have either authored effects or a documented reason for having none. pnpm test:run passes.
close_reason: Authored effects for all 15 items: heal for medkit, tradeValue for 5 trade goods and 3 materials, enableAction for 5 quest documents, affinityBonus+enableAction for crest ring. Added tradeValue and enableAction types to itemEffectSchema.

title: Add House Merrow to factionStandings in initial game state
description:
**Why:** faction-house-merrow is defined in data/definitions/factions.json and described as the primary antagonist of the Valdris succession arc. It controls Ash Quay, has a daily agenda hook, and defines three existential fears (Orven Pell's ledger, Torvald Messe's copy, Brannic Thule's testimony). But it is entirely absent from factionStandings in initial-game-state.json. No code can track or change the player's standing with House Merrow.

**What:**
1. Add `faction-house-merrow` to `factionStandings` in data/runtime/initial-game-state.json with a starting value (suggest -10 to -20: they hold Valdris assets and have adversarial alignment).
2. Verify gameStateSchema allows the new key (it should use Record<string,number>).
3. Update any faction-list selectors if they filter to a known list.
4. Add House Merrow standing display to the Factions screen.

**Why now:** The antagonist faction being invisible in the tracking system is a narrative and mechanical gap. Quest consequences that affect Merrow cannot be applied or shown.

**Player impact:** Player can now see and track their Merrow standing. Quest rewards/penalties affecting Merrow become meaningful.
**System impact:** factionStandings gains one more key. Schema and initial state must stay in sync.

**Non-goals:** Do not author full Merrow quest arc here — just add the tracking infrastructure.

**Acceptance:** Factions screen shows House Merrow with correct starting standing. pnpm test:run passes. pnpm typecheck passes.

title: Fix ESLint errors: 5 active errors blocking CI confidence
description:
**Why:** 5 ESLint errors exist in production code — prefer-const, no-unused-vars (x3), no-useless-assignment — plus 1 warning. These are active CI failures and signal accumulated dead code.

**What:** Fix all 5 errors:
1. applyRumorSpread.ts:223 — change `let next` to `const next`
2. dialogue.ts:165 — remove or use `_removed`
3. grief.ts:36 — remove or use `_relationships`
4. gameSlice.ts:837 — fix `sellPrice` dead assignment (verify sell price logic is correct)
5. ItemCard.tsx:14 — remove or use `_instanceId`
Also remove unused eslint-disable in adjustRelationship.ts:26.

**Why now:** Lint errors erode confidence in the quality gates and mask real bugs. The sellPrice dead assignment may mean players are getting wrong sell prices.

**Player impact:** Fixing sellPrice may correct sell-price calculations. Rest is developer hygiene.
**System impact:** gameSlice.ts sell logic should be verified for correctness, not just linted clean.

**Non-goals:** Do not refactor beyond the lint fixes.

**Acceptance:** pnpm lint exits 0. pnpm test:run still passes 757+ tests.

title: Make Mira and house-story progression depend on player action instead of passive day gates
description: Why: the current story progression can advance Mira's arc through elapsed days and generic quest counts, including an automatic rescue-stage transition in political/world progression that bypasses the dedicated rescue quest. That breaks player agency and collapses the emotional weight of the main story. What: rework main-quest progression so story stages advance through explicit player-triggered leads, dialogues, discoveries, and completed story actions rather than passive timers alone.
design: The main story should use timers only for urgency and world reaction. They must not substitute for rescue, revelation, or confrontation.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Mira cannot be 'rescued' without the actual rescue path being completed. 2. Passive day or politics processing cannot silently move the story into a rescued state. 3. Story stages advance through explicit in-world actions or authored story beats. 4. Passive time can add pressure, rumors, or consequences, but cannot replace the core story action itself.

title: Quest rewards: rewardMarks and standing delta never applied on completion
description: CRITICAL BUG: Quest templates define rewardMarks, rewardStandingFactionId, rewardStandingDelta but these are never read when a quest completes. Players complete investigations and combats but receive zero Marks or standing. Only renown is applied. Fix: in the combat victory path and mission success path, read the linked quest template and apply rewardMarks to state.money, rewardStandingDelta to state.factionStandings, and log the reward. Also apply penaltyStandingDelta for expired quests (currently only missions get this).

title: Main quest: no endgame after 'rescued' stage
description: Main quest progresses searching→lead-found→location-known→rescued. After 'rescued', DashboardScreen shows 'Mira is out. She is not the same. Neither are you.' and nothing more happens. No epilogue content, no endgame quests, no new faction dynamics, no win state or reflection. The campaign just… continues as normal. Need: post-rescue content (epilogue quests, faction reshuffling, narrative payoff, or a soft win screen).

title: Institutional standing: cosmetic only, never enforced
description: institutionalStanding per faction (hostile/blacklisted/watched/neutral/allied) is set and logged but never checked downstream. Being 'blacklisted' with a faction should block their shop, prevent their NPC recruits, and gate faction quests. Being 'hostile' should trigger active antagonism events. Currently the entire reputation tier system is display-only. Need enforcement points: shop access check, quest filter, recruitment filter, and event triggers at hostile/blacklisted.

title: Delivery and survival quest types: no UI or mechanics
description: Quests with objectiveType 'delivery' and 'survival' exist in quests.json but ContractBoardScreen only routes to InvestigationScreen (investigation) or CombatScreen (combat). Accepting a delivery/survival quest creates an activeQuest entry with no way to complete it — permanent quest backlog clog. Need dedicated screens or wire these types into existing mission flows. ~20% of quest types are silently broken.

title: Vault quest chain: unlock path is opaque to player
description: The Vault (Cellar/Vault room) is locked and shows 'Sealed. Requires a key or a quest.' but players have no visible quest or hint about how to unlock it. The vaultUnlocked flag is only set by the unlockVault reducer which is never called from any current quest or event. The main quest narrative pivots on the vault but the path to unlocking it is a complete dead end. Need: (a) a quest or event chain that leads to unlockVault, (b) visible hint in the UI about how to proceed.

title: Fix shop navigation dead end in districts
description: Shops show 'travel to district' but provide no navigation link — a dead end UX flow. Add navigation links from shop UI to district view. Audit all district-related navigation for similar dead ends and fix.

title: Add tooltips to all stats, dials, and standing indicators
description: Zero tooltips exist on any stat bars, renown counter, city dials, or faction standing — players cannot learn what anything means. Add hover/tap tooltips to: all NPC attribute bars, renown (what it unlocks), city dials (what each affects), faction standing (current tier, next threshold, what it gates), equipment stats.

title: Dashboard cognitive overload: split into 3 focused zones
description: Dashboard has 18 sections causing severe cognitive overload. Split into 3 distinct zones: (1) Command Center — active quests, day controls, alerts; (2) Roster — NPC management, status, assignments; (3) City Overview — faction standings, district dials, economy. Add navigation between zones. Each zone should have at most 5-6 sections.

title: Marion Vale: expand to reactive companion arc
description: Marion Vale has only 3 dialogue nodes and no state-reactive content — the game's most prominent NPC is a placeholder. Expand to 10+ dialogue events tied to game state (debt level, faction standing, roster size, combat outcomes). Add 5 branching dialogue choices with relationship tracking. She should feel like a co-protagonist, not a quest board.

title: Faction standing: add concrete gates and consequences
description: Faction standings change based on quests but gate absolutely nothing — no district access, no exclusive hires, no quest availability. Wire standings to: district access locks, exclusive NPC hire offers, quest tier unlocks, dialogue options, and passive income grants. Make faction reputation feel meaningful.

title: Debt and economic pressure rebalancing
description: 500M debt is solvable in 2-3 sessions — no sustained threat after that. Once debt resolves there is no escalating pressure. Redesign: raise debt ceiling or shorten repayment window, introduce escalating threats (debt collectors, faction hostility, district deterioration) that ramp if ignored. Pressure must not fully resolve without player action.
close_reason: Raised debt to 800M, added interest >day15, added faction warning at day20

title: Wire city dials to economic and systemic consequences
description: City dials (Prosperity/Control/Unrest/Corruption) produce numbers but nothing reads them — they have no economic consequences. Faction standings change but gate nothing. Connect dials to: quest availability, hire costs, passive income rates, district event probabilities. This root cause (systems produce state but nothing consumes it) also applies to faction standing gates.

title: Enforce renown roster gating in recruitNpc
description: Renown-based roster size limits are defined but never enforced in recruitNpc — players can hire unlimited NPCs. Enforce the gate: check renown tier before hire, show NPC rarity in hire UI, surface the cap to the player so it feels like a meaningful progression gate.

title: Fix guard spam degenerate strategy
description: Guard action is a free action (no cost) with 55% damage reduction — a dominant, low-skill strategy that trivializes combat. Rebalance: add stamina/action cost to Guard, cap guard frequency per round, or reduce damage reduction to ~25-30%.

title: Wire dead combat mechanics: stagger flag and evasionPenalty
description: The 'staggered' status flag is set on enemies but never checked anywhere — dead code. The 'evasionPenalty' field on armor is never read or applied. Both represent mechanics scaffolding that was built but never connected. Wire them into the combat resolution loop.

title: Break up endDay.ts into focused command modules
description: endDay.ts is 969 lines handling 10+ independent systems (income, events, NPC state, faction drift, etc.) making it untestable and fragile. Extract each system into its own command/service module. Also contains 16 Math.random() calls making state non-deterministic — inject a seeded RNG to enable deterministic testing.

title: State versioning and save migration system
description: No state versioning exists — schema changes silently discard player saves. Need a versioned save schema with migration path so players don't lose progress on game updates. Implement a version field in Redux state, a migration registry, and upgrade logic run on load.

title: Onboarding: first-run directive after opening screen
description:
After OpeningScreen, player lands on Operations with zero direction. No tutorial, no CTA, no highlighted action. Activity log says 'Nothing has been logged yet'. 11-item nav. Player has no idea what to do.

UX SPECIALIST (most damaging issue): 'Lost players at session start are not recoverable. Every other issue is recoverable through iteration. Fix onboarding before anything else.'
GAME DESIGNER agrees: 'No feedback loop visible. Player cannot tell if management actions matter.'

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add isFirstRun flag to game state (set false after first End Day)
- On Operations, when isFirstRun=true: show a persistent directive banner
  'House Valdric has debts and one open contract. → Work Board'
- Highlight the Work Board nav item with a subtle pulse when unvisited
- Remove directive after player has accepted first quest
- Add one contextual log entry on day 1: 'Marion has reviewed the ledger. There is one contract available.'

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- New player sees a clear first action on Operations
- Directive disappears after first quest accepted
- No intrusive tutorial — one line, one arrow
close_reason: First-run directive banner renders on DashboardScreen when isFirstRun:true, links to /contracts, set false on endDay. 8 equip tests pass. Verifier finding fixed: slot buttons now show item names.

title: Implement equip gear — reducer, item-selection modal, selector export
description:
The Equip gear button in NpcDetailPanel.tsx has disabled hardcoded, no onClick, no reducer, no modal. Equipment cannot be assigned to any NPC. The loadout schema exists in game state but is completely invisible to player agency.

ALL SPECIALISTS agree this is P0.
GAME DESIGNER: 'Equipment is the primary external expression of NPC value. The player should feel that kitting out a character matters.'
UX SPECIALIST: 'False affordance — disabled button implies not-yet-unlocked or requires-items, not broken. Actively erodes trust in the UI.'

REQUIRED:
1. gameSlice.ts: Add equipItem(npcId, slot, itemId) reducer
2. selectRosterDetail: Export loadout in return value
3. NpcDetailPanel: onClick opens item-selection modal filtered by slot
4. ItemSelectionModal: lists owned weapons/armor/accessories, dispatches equipItem
5. Remove disabled attr from button

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Player can open modal, select an item, assign to primaryWeapon/armor slot
- Equipped item appears in durability panel
- Can unequip (set slot to null)
- Tests cover reducer
close_reason: ItemSelectionModal implemented with slot-filtered weapon/armor selection. Weapons/armor correctly show all catalog items (no inventory tracking for equipment yet — separate bead created). Verifier: item names now shown in slot buttons. 327 tests pass.

title: Dark fantasy typography — IM Fell English + Crimson Pro
description:
Zero web fonts. System fonts everywhere. Typography is the fabric of the entire UI — every name, header, badge, label.

ART DIRECTOR (escalated to P0): 'Font is applied to every character name, every screen title, every badge, every header. Switching takes 2 hours and transforms the aesthetic globally. The portrait problem takes months. Do the font first.'
GAME DESIGNER agrees: 'Low effort, high signal. This one is worth doing this week.'

IMPLEMENTATION (index.html + App.css):
1. Add to index.html:
   <link href='https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=IM+Fell+English:ital@0;1&family=Crimson+Pro:ital,wght@0,300;0,400;0,600;1,400&display=swap' rel='stylesheet'>
2. Add CSS tokens:
   --font-display: 'IM Fell English', Georgia, serif;
   --font-body: 'Crimson Pro', Palatino, serif;
3. Apply: h1,h2 + .npc-identity-name + .sidebar-brand + .opening-house-name → display font
4. Apply: body, .stat-label, .badge, .nav-link → body font

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Display font on all headings and NPC names
- Body font replaces system font everywhere
- No regression on readability at small sizes
- Build passes
close_reason: IM Fell English + Crimson Pro applied globally

title: Wire event outcomes — all 17 events have empty outcomes array
description:
All 17 events in events.json have outcomes:[]. Every player choice evaporates. No faction standing delta, no follow-up event, no log entry.

NARRATIVE DESIGNER (P0): 'Choices exist without consequences. This actively trains players to stop caring about events. After 2-3 events that lead nowhere, players stop reading them.'
GAME DESIGNER (P1, raised to P0 in synthesis): 'Core loop feedback void. The event system is theater not agency.'

MINIMUM OUTCOME STRUCTURE per choice:
{
  factionStanding: { faction-id: +/-N },
  followUpEventId: event-id or null,
  logEntry: authored text line
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Every event choice writes at least one state delta (standing, flag, or log)
- At least 5 events have a followUpEventId creating a 2-step chain
- evaluateEvents can condition on flags set by prior events
- No choice silently disappears
close_reason: Event outcomes wired on 17 events with faction standing deltas and log entries. Verifier passed.

title: P0: Production build fails — CSS media query malformed in App.css
description: App.css around line 749: media query block has a bare property (grid-template-columns: 1fr) with no selector, and an extra closing brace at ~line 790. lightningcss minifier aborts. pnpm build cannot complete. Fix the responsive block structure.
close_reason: Missing .app-shell selector in media query fixed. Build passes.

title: Critical bug sprint: schema conflicts, type safety, non-deterministic reducer
description:
Four critical bugs found in architecture audit that silently corrupt game state or bypass type safety.

1. Math.random() inside resolveCouncilVote reducer (gameSlice.ts) — Redux reducers must be deterministic. Move randomness to a thunk; pass pre-rolled outcome as action payload.

2. as-any cast on institutionalStanding tier (gameSlice.ts) — payload typed as string bypasses InstitutionalTier enum. Change to typed payload.

3. politicalDials vs cityDials — same schema, conflicting values, unclear source of truth. Merge into one field or document the split. Selector references cityDials; endDay patches cityDials.

4. factionStandings (flat Record) vs factionStates[].standingWithPlayer — two conflicting faction standing representations. factionStandings is authoritative. Stop updating standingWithPlayer everywhere.

5. applyEventOutcome casts outcome.target as keyof typeof cityDials without validation — runtime error on invalid dial name.

6. Zod parse on initial-game-state has no try/catch — app crashes with cryptic error on schema violation.
close_reason: All 6 critical bugs fixed: deterministic reducer, typed payload, unified dials, unified standings, validated cast, error boundary.

title: Simulation invariant suite: multi-day endDay run asserting event-system health
description:
## Why
The audit's strongest findings (spurious system events, burned content, unbounded queue, duplicate React keys) were found by a throwaway 40-day headless simulation — no existing test shape could catch them, and one unit test even asserted the burn as correct. This detection power must become permanent.

## What
A playthrough-suite scenario runs N days (>=40) of endDay from a fresh save (idle variant + clear-queue-daily variant) and asserts five named invariants. Each invariant is tied to the fix bead that turns it green and ships expected-red (test.todo or skipped-with-reason) until that bead merges:
1. no firingMode:'system' template enters pendingEvents via evaluateEvents — green with destiny-nflm
2. pending queue size stays under a documented bound — green with destiny-2udm
3. no non-repeatable event appears in lastFiredDay without having been pending — green with destiny-9hlx
4. activityLog ids unique across the run — green with destiny-fmy2
5. no eventInstance with resolvedOnDay null older than the expiry horizon — green with destiny-2udm

## Why now
Guards the rework while it happens; cheapest moment to encode the audit's lessons as executable checks.

## Player impact
Indirect: the day-2 modal flood class of regression cannot return unnoticed.

## System impact
src/application/playthrough/ (new scenario + invariant helpers), package.json script entry alongside test:playthrough:*.

## Does not include
- no fixes (engine epic owns them)
- no UI assertions beyond state-level key uniqueness

## Evidence
Audit method (reports/event_system_audit_report.md, verification section); L2 (wrong-invariant unit test). The throwaway prototype this bead recreates permanently is described there.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: n/a.
2. System/technical: suite runs via a pnpm script in CI-equivalent local gates; each invariant has a named, individually-failing assertion; expected-red invariants carry the blocking bead id in their skip reason.
3. Tests/verification: invariants 1 and 3 green once destiny-nflm + destiny-9hlx are merged (this bead's dependency point); 2/4/5 flip green with destiny-2udm/destiny-fmy2 and their skip markers are removed in THOSE beads' DoD; reverting destiny-9hlx turns exactly invariant 3 red (verified once, recorded in bead).

## Finding coverage
Prevention side of C1, C2, H1, J3; encodes L2's correction permanently.
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW — mid-tier; the playthrough runner pattern exists in src/application/playthrough/. The five invariants are fully specified above; no design decisions left.
Depends on: destiny-nflm, destiny-9hlx (for invariants 1+3 green; the suite itself may be developed in parallel with invariants expected-red).
Follow-up duty encoded here: destiny-2udm and destiny-fmy2 must flip their invariants green and remove the skip markers as part of THEIR DoD — note this in a comment on both when this bead closes.
Files you may touch: src/application/playthrough/**, package.json (scripts only).
Forbidden: production code changes, fixing failures found (report instead).
Definition of done: pnpm run <new script> green for invariants 1+3 post-deps; pnpm test:run unaffected; revert-check recorded.
Stop conditions: nested-worktree test discovery issues (see CLAUDE.md note) — treat as hygiene, report.

title: Catalog validator: outcome field matrix, enum targets, every ID reference — build fails on bad content
description:
## Why
The existing boot-time validator (destiny-ws6b) checks only outcome npcId/questId existence. It missed: npcId-spelled-as-target (both fields optional on the shared schema), invalid sourceDistrictIds, and cannot catch invalid dial/resource/corridor targets or missing required-field combinations. Two of these shipped and silently no-opped player rewards (audit C4/M3).

## What
contentCatalog validation extends to: (1) per-outcome-type required-field matrix (adjustNpcRelationship requires npcId+axis+delta; adjustCityDial requires valid dial target+delta; etc.); (2) enum targets validated (dials, resources, corridor values, axes); (3) every ID-bearing field on templates and conditions resolved against its catalog (sourceNpcId, sourceDistrictId, currentDistrict, requiredRosterNpcId, factionIds, quest stageIds); (4) clear multi-error report; build/startup fails on violations.

## Why now
Protects the entire rework: retrofit and content beads land on a hard gate instead of review vigilance. Direct gap-closure for destiny-dbyw.

## Player impact
Indirect: no more silently dead rewards from content typos.

## System impact
src/application/content/contentCatalog.ts (validator section), validator tests with deliberately broken fixtures.

## Does not include
- no fixing of content (destiny-g3eu owns the current fixes — land that first or together)
- no runtime warn layer (destiny-iyih)
- no CI pipeline changes beyond the existing startup/parse path

## Evidence
Audit C4/M3 escape analysis; contentCatalog.ts:172 (current checks); destiny-dbyw lesson (validator existed, classes uncovered).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: n/a (gate).
2. System/technical: each defect class from the audit, injected into a fixture copy of one template, fails validation with a message naming event id, choice id, and field.
3. Tests/verification: one red-fixture test per class (wrong-field-name, bad enum, bad district, bad stageId, missing delta); pnpm test:run && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build.

## Finding coverage
Prevention side of C4, M3, M2 (build-time half); destiny-dbyw gap closure.
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW — mid-tier; pattern exists in contentCatalog.ts:172ff, extend it. The required-field matrix is authored below; no design decisions remain.
REQUIRED-FIELD MATRIX (validate per outcome.type; unknown extra semantic fields like 'target' on npc-typed outcomes are violations):
- adjustFactionStanding: target must be a faction id in factions catalog; delta required number.
- adjustCityDial: target in {control, prosperity, unrest, corruption}; delta required.
- adjustCityResource: target in {foodSecurity, waterAccess, materialStock}; delta required.
- addCredits: delta required.
- addActivityLogEntry: message required non-empty.
- setCorridorStatus: value in {open, disrupted, blocked}.
- adjustNpcRelationship: npcId required and in npcs catalog; axis in {affinity, respect, fear, trust, loyalty}; delta required; presence of a 'target' key on this type is a VIOLATION (the C4 bug class).
- createQuestLead: questId required and in quests catalog.
- updateQuestStage: questId in quests catalog; stageId in that quest's stages; objectiveLabel/message optional.
- unlockNpc: npcId required and in npcs catalog.
- addNpcToRoster: npcId required and in npcs catalog; arcId optional (validate against npc-arcs definitions if present).
- transferBondedNpc: no required fields (context-driven at resolve time) — assert no stray target/delta.
- (forward-compat) adjustNpcState once destiny-vase lands: subject required (npcId:<id> with existing id, or known rule enum); axis in npc-state keys; delta required.
Template-level checks: sourceNpcId in npcs catalog when non-null; sourceDistrictId in districts catalog when non-null; triggerConditions.currentDistrict / requiredRosterNpcId / npcRelationshipMin.npcId / factionStanding*.factionId / activeQuestId resolved against their catalogs.
Files you may touch: src/application/content/contentCatalog.ts, its test file.
Forbidden: changing schemas (validation only), changing events.json (other beads own fixes).
Definition of done: red-fixture tests per matrix row class; pnpm test:run; pnpm typecheck; pnpm build.
Stop conditions: if validation finds NEW violations in current content beyond destiny-g3eu's four, list them in the bead and create follow-ups — do not fix silently.

title: adjustNpcState outcome with subject selection: events can affect the person they describe
description:
## Why
The outcome vocabulary cannot touch a roster NPC's state (stress/morale/loyalty/health): events about 'one of your people' compensate by nudging unrelated city dials (audit N5/N6). The runtime knows exactly who is stressed (states.stress) but no outcome can select or affect them.

## What
New outcome type adjustNpcState with: subject ('npcId:<id>' | 'highest-stress' | 'lowest-morale' | 'highest-loyalty' — extensible enum), axis (NPC state key), delta, clamping per state contract. Subject resolution happens at APPLY time inside applyOutcomes: a pure deterministic function over the current roster picks the subject when the outcome executes, and the activity-log line names the resolved NPC. (Fire-time subject resolution with name templating onto the event instance is explicitly OUT of scope here — it belongs to destiny-2udm, which owns instance creation; this bead must not depend on it.)

## Why now
Capability before content: blocks the retrofit bead and most Living-World content. The single pattern that turns anonymous events into living-NPC moments.

## Player impact
Indirect until retrofit: the engine can finally deliver 'your medic is exhausted' as a fact about a named person, and the log line names them.

## System impact
src/domain/events/contracts.ts (outcome schema), src/application/commands/applyEventOutcome.ts, tests. NOT evaluateEvents (apply-time resolution).

## Does not include
- no content edits (retrofit bead)
- no dialogue/topic outcomes (later vocabulary slice)
- no UI changes
- no fire-time subject templating into instance presentationText (destiny-2udm owns instances)

## Evidence
Audit N5/N6/N7 (reports/event_system_narrative_presentation.md): event-loyal-npc-milestone pays loyalty → city unrest moves; vocabulary inventory shows no NPC-state outcome exists; the runtime knows the subject, the event does not.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: the activity-log line produced by an adjustNpcState outcome names the resolved NPC ('Cress finally rests.' style, voice-matched).
2. System/technical: subject resolution deterministic and total (empty roster → outcome skipped with warn); state deltas clamp to the npc state contract bounds; resolution is a pure exported function reusable by destiny-tbia's npcState trigger condition.
3. Tests/verification: tests for each subject rule, clamping, empty-roster skip, determinism (same state → same subject; ties broken by stable order); pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts && pnpm typecheck.

## Finding coverage
N6 fully, N7 (mechanism), N5 (mechanism; content fixed in retrofit bead destiny-opv4).

## Fiction contract
When the world says someone, the runtime knows exactly who — and the consequence lands on that person, by name.
notes:
DELEGATION: RED — strong model (schema + applier changes; subject-resolution function design).
Blocked by destiny-nflm for hot-file sequencing only (applyEventOutcome.ts is also touched by destiny-gueb/destiny-iyih — coordinate, do not run in parallel with them).
Handoff duty: after merging, paste the exact adjustNpcState JSON shape and two worked examples into destiny-opv4 (its readiness criterion).
Definition of done: acceptance tests; pnpm test:run; pnpm typecheck; pnpm test:playthrough:golden.
Stop conditions: if subject resolution needs relationship data not on the roster entry, report; do not reach into selectors from a command.
close_reason: Implemented adjustNpcState outcome with deterministic subject resolution, clamping, named log output, and validator support.

title: Chronicle domain model: day-bucketed persistent history with eviction policy
description:
## Why
The game has no durable history model — only the 40-entry activityLog ring buffer (2 days of retention at day 7, measured live). Rich history already exists in runtime (eventInstances with chosenOptionId, lastResolvedEventSummary effect lines, quest journals, combat results) and is discarded or invisible. Journal audit J2/J7.

## What
A chronicle aggregate in src/domain/ (contracts.ts with Zod schema): day-bucketed entries with kind (scene/world/household/economy/combat/quest), actor/place references, headline, optional detail lines (the lastResolvedEventSummary playerEffects/npcEffects/worldEffects shape), and link target. Eviction by age/compaction policy, not entry count. GameState field + save migration + fixtures update. NO writers and NO UI in this bead (capability slice).

## Why now
Capability-first (bead-creation.md): writers (events, quests incl. destiny-y1et archive, combat) and the Journal UI all consume this model. First Chronicle-epic slice after the D1 bugfix.

## Player impact
None yet (enabling infrastructure) — the epic's player impact lands with writers + UI.

## System impact
src/domain/chronicle/contracts.ts (new), src/domain/game/contracts.ts, src/infrastructure/localSaveSnapshot.ts (migration), src/application/commands/testFixtures.ts.

## Does not include
- no writers (separate beads)
- no UI (separate bead)
- no activityLog removal (parallel until strangled)

## Evidence
Journal audit J2 (retention measured: oldest day 5 on day 7), J7 (inventory of discarded history sources), lastResolvedEventSummary shape in worldReducers.ts.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: n/a (capability bead; justified per bead-creation.md as enabling infrastructure).
2. System/technical: chronicleSchema validated in gameStateSchema; old saves migrate with empty chronicle; eviction policy is a pure function with explicit constants; entry shape supports the three-lens effect lines.
3. Tests/verification: schema round-trip test, migration default test, eviction policy tests (keeps N days, compacts older); pnpm test:run && pnpm typecheck.

## Finding coverage
J2, J7 (model side). J1/J4/J5/J6 land in the UI bead; J3 in destiny-D1.

## Fiction contract
The chronicle is the house's memory, kept in-world: entries name people and places, never systems.
notes:
DELEGATION: RED — strong model only (new domain aggregate + save migration).
Stop conditions: if eviction/compaction policy needs a product decision (how many days verbatim, what a compacted day keeps), propose a default in the bead notes and get sign-off before implementing UI-visible behavior — but the model itself may land with the proposed default.
Definition of done: migration test green; pnpm test:run; pnpm typecheck; fixtures updated so existing command tests stay green.

comments.author: André Dittrich
comments.text: Phantom-id correction (review finding): in Finding coverage read 'J3 in destiny-D1' as destiny-fmy2 (activityLog ID fix). Plan-internal codes A1/B1/D1/D2 map to: A1=destiny-nflm, B1=destiny-vase, D1=destiny-fmy2, D2=destiny-9hd6.

title: activityLog: unique entry IDs and cap aligned with docs
description:
## Why
activityLog.ts:14 builds entry ids from the OLD array length; once the log is at cap (40), every new entry within the same day+slot gets the identical id (e.g. log-7-morning-41). Verified live: 3 duplicate ids in a day-7 save, 37 React key errors on the Journal screen, React explicitly warns entries may be duplicated or omitted. Also: MAX_ACTIVITY_ENTRIES is 40 while CLAUDE.md documents 100 (doc drift), and rosterReducers.ts builds its own id format manually.

## What
DECIDED VARIANT (no schema change, no migration): the id embeds a per-day sequence derived from existing entries — count entries already present for the same day, e.g. log-<day>-<timeSlot>-<sameDayCount+1>; collision-free because eviction only removes OLDER days' entries (verify this assumption in the test; if same-day eviction is possible at cap, fall back to <day>-<slot>-<maxExistingSeqForDay+1>). rosterReducers.ts uses the same exported helper instead of hand-building ids. MAX_ACTIVITY_ENTRIES is raised to 100, matching CLAUDE.md — after which the doc needs NO edit (destiny-sbha verifies, does not edit, the cap claim).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Play to day 7+ (log at cap), open /event-log.
2. Browser console: 'Encountered two children with the same key, log-7-morning-41'.

## Why now
Active rendering corruption on a shipped screen; two-file fix; independent of all other event work.

## Player impact
The Journal stops silently dropping/duplicating lines.

## System impact
src/application/commands/activityLog.ts, src/application/store/slices/rosterReducers.ts, tests. NO schema change, NO migration, NO CLAUDE.md edit (cap matches doc after the constant change).

## Does not include
- no chronicle model, no journal UI changes, no category redesign
- no persisted counter field (decided against: avoids schema+migration)

## Evidence
Audit J3 (reports/journal_report.md): duplicateIds ['log-5-morning-41','log-6-morning-41','log-7-morning-41'] in live save; 37 console errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: /event-log renders with zero React key warnings at any log size.
2. System/technical: ids provably unique across appends at cap; rosterReducers uses the shared helper; MAX_ACTIVITY_ENTRIES === 100 and CLAUDE.md needs no change.
3. Tests/verification: test 'appending 50 entries in one day+slot yields 50 unique ids'; test covering the at-cap eviction path; pnpm exec vitest run on the touched test files; pnpm typecheck.

## Finding coverage
J3 fully; resolves the CLAUDE.md cap drift by raising the constant (destiny-sbha drops its cap-edit item accordingly).
notes:
DELEGATION: GREEN — the variant is decided (derived per-day sequence, no schema, no migration); zero design decisions remain.
Files you may touch: src/application/commands/activityLog.ts, src/application/store/slices/rosterReducers.ts, co-located test files.
Template to imitate: appendActivityLogEntry itself.
Forbidden: changing entry shape, schema edits, touching EventLogScreen, reordering log semantics, CLAUDE.md edits.
Definition of done: (1) uniqueness tests pass incl. at-cap path; (2) pnpm test:run; (3) pnpm typecheck; (4) open /event-log at cap with zero console key errors (screenshot in bead).
Stop conditions: if the same-day-eviction assumption fails in practice (uniqueness test red), report with the failing case — do not invent a third variant.

title: Content hotfix: npcId-vs-target in two milestone events, two invalid sourceDistrictIds
description:
## Why
Two authored events use "target" instead of "npcId" on adjustNpcRelationship outcomes; applyOutcomes requires npcId, so the trust rewards have never applied — silently, because the existence check only runs when npcId is present. Two other events reference districts that do not exist.

## What
Four JSON values in data/definitions/events.json are corrected. No code changes.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Build a state where event-marion-milestone-motivation resolves (or call applyOutcomes with its choice outcomes directly).
2. Inspect relationships[player|npc-marion-vale].trust before/after: unchanged — the outcome silently no-ops because npcId is undefined (the value sits in the unused target field).

## Why now
Player-facing rewards (Marion/Doyle milestone trust) are silent no-ops today; two-line fix.

## Player impact
The Marion and Doyle milestone scenes actually deepen trust as their text promises.

## System impact
data/definitions/events.json only.

## Does not include
- no validator work (Prevention epic owns the gate that would have caught this)
- no other content edits

## Evidence
Audit C4 + M3 with exact locations:
1. event-marion-milestone-motivation: outcome {"type":"adjustNpcRelationship","target":"npc-marion-vale",...} → rename key target to npcId.
2. event-doyle-milestone-holst: TWO outcomes with "target":"npc-garet-doyle" → rename key to npcId in both.
3. event-gilded-court-approach: "sourceDistrictId":"district-gilded-quarter" → "district-gilded-heights".
4. event-ring-smuggler-contact: "sourceDistrictId":"district-pale-warrens" → "district-the-warrens".
Valid district ids: see data/definitions/districts.json.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: resolving the Marion milestone event raises player-Marion trust by the authored delta (assert via applyOutcomes test or manual save inspection).
2. System/technical: grep '"target": "npc-' data/definitions/events.json returns nothing; both sourceDistrictIds exist in districts.json.
3. Tests/verification: pnpm test:run green (catalog parse), pnpm typecheck.

## Finding coverage
C4 fully, M3 fully (fix side; prevention side lives in the validator bead).
notes:
DELEGATION: GREEN — safe for a lower-tier model.
Files you may touch: data/definitions/events.json — nothing else.
Template to imitate: any adjustNpcRelationship outcome that already uses npcId (e.g. in event-marion-first-success).
Forbidden: any other edits to the file beyond the four listed values; code changes; reformatting.
Definition of done: (1) the four exact edits from Evidence applied; (2) pnpm test:run passes; (3) pnpm typecheck passes.
Stop conditions: if any of the four locations does not match the description, report instead of guessing.

comments.author: André Dittrich
comments.text: Review nit accepted: acceptance 1's 'applyOutcomes test' is outside the allowed file list — verification path is acceptance 2's grep plus the catalog-parse suite (pnpm test:run). Bonus context for the implementer: worldReducers.ts:75 masks this bug by falling back outcome.npcId ?? outcome.target when building the player-facing summary — the player was TOLD trust deepened while nothing applied. Do not 'fix' that fallback here; it becomes dead code once the JSON is corrected.

title: Truncated events are not burned: lastFiredDay only records events that became pending
description:
## Why
evaluateEvents.ts:61-91 writes ALL eligible events into lastFiredDay but only queues 5 regular ones. A non-repeatable event that loses the day's lottery is permanently consumed without ever being shown (isOnCooldown returns true forever). Simulation proof: event-heir-announcement is burned on day 2 of every playthrough; 17-19 events burned in 40 days. The code comment claims RNG determinism requires this — it does not: determinism comes from consuming rng() uniformly, which already happens during condition checks.

## What
lastFiredDay records exactly the events added to newPending. Truncated events stay eligible and compete again next tick. The misleading comment is rewritten. The existing test that enshrines the wrong behavior ('populates lastFiredDay after evaluateEvents runs') is updated to assert the new contract.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Fresh save (isFirstRun false to skip tutorials), run endDay once: more than 5 regular events are eligible on day 2.
2. Inspect state.lastFiredDay: it contains event ids (e.g. event-heir-announcement) that are NOT in state.pendingEvents.
3. Run endDay for 40 days: event-heir-announcement never fires again (non-repeatable + lastFiredDay set) although the player never saw it.

## Why now
Silent permanent loss of authored content, every playthrough, invisible to the player and to tests (the current test asserts the bug). Finding C2.

## Player impact
Events that the world generated actually reach the player eventually instead of vanishing.

## System impact
src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts, evaluateEvents.test.ts only.

## Does not include
- no change to the per-tick budget value (5)
- no priority/ordering changes
- no firingMode work (separate bead)

## Evidence
Audit C2 + L2 (reports/event_system_audit_report.md). 40-day simulation: 17 burned events incl. non-repeatable event-heir-announcement on day 2 (method described in the report; reproduce with steps above).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: an eligible non-repeatable event truncated on day N can fire on day N+1 (test-proven; observable as the event eventually appearing).
2. System/technical: lastFiredDay keys after evaluateEvents === pendingEvents additions; WITHIN A SINGLE TICK the rng consumption order is unchanged (same seed → same chosen subset for the first 5 on that tick). NOTE: later days legitimately diverge from old saves because previously-burned events re-enter the pool — that is the point of the fix, not a regression.
3. Tests/verification: new test 'does not record lastFiredDay for truncated events'; updated test for the new contract replaces the old assertion; existing budget tests stay green. pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test:playthrough:golden.

## Finding coverage
C2 fully, L2 fully (the wrong-invariant test is corrected here).
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW — mid-tier model with this contract.
Files you may touch: src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts, src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts
Template to imitate: existing structure of evaluateEvents (keep eligible[] loop; move newLastFiredDay assignment after slice).
Forbidden: Math.random, changing MAX_REGULAR_EVENTS_PER_TICK, schema edits, edits outside file list.
Definition of done: (1) it('does not record lastFiredDay for truncated events') passes; (2) it('truncated event can fire on a later day') passes; (3) pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts; (4) pnpm typecheck; (5) pnpm test:playthrough:golden.
Stop conditions: if the golden playthrough breaks because the multi-day seed sequence legitimately changed, report with the diff — do not silently regenerate fixtures. If priority/isFirstRun handling becomes ambiguous, report; do not redesign the budget.

title: Add firingMode to event templates and exclude system events from evaluateEvents
description:
## Why
evaluateEvents scans all 127 templates in events.json, but ~14 systems (recognizeHeir, tickWardStages, applyNpcPairing, bondService, captivityPregnancyDiscovery, applySiteStateHooks, simulateRivalOrgs, applyNpcConsequences milestones) push their events into pendingEvents directly. Their templates carry no real trigger gate, so evaluateEvents fires them spuriously: verified live, a fresh save shows bond-service scenes on day 2 with zero bonded NPCs. Some templates defend with the implicit probability:0 convention; others forgot it.

## What
eventTemplateSchema gains firingMode: 'world' | 'system' (default 'world'). evaluateEvents skips firingMode==='system'. All system-pushed templates are flagged 'system' and their probability:0 hack values removed. checkRelationshipMilestones' defensive double-write of the eventId into lastFiredDay (applyNpcConsequences.ts:71-77) becomes unnecessary ONLY because firingMode now provides the same protection — remove it in the same change and keep the existing regression test green (evaluateEvents.test.ts: 'does not generically reschedule the Marion milestone event after it already fired') by relying on firingMode, not on the double-write.

## Why now
Root cause of audit finding C1, the most player-visible defect. Blocks the typology presentation slice (destiny-73ji), destiny-i702, the trigger-schema work, and the simulation invariant suite.

## Player impact
Day 2 no longer opens with scenes about bonded workers, freed contracts, ward growth, or heirs that do not exist.

## System impact
src/domain/events/contracts.ts (schema), src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts, applyNpcConsequences.ts (remove workaround), data/definitions/events.json (flag templates), evaluateEvents.test.ts.

## Does not include
- no change to how owning systems push their events
- no queue/instance changes (separate bead)
- no removal of the per-tick budget

## Evidence
Audit C1/L1 (reports/event_system_audit_report.md). Must-flag list, system-pushed templates: event-bound-npc-notices-difference, event-npc-freed, event-title-npc-bond-objection, event-captivity-pregnancy-discovery, event-heir-announcement, event-npc-pairing-noticed, event-npc-pairing-pregnancy-discovery, event-ward-stage-infant-to-child, event-ward-stage-child-to-teenager, event-ward-stage-teenager-to-young-adult, event-ida-milestone-contact, event-doyle-milestone-holst, event-vael-milestone-network, event-elyn-arrival, AND event-marion-milestone-motivation (pushed by checkRelationshipMilestones like the other three milestones — NOTE: this one is NOT caught by the probability:0 grep below because it carries probability 1.0 and real trust conditions; flag it explicitly). Additionally flag every template currently using probability 0 (the implicit convention): grep '"probability": 0' data/definitions/events.json. Cross-check completeness: every eventId pushed into pendingEvents by a command (grep -rn "pendingEvents" src/application --include='*.ts' | grep -v test) must map to a firingMode:'system' template or be a non-template key.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: fresh save, end day 1-4: no firingMode:'system' template ever appears in the modal unless its owning system pushed it (playthrough assertion).
2. System/technical: schema default keeps old saves valid (firingMode defaults 'world'); every template in the must-flag list plus every probability:0 template is flagged 'system'; no template uses probability 0 anymore; evaluateEvents has no knowledge of individual template ids; the applyNpcConsequences double-write is removed.
3. Tests/verification: new tests — 'system templates are never fired by evaluateEvents even with always-true conditions'; 'world templates unaffected'; existing milestone regression test stays green WITHOUT the double-write; pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test:playthrough:golden.

## Finding coverage
C1 fully, L1 fully, H3 partially (removes one ad-hoc workaround).

## Fiction contract
A scene authored for a system (heir recognition, ward growth, bond service) appears only when that system says its premise is true.
notes:
DELEGATION: RED — strong model only (schema + cross-system migration).
Files you may touch: src/domain/events/contracts.ts, src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts, src/application/commands/applyNpcConsequences.ts, data/definitions/events.json, src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts
Template to imitate: existing optional schema fields with .default() in eventTemplateSchema; isOnCooldown() guard style in evaluateEvents.
Forbidden: Math.random, touching gameSlice.ts, renaming existing fields, edits outside file list.
Definition of done: (1) it('never fires firingMode system templates via evaluateEvents') passes; (2) pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts; (3) pnpm typecheck; (4) pnpm test:playthrough:golden.
Stop conditions: if a pushed eventId has no template in events.json, report instead of inventing one.

title: Prevention Infrastructure: content gates, simulation invariants, dead-content checks
description:
## Why
The 2026-06-12 audits found defect classes that existing guardrails missed by design: the catalog validator checks only npcId/questId on outcomes (so npcId-vs-target and invalid sourceDistrictId shipped), a unit test enshrined the burn-on-truncation bug as correct behavior, conventions lived in magic values (probability:0 = system-driven), and schema fields shipped without consumers (presentationFlavour authored on 23 events, rendered nowhere). Root causes are systemic, not per-feature.

## What
Four guardrail layers: (1) catalog validator extended to outcome field matrices, enum targets, and all ID references; (2) a permanent multi-day simulation invariant suite; (3) a dead-content audit (schema fields/flags without complete code paths); (4) workflow gates: fresh-eyes playthrough evidence required in player-facing beads, plus docs updates encoding the lessons.

## Why now
Must start immediately: it protects the event/journal rework itself while it happens, and every future feature inherits the gates.

## Player impact
Indirect but decisive: broken premises, silent no-op choices, and authored-but-invisible content stop reaching the player.

## System impact
src/application/content/contentCatalog.ts (validator), src/application/playthrough/ (invariant suite), docs/task-contract.md, docs/workflows/bead-creation.md, docs/engineering-standards.md, CLAUDE.md.

## Does not include
- no production behavior changes (validators and tests only)
- no fixing of the defects themselves (owned by the other epics)

## Evidence
2026-06-12 audits: C4/M3 escaped destiny-ws6b's validator; L2 (test asserts wrong invariant); L1 (probability:0 convention); presentationFlavour dead field; isAutoResolved half-wired (H2); destiny-44ci as same defect class (dead risk systems).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: n/a (infrastructure) — justified as enabling per bead-creation.md.
2. System/technical: validator fails the build on every defect class found in the audit (field matrix, enums, all ID refs); simulation suite asserts queue bounds, no system-event leakage, no unseen burn; docs encode the new rules.
3. Tests/verification: deliberately broken fixture content fails the validator; invariant suite green on main after engine fixes land.

## Success Criteria
- Re-running the 2026-06-12 audit finds zero reproducible findings of the same classes.
- A new feature cannot ship a value-encoded convention, an unconsumed schema field, or an unvalidated content reference without a red gate.

## Finding coverage
C4/M3 (prevention side), L1, L2, H2/H3 (convention/namespace documentation), J3 (invariant), plus the five root causes in Plan_Event-System_Journal_Epic.md.

title: Chronicle: durable day-by-day history and a Journal worth reading
description:
## Why
The journal audit (2026-06-12) found the Journal screen has no player value: a 40-entry ring buffer retains ~2 days of history, 100% of entries are stamped morning, 90% carry the system badge, an ID-collision bug makes React drop/duplicate entries, half the screen is a permanently empty Combat Recap pane, and the rich history the game already records (eventInstances with chosenOptionId, quest journals, combat results) is shown nowhere.

## What
A chronicle domain concept: day-bucketed, persisted history fed by event resolutions (including the player's choice and consequence summary), quest beats, and combat results; the Journal screen becomes a grouped, filterable, linked timeline. The activityLog bug fixes land immediately and independently.

## Why now
56% of event outcomes are log-only — the chronicle is the only witness of most world changes, and it currently forgets them in 2 days. Also resolves the swallowed-summary problem at the surface level: missed modal summaries become readable history. destiny-y1et (failed-quest archive) needs this surface to be visible.

## Player impact
The player can answer: what happened, what did I decide, what changed, what should I follow up on — for the whole campaign, not 2 days.

## System impact
src/domain/ (new chronicle contracts), src/application/commands (writers), selectors/eventLog.ts replacement, src/ui/screens/EventLogScreen.tsx rebuild, activityLog.ts fixes, save migration.

## Does not include
- no event engine changes
- no quest settlement changes beyond consuming destiny-y1et's archive
- no morning-report surface (destiny-i702)

## Evidence
2026-06-12 journal audit J1-J7 (journal_report.md): activityLog.ts:3 (cap 40 vs CLAUDE.md claim 100), activityLog.ts:14 (ID collision, 37 live React errors), EventLogScreen.tsx, combat.ts:345 (recap data destroyed), save inspection (retention day 5-7 on day 7).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: on day 30 the Journal still shows day 1; entries are grouped by day, filterable, and resolved events show the choice that was taken.
2. System/technical: chronicle is a versioned, migrated GameState field with day-based eviction/compaction; activityLog IDs are unique; Combat Recap pane removed or repurposed.
3. Tests/verification: writer tests per source, migration default test, screen test for grouping and choice display; no duplicate React keys (invariant test).

## Success Criteria
- The Journal answers what happened / what did I decide / what next for the full campaign.
- One name for the surface (Journal) across nav, heading, route naming in code.
- No permanently empty panel on the screen.

## Finding coverage
J1-J7 of the 2026-06-12 journal audit; surfaces C3 (swallowed summaries) historically.

## Fiction contract
The Record is the house's memory. What the house lived through, decided, and paid for is never silently forgotten.

title: Event Outcome Vocabulary: events can keep their promises
description:
## Why
The narrative audit (2026-06-12) found events promise personal consequences the outcome vocabulary cannot deliver: a 30-Mark loyalty bonus adjusts the CITY unrest dial instead of anyone's loyalty; buying 60 Marks of supplies grants no supplies; 208 of 344 authored outcomes are bare activity-log lines. There is no outcome type that targets a roster NPC's state, grants items, or opens a conversation.

## What
The outcome vocabulary gains: NPC-state adjustment with subject selection (named NPC or rule like highest-stress), resource/item grants, and route-opening outcomes (dialogue topic / quest lead). Existing dishonest events are retrofitted to use them.

## Why now
Capability-before-content (bead-creation.md preferred shape): the Living-World content epic and any retrofit depend on this vocabulary existing first. Fixes false promises already visible to the player.

## Player impact
When an event says your people are affected, your people are visibly affected. Paid choices deliver what they name. Character scenes can open conversations instead of dead-ending.

## System impact
src/domain/events/contracts.ts (outcome schema), src/application/commands/applyEventOutcome.ts, selected entries in data/definitions/events.json, tests.

## Does not include
- no engine/queue changes (Event Engine Integrity epic)
- no UI changes
- no new event content beyond retrofitting existing dishonest events

## Evidence
2026-06-12 narrative audit findings N4, N5, N6, N7 (Plan_Event-System_Journal_Epic.md): event-loyal-npc-milestone, event-stressed-npc-warning, event-market-price-spike concrete cases; outcome usage stats (208/344 log-only).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: resolving the loyalty-bonus choice visibly raises the targeted NPC's loyalty; the stressed-NPC event names the actual highest-stress NPC and lowers their stress.
2. System/technical: new outcome types validated by schema + applyOutcomes with warn-and-skip on bad refs; subject selection is deterministic (rngSeed convention).
3. Tests/verification: one test per new outcome type incl. clamping and missing-ref skip; retrofit events covered by content validation.

## Success Criteria
- No shipped event text promises a consequence the outcome list cannot produce.
- Events about roster NPCs select and name a real roster NPC.
- The outcome vocabulary supports state, goods, and route consequences, not only dials and log lines.

## Finding coverage
N4, N5, N6, N7 of the 2026-06-12 narrative audit.

## Fiction contract
A choice label is a promise. Whatever the label names — money spent, loyalty rewarded, supplies bought — must exist in the runtime afterwards.

title: Event Engine Integrity: system-event exclusion, fair event budget, unified instances
description:
## Why
The 2026-06-12 event-system audit found the engine fires system-driven events spuriously (bond/ward/pairing/heir scenes on day 2 with no matching world state), permanently burns eligible-but-truncated events into lastFiredDay (event-heir-announcement is consumed unseen in every playthrough), keeps two half-connected event models (pendingEvents vs eventInstances), and lets the pending queue grow unbounded (36 modals after 40 idle days).

## What
After this epic: evaluateEvents only considers world events; system events fire exclusively through their owning systems; no event is marked fired unless the player could actually see it; one instance model carries provenance and resolution history; the queue is bounded and events expire.

## Why now
Every other event workstream (outcome vocabulary, presentation, living-world content, morning report destiny-i702) builds on engine correctness. New event content on the current engine inherits all four defect classes.

## Player impact
No more context-free scenes about people who do not exist. Milestone and heir scenes arrive when they are earned. The world stops silently discarding authored content.

## System impact
src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts, applyEventOutcome.ts, endDay.ts, store/slices/worldReducers.ts, src/domain/events/contracts.ts (schema + migration), data/definitions/events.json, evaluateEvents.test.ts.

## Does not include
- no new outcome types (Epic: Event Outcome Vocabulary)
- no presentation/UI changes (destiny-73ji)
- no new event content (Living-World epic)
- no chronicle/journal work

## Evidence
2026-06-12 audit findings C1, C2, H1, H2, H3, H4, M1, M2, L1, L2 (engine report; see Plan_Event-System_Journal_Epic.md). Verified live via Playwright fresh-save playthrough and a 40-day headless endDay simulation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: a fresh save reaches day 5 without a single bond/ward/pairing/heir/captivity event firing via evaluateEvents (verified by playthrough scenario).
2. System/technical: eventTemplateSchema carries an explicit firingMode; the probability:0 convention is gone; lastFiredDay only records events that became pending; pendingEvents derive from or coexist consistently with eventInstances.
3. Tests/verification: simulation invariant suite (Prevention epic) green; pnpm test:run, typecheck, build pass.

## Success Criteria
- A fresh save never shows a scene whose premise does not exist in the runtime.
- Non-repeatable authored content is never consumed without being shown.
- One event instance model; bounded, expiring queue.
- The probability:0 convention is replaced by an explicit schema contract.

## Finding coverage
C1, C2, H1, H2, H3 (partial: namespace doc), H4, M1, M2, L1, L2 of the 2026-06-12 engine audit.

## Fiction contract
An event is a specific world or character reaction to a situation that actually exists in the runtime. The engine must make it impossible to show the player a scene whose premise is false.

title: Quest deadlines and debt crisis are skipped when time passes via wait/sleep (path-dependent midnight logic)
description:
## Why
Whether time-limited quests expire and whether the debt crisis fires depends on WHICH button the player uses to pass time. There are two parallel midnight implementations that have already drifted.

## Root cause (G1)
`timeReducers.ts` has `runEndOfDay` (endDay command + expireTimedQuestsOnState + debt-crisis check), used only by the `endDay` and `advanceTimeSlot` reducers. But `wait`, `sleepBrief`, and `sleepToMorning` reducers delegate to `advanceTimeSlotInState`/sleep commands in timeAdvance.ts, which call the bare `endDay` command — NO quest expiry, NO debt-crisis check. DashboardScreen's Wait button uses `gameActions.wait()`, so a player who passes midnight by waiting (or any future sleep UI) never triggers contract expiry or the debt seizure, while a player using advanceTimeSlot/endDay does.

## What
Crossing midnight runs identical end-of-day consequences (quest expiry, debt crisis, prune leads) regardless of which action advanced the clock; one shared code path owns the midnight transition.

## Why now
Deadline pressure and the debt clock are core tension mechanics; both are currently unenforceable via the most accessible time-passing button, and the duplicated slot-advance logic guarantees further drift.

## Player impact
Time limits and the debt due date actually mean something; consistent world reaction to passing time.

## System impact
src/application/store/slices/timeReducers.ts, src/application/commands/timeAdvance.ts (consolidate midnight handling, likely move expiry+debt into the endDay command or a single end-of-day orchestrator), tests for each time-advance path.

## Does not include
No changes to what endDay simulates; no rebalancing of timeLimitDays; no UI changes.

## Evidence
timeReducers.ts:11-32 (runEndOfDay) vs 60-73 (wait/sleep bypassing it); timeAdvance.ts:11-23; DashboardScreen.tsx:71; endDay.ts has no expireTimedQuestsOnState/debt logic (only pruneExpiredQuestLeads).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Accept a quest with timeLimitDays, note debtDueDay.
2. Pass days exclusively via Dashboard 'Wait' until both deadlines are exceeded.
3. Observe the quest never expires and the debt crisis never triggers; do the same via the status-bar End Day button and both fire.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: a timed quest expires on the same in-game day no matter how midnight was crossed; debt crisis fires on schedule via every path.
2. System/technical: exactly one function owns end-of-day consequences; wait/sleep/advanceTimeSlot/endDay all route through it (list all four entry points per the 'Cannot X' rule).
3. Tests: one test per time-advance action asserting quest expiry and debt-crisis trigger at midnight crossing.

## Finding coverage
G1 from 2026-06-12 quest-system audit. Related: [[destiny-vdb0]] (expiry race in same-click advance+resolve).

comments.author: André Dittrich
comments.text: 2026-06-12 coordination note: the event engine epic destiny-fidq (esp. destiny-nflm, destiny-9hlx, destiny-c5wo) edits endDay.ts and evaluateEvents.ts. This bug's midnight/path-dependency fix touches the same day-advance flow — do not work them in parallel on the same files; sequence whichever starts second behind the first.

title: Multi-day investigation quests can never progress past day 1 (quest-compact-watch unwinnable)
description:
## Why
Multi-day investigation quests (executionDurationDays > 1, e.g. quest-compact-watch 'Three Days on Assessor Vorn') are unwinnable through the real UI flow. Progress sticks at 'Surveillance day 1 of 3 logged' forever. Confirmed by a manual playthrough report (2026-06-12).

## Root cause (F1)
`resolveInvestigation` (src/application/store/slices/questReducers.ts:204) de-dupes per-day progress via `alreadyWorkedToday = surveillanceDaysLogged > 0 && runtime.progress.lastAdvancedDay === state.day` (lines 249-254). But `lastAdvancedDay` is overloaded as a generic 'last touched' stamp:
- `startInvestigation` (line 173) stamps `lastAdvancedDay = state.day` every time it runs
- `updateQuestRuntime` (line 77) stamps it unconditionally on any update
Because `resolveInvestigation` nulls `state.activeInvestigation` after each surveillance day (line 275), the player MUST re-dispatch `startInvestigation` the next day — which re-stamps `lastAdvancedDay` to today and makes the guard treat every subsequent day as 'already worked'. Day 1 only logs because `surveillanceDaysLogged === 0` bypasses the guard.

Edge case that makes it fully unwinnable (F6): the only accidental escape is running during the night slot (InvestigationScreen handleRun dispatches `advanceTimeSlot` BEFORE `resolveInvestigation`, so midnight crossing bumps state.day past the stamp). But `advanceTimeSlot` also runs `expireTimedQuestsOnState` (timeReducers.ts:14), and quest-compact-watch has timeLimitDays=3 == executionDurationDays=3, so the final night-slot crossing expires/fails the quest before the third day can resolve.

## Secondary defects
- F2 journal duplication: each daily restart re-appends the start journal entry (startInvestigation line 174) and the approach clueText (chooseInvestigationApproach line 200) unconditionally → duplicated entries observed in playthrough.
- F5 wasted slot + no feedback: a same-day re-run still consumes a time slot and operative assignment, produces no journal entry, no activity log, and no on-screen explanation; only hint is the objective label 'Continue surveillance tomorrow'.
- F4 test gap: investigation.test.ts (lines 277-321) injects `readyInvestigation` state and hand-sets `progress`/`lastAdvancedDay` directly, bypassing the real startInvestigation→chooseApproach→resolve daily loop, so the regression is invisible to the suite.

## What
