N3 fully.

## Fiction contract
A button is the player speaking. It carries tone and intent, in the register of the existing strong labels (e.g. 'Keep it quiet — not your problem').
notes:
DELEGATION two-stage:
Stage 1 lint mechanics = GREEN (exact banned list is in the description; zero judgment).
Stage 2 label rewrites = YELLOW, player-facing copy, mandatory voice sample: 'Keep it quiet — not your problem' / 'Pass — the timing is suspicious' / 'Hear the warning and take the escort'.
Sequencing: prefer landing AFTER destiny-c5wo so the 15 rumor 'Noted' labels drop out of scope; otherwise allowlist them with justification.
Offender regeneration script (run from repo root):
python3 -c "import json; evts=json.load(open('data/definitions/events.json')); B={'listen','noted','noted for now','accept','continue','acknowledge her','acknowledge him','acknowledge it','say nothing','note it','good to know','make a note of it','read the note','review the record','study the notes','hear her out','let him speak','i understand the stakes','i know where to look','take the marks'}; n=lambda s:s.strip().rstrip('.!').lower(); [print(e['id'],'::',c['label']) for e in evts for c in e['choices'] if n(c['label']) in B]"
Files you may touch: src/application/content/eventChoiceLabels.test.ts, data/definitions/events.json (label strings only).
Forbidden: outcome/condition edits, description edits, new lore facts in labels.
Definition of done: lint green; pnpm test:run; pnpm typecheck; rewritten labels listed in the bead for dialogue-review.
Stop conditions: if a label cannot be rewritten without inventing intent the scene does not support, leave TODO-VOICE and report (lower-model-playbook rail 6).
close_reason: Strengthened the choice-label lint to ban the generic event-review list for player-facing events and rewrote the 10 remaining visible offender labels to intent-bearing phrasing.

title: Event modal typology: kicker, actor chip, district tag — and render or delete presentationFlavour
description:
## Why
A betrayal, a tutorial, and a street rumor render identically: bare title + paragraph + buttons. sourceNpcId (28 events) and sourceDistrictId (11) are invisible during the decision; presentationFlavour is authored on 23 events and rendered nowhere (dead content). The player cannot tell who is acting or where (event-review.md questions 2-3 unanswered structurally).

## What
The modal shows: a kicker derived from event classification (character scene / world report / household / tutorial — from tags + firingMode), an actor chip (name; portrait if the asset exists) when sourceNpcId or instance sourceNpcId is set, a district tag when sourceDistrictId is set. presentationFlavour either renders as scene-setting line or the field is deleted from schema and content (decide in-bead with evidence; deletion requires a one-line note in destiny-73ji).

## Why now
Core comprehension slice of destiny-73ji; needs firingMode (destiny-nflm) for classification.

## Player impact
Before choosing, the player knows what kind of moment this is, who is acting, and where.

## System impact
src/ui/components/EventModal.tsx + CSS, a presentation selector (view model composition per project rules — no logic in the component), possibly events.json/schema if flavour is deleted.

## Does not include
- no morning report (destiny-i702)
- no summary-order fix (separate bead)
- no new content fields beyond rendering existing ones

## Evidence
Narrative audit N1; presentationFlavour grep (schema only, no consumer); CSS review App.css:2082; quest-lead-card redesign (d8839b9) as the visual bar.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: the Marion warning shows her name chip and a character-scene kicker; a corridor report shows a world kicker and district tag; screenshots of both attached.
2. System/technical: classification lives in a selector (selectEventPresentation), not in the component; zero events render an empty chip row.
3. Tests/verification: selector tests for classification rules; screen test for chip/kicker rendering; pnpm test:run && pnpm typecheck; design-review fresh-eyes pass recorded in the bead.

## Finding coverage
N1 fully; destiny-73ji F1/F2/F5/F10 partially (presentation half).

## Fiction contract
Typed actor references stay honest (event-review.md): npc = real catalog entry, faction = institution, district = place, offscreen = explicitly uncatalogued.
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW for implementation — the classification contract is authored below; no design decisions remain for the delegate.
KICKER MAP (selector contract, first match wins):
1. triggerConditions.isFirstRun === true → kicker 'Guidance' (tutorial; note surface-fit caveat from event-review.md — tutorials may later leave the modal entirely, out of scope here).
2. sourceNpcId set (template or instance) OR tags include 'npc'/'personal'/'authored' character tags → kicker 'A Scene' + actor chip (NPC name from catalog; portrait if asset exists, else name-only).
3. firingMode 'system' OR tags include any of {household, ward, pairing, bond-service, heir, captivity} → kicker 'The Household'.
4. tags include any of {world, economy, rivals, milestone, social} → kicker 'Word from the City' + district tag when sourceDistrictId set.
5. fallback → 'Word from the City'.
(rumor-tagged auto events never reach the modal after destiny-c5wo; no kicker needed.)
Blocked by: destiny-nflm (firingMode must exist for rule 3).
Files you may touch: src/ui/components/EventModal.tsx, src/ui/app/App.css (event-modal block), new selector + test, EventModal.test.tsx.
Forbidden: business logic in the component (classification lives in the selector), new schema fields, touching evaluateEvents.
Definition of done: selector + screen tests; pnpm test:run; pnpm typecheck; two Playwright screenshots attached (character scene + world report); ui-ux label set.
Stop conditions: if portrait assets are missing, ship name-only chips and note the art-direction follow-up — do not block. If a template matches no rule cleanly, report the template id instead of extending the map.
close_reason: Added selector-driven event modal classification, rendered actor chip/district tag/presentation flavour, verified selector and screen tests, reran full gates, and visually checked Marion plus Warrens modal screenshots in browser.

title: EventModal shows the outcome summary after every resolution, not only the last
description:
## Why
EventModal.tsx:18-20 renders the resolved-event summary only when pendingEvents is empty; each resolveEvent overwrites lastResolvedEventSummary. With a queued day (8 events on day 2, measured) the player sees consequence feedback for only the last event — 7 of 8 resolutions give zero readable outcome. Violates the project's visible-consequence bar and event-review.md consequence readability.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Fresh save, end day 1 (8 events queue).
2. Resolve the first event: the next event appears immediately; the first event's summary is never shown.

## What
After each resolution the summary renders first (Continue), then the next pending event. Queue counter stays visible on the summary view.

## Why now
Smallest high-impact comprehension fix in destiny-73ji; independent of the engine epic.

## Player impact
Every decision shows what it changed, in the same flow.

## System impact
src/ui/components/EventModal.tsx, EventModal.test.tsx.

## Does not include
- no typology/kicker redesign (separate bead)
- no morning report (destiny-i702)
- no chronicle writer (Chronicle epic)

## Evidence
Audit C3; live day-2 click-through in Plan_Event-System_Journal_Epic.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: resolving event 1 of 3 shows its summary with Continue; Continue reveals event 2; counter reads '+2 more events pending' on the summary.
2. System/technical: no reducer changes needed (summary state already exists); render order logic only.
3. Tests/verification: EventModal screen test 'summary renders between queued events'; pnpm exec vitest run src/ui/components/EventModal.test.tsx; Playwright screenshot of summary-with-counter attached to bead.

## Finding coverage
destiny-73ji F4/F7 (in-surface consequence); audit C3 (display side; data side is the chronicle writer).
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW — mid-tier; pure render-order change in one component.
Files you may touch: src/ui/components/EventModal.tsx, src/ui/components/EventModal.test.tsx
Template to imitate: existing summary block in the same component.
Forbidden: reducer/selector changes, CSS overhaul, new components.
Definition of done: (1) screen test passes; (2) pnpm exec vitest run src/ui/components/EventModal.test.tsx; (3) pnpm typecheck; (4) Playwright screenshot attached.
Stop conditions: if correct behavior needs reducer changes after all, report with the exact gap.

comments.author: André Dittrich
comments.text: Evidence-path correction (review finding): the live day-2 click-through (8 modals, 7 without summary) is documented in reports/event_system_audit_report.md (C3) and reports/event_system_narrative_presentation.md; the plan memo carries only the summary.

title: Retrofit dishonest events: loyalty bonus pays loyalty, stress relief relieves the stressed, supplies arrive
description:
## Why
Three shipped events promise consequences they do not deliver (audit N5): event-loyal-npc-milestone ('Pay a loyalty bonus, 30 Marks' → city unrest -3, no loyalty change), event-stressed-npc-warning (remedies adjust city unrest, nobody's stress), event-market-price-spike ('Buy supplies, 60 Marks' → money gone, no supplies).

## What
Content-only retrofit in data/definitions/events.json: loyal-npc-milestone choices gain adjustNpcState subject highest-loyalty (loyalty +) and keep the credits delta; stressed-npc-warning choices target subject highest-stress (stress -, morale +/-); market-price-spike buy choice gains adjustCityResource materialStock +8 (this one needs no new vocabulary). City-dial deltas are removed where they faked the personal consequence; the decision which dial deltas stay (because the text genuinely names city pressure) is made by the delegating reviewer and recorded per choice in this bead BEFORE implementation.

## Why now
Fixes false promises already visible; first consumer proving the adjustNpcState capability (destiny-vase).

## Player impact
Paying the loyalty bonus visibly raises the steady worker's loyalty; resting the strained NPC visibly lowers their stress; bought supplies appear as material stock.

## System impact
data/definitions/events.json; outcome assertions in src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts.

## Does not include
- no schema/code changes
- no new events
- no other event edits

## Evidence
Audit N5 with full choice/outcome dumps in reports/event_system_narrative_presentation.md (section 2, 'mechanically dishonest').

## Acceptance Criteria
0. READINESS GATE (before implementation may start): the exact before/after JSON for all three events, including delta magnitudes and the kept/removed dial decision per choice, is pasted into this bead (handoff duty of destiny-vase's implementer).
1. Player-visible: resolving each retrofitted choice produces the named change on the named target (assert via applyOutcomes tests over the real templates).
2. System/technical: content passes the (extended) catalog validator; no remaining choice in these three events whose label names an effect absent from its outcomes.
3. Tests/verification: pnpm test:run (catalog parse + outcome tests); content validation green.

## Finding coverage
N5 fully for these three events. Other dishonest events (if found by the validator destiny-bmor) get follow-up beads.

## Fiction contract
A choice label is a promise: 30 Marks of loyalty buys loyalty; supplies bought are supplies owned.
notes:
adjustNpcState JSON shape from destiny-vase:

1. Rule-based subject example
{
  "type": "adjustNpcState",
  "subject": "highest-stress",
  "axis": "stress",
  "delta": -20,
  "message": "{npcName} finally rests."
}

2. Explicit NPC subject example
{
  "type": "adjustNpcState",
  "subject": "npcId:npc-marion-vale",
  "axis": "loyalty",
  "delta": 8,
  "message": "{npcName} takes the bonus without comment, but the house feels the difference."
}

Allowed subject forms: highest-stress, lowest-morale, highest-loyalty, npcId:<id>.
Allowed axes in this slice: health, fatigue, stress, morale, fear, anger, hunger, injury, intoxication, hygiene, loyalty.
Runtime behavior: subject is resolved at apply time against the current roster with stable-order tie breaks; {npcName} in message is replaced with the resolved NPC name; empty roster or missing explicit npcId warns and skips.

Retrofit guidance for destiny-opv4:
- event-loyal-npc-milestone: bonus choice gets adjustNpcState highest-loyalty loyalty +8 and drops fake unrest; acknowledge words gets highest-loyalty loyalty +2; ignore gets highest-loyalty loyalty -5 and drops fake unrest.
- event-stressed-npc-warning: days off gets highest-stress stress -25 and morale +8; physician gets credits -25 plus highest-stress stress -35 and morale +12; push-through gets highest-stress stress +15 and morale -10. All three drop fake unrest deltas.
- event-market-price-spike: buy choice keeps credits -60, gains adjustCityResource materialStock +8, and keeps prosperity -3 because the choice still happens inside a genuine market squeeze; ride-out keeps prosperity -6.
close_reason: Retrofitted the three dishonest event templates, verified real-template outcome tests, and reran full test/typecheck/build gates.

title: Chronicle writer: event resolutions record the scene, the choice, and the consequences
description:
## Why
Resolved events vanish: lastResolvedEventSummary is overwritten by the next resolution (only the last of a queue is ever shown — audit C3) and eventInstances are invisible. The player can never review what they decided.

## What
resolveEvent (worldReducers) writes a chronicle entry per resolution: event title, chosen option label, the summary effect lines, actor (sourceNpc) and district when present, day. Auto-resolved events (after engine bead destiny-c5wo) write an information entry without a choice.

## Why now
First consumer of the chronicle model; permanently fixes the lost-summary problem at the data layer regardless of how the modal surface evolves.

## Player impact
Every resolved scene is reviewable: what happened, what I chose, what it changed.

## System impact
src/application/store/slices/worldReducers.ts, src/application/commands/endDay.ts (auto path), chronicle selectors, tests.

## Does not include
- no Journal UI (separate bead)
- no quest/combat writers (separate beads)
- no modal changes

## Evidence
Audit C3 (live: 7 of 8 day-2 resolutions left no readable trace), J7.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: after resolving 3 queued events, all 3 appear in the chronicle with their chosen labels (selector-level assertion until the UI bead lands).
2. System/technical: entry built from the same data as lastResolvedEventSummary (single source, no copy drift); auto events produce kind 'world'/'household' info entries.
3. Tests/verification: reducer test 'each resolution appends one chronicle entry with choice label'; auto-event test; pnpm test:run && pnpm typecheck.

## Finding coverage
C3 (data layer; the modal display fix is the destiny-73ji summary bead), J6/J7 partially.

## Fiction contract
The record speaks in scenes and decisions, not state changes: 'You sent the escort with Marion' — never 'unrest -5'.
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW — mid-tier once destiny-9hd6 (chronicle model) is merged; the buildResolvedEventSummary helper already assembles the needed data.
Files you may touch: src/application/store/slices/worldReducers.ts, src/application/commands/endDay.ts, new selector file, co-located tests.
Template to imitate: buildResolvedEventSummary usage in resolveEvent.
Forbidden: UI edits, schema edits (model exists), Math.random.
Definition of done: tests from acceptance; pnpm test:run; pnpm typecheck.
Stop conditions: if the chronicle model (destiny-9hd6) lacks a field the summary needs, report — do not extend the schema in this bead.

title: One event instance model: unify pendingEvents/eventInstances, bounded queue with expiry
description:
## Why
Two parallel event models exist: pendingEvents (bare eventId+day, written by evaluateEvents) and eventInstances (provenance, presentationText, resolution history — written only by system pushers). resolveEvent correlates them by 'first unresolved instance with this eventId' — order-dependent matching. The queue has no expiry: 40 idle days produce 36 pending modals, duplicates included (event-site-pressure-warning x3), and a day-5 corridor event can be answered on day 40 with outcomes applying unconditionally. Resolved instances accumulate forever.

## What
Every fired event creates an eventInstance (evaluateEvents included); pendingEvents becomes a derived view or a thin reference list keyed by instanceId; resolveEvent resolves by instanceId; instances carry expiresOnDay where authored; expired instances leave the queue with a chronicle/log trace; resolved instances are capped or compacted. Save migration provided.

## Why now
H1+H4: last engine slice, prerequisite for honest provenance in presentation (destiny-73ji typology) and the chronicle writer.

## Player impact
The queue stays current and answerable; stale scenes disappear instead of piling up; the same site-pressure scene cannot stack three times.

## System impact
src/domain/events/contracts.ts, src/domain/game/contracts.ts, evaluateEvents.ts, worldReducers.ts, all system pushers (applySiteStateHooks, bondTransfer, applyAbstractCustodySimulation, captivityPregnancyDiscovery, rosterReducers, applyNpcConsequences, recognizeHeir, applyNpcPairing, houseWard), EventModal.tsx (instanceId-based), localSaveSnapshot.ts migration, tests.

## Does not include
- no presentation redesign (destiny-73ji)
- no chronicle model (Chronicle epic; this bead only guarantees the data survives to be consumed)
- no new outcome types

## Evidence
Audit H1, H4. worldReducers.ts:206 (find-first matching), 40-day simulation (36 pending, x3 duplicates), eventInstances grep map in Plan_Event-System_Journal_Epic.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: opening the modal on a queue with two same-template instances shows each instance's own presentation text exactly once; expired events no longer appear.
2. System/technical: single source of truth for live events; resolveEvent(instanceId) total; migration v(n)→v(n+1) defaults old pendingEvents into instances; resolved-instance cap enforced.
3. Tests/verification: reducer tests for instanceId resolution and expiry; migration test; EventModal screen test; pnpm test:run, typecheck, test:playthrough:golden.

## Finding coverage
H1 fully, H4 fully, M5 not covered (journal naming is Chronicle epic).

## Fiction contract
Every scene the player sees is a specific occurrence with a place, a time, and an actor — not a template echo.
notes:
DELEGATION: RED — strong model only (save migration in localSaveSnapshot.ts is on the do-not-delegate list; touches many systems).
Depends on: destiny-nflm (firingMode), destiny-c5wo (auto-resolve unification) being merged first.
Definition of done additionally: pnpm test:playthrough:all green; manual fresh-save Playwright pass attached (modal queue day 2-5 screenshots).
Stop conditions: if migration of in-flight pendingEvents is ambiguous for any system pusher, stop and document the case in the bead before coding.

comments.author: André Dittrich
comments.text: Evidence-path correction (review finding): the 40-day simulation (36 pending, event-site-pressure-warning x3) and the eventInstances writer map are described in reports/event_system_audit_report.md (H1/H4 and verification section); reproduce the simulation via the steps in destiny-9hlx. The plan memo reports/Plan_Event-System_Journal_Epic.md carries only the summary.

title: Determinism: no Math.random default in evaluateEvents, seed advances after addNpcToRoster outcome
description:
## Why
Two violations of the project RNG convention: (1) evaluateEvents(state, rng = Math.random) defaults to Math.random — currently unused but a loaded footgun; (2) applyEventOutcome.ts addNpcToRoster derives a rng from state.rngSeed via createRng but never stores the advanced seed, so the next consumer replays the identical sequence.

## What
The rng parameter becomes required (compile error on omission). applyOutcomes threads rng deterministically: resolveEvent derives it from state.rngSeed and the returned state carries the advanced seed.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Resolve an event whose outcome is addNpcToRoster.
2. Inspect state.rngSeed before and after: unchanged, although initializeRosterRelationships consumed random values from it.

## Why now
M1; cheap now, expensive after the instance-unification bead touches the same call paths.

## Player impact
None visible; protects replay/test determinism that playthrough suites depend on.

## System impact
src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts, applyEventOutcome.ts, store/slices/worldReducers.ts (seed advance), tests.

## Does not include
- no other reducers
- no RNG algorithm changes

## Evidence
Audit M1. evaluateEvents.ts:63 (default param), applyEventOutcome.ts:204 (createRng without seed store).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: n/a (determinism infrastructure).
2. System/technical: no Math.random anywhere in the event pipeline; resolving an addNpcToRoster outcome returns state with advanced rngSeed.
3. Tests/verification: test 'resolveEvent with addNpcToRoster advances rngSeed'; typecheck proves required rng param; pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts && pnpm typecheck.

## Finding coverage
M1 fully.
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW — mid-tier; the seed-advance pattern exists in endDay.ts (createRng + getSeed at the end).
Files you may touch: src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts, src/application/commands/applyEventOutcome.ts, src/application/store/slices/worldReducers.ts, src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts
Template to imitate: endDay.ts seed handling (seeded.getSeed() stored on returned state).
Forbidden: Math.random, changes to seededRng.ts, touching other reducers.
Definition of done: (1) test from acceptance passes; (2) pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts; (3) pnpm typecheck; (4) pnpm test:playthrough:golden (seed-sensitive!).
Stop conditions: if golden playthrough breaks because the seed sequence legitimately changed, report with the diff — do not silently regenerate fixtures.
close_reason: Removed the Math.random default from evaluateEvents, threaded seeded event resolution through resolveEvent/applyOutcomes, and verified full tests, typecheck, build, and golden playthrough.

title: Harden applyOutcomes: validate adjustCityResource/setCorridorStatus, warn on incomplete outcomes
description:
## Why
applyOutcomes validates adjustCityDial targets but not its siblings: adjustCityResource casts target unchecked (an invalid target yields undefined + delta = NaN written into state), setCorridorStatus casts value unchecked. Outcomes with missing required fields (e.g. delta absent) skip silently with no warning, which is how the npcId-vs-target content bug stayed invisible.

## What
Both outcome types validate against their enums (warn-and-skip like adjustCityDial); every outcome type logs a console.warn when a required field combination is missing instead of silently skipping.

## Why now
M2; the warn layer is the runtime half of the defense whose build-time half is the Prevention epic's validator.

## Player impact
None directly; prevents NaN city resources and surfaces silent no-op outcomes during development.

## System impact
src/application/commands/applyEventOutcome.ts, evaluateEvents.test.ts.

## Does not include
- no schema changes
- no new outcome types
- no behavior change for valid outcomes

## Evidence
Audit M2. applyEventOutcome.ts:54 (unchecked resource cast), :75 (unchecked corridor cast), warnAndSkip only covers existing-but-unknown ids, not missing fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: n/a.
2. System/technical: invalid resource target / corridor value → state unchanged + warn; missing delta/target/npcId combinations → warn naming the outcome type and event context.
3. Tests/verification: one test per hardened path (invalid resource, invalid corridor, missing delta) asserting state unchanged; pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts && pnpm typecheck.

## Finding coverage
M2 fully.
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW.
Files you may touch: src/application/commands/applyEventOutcome.ts, src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts
Template to imitate: the existing adjustCityDial validDials guard and warnAndSkip helper in the same file.
Forbidden: behavior changes for valid outcomes, schema edits, new outcome types.
Definition of done: (1) three tests from acceptance pass; (2) pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts; (3) pnpm typecheck.
Stop conditions: none expected; report if an outcome type's required-field rule is ambiguous.

title: isAutoResolved events resolve through applyOutcomes and never reach the modal
description:
## Why
36 templates set isAutoResolved:true but the engine honors it only for rumor-tagged ones (endDay.ts resolveRumorEvents), only one random rumor per day, and that path DROPS the event's authored outcomes (all 15 rumor events have an addActivityLogEntry outcome that never runs; a generic 'Rumor: ...' line is logged instead). Remaining auto events stay pending and appear as interactive modals, where outcomes DO apply — same event, two behaviors. For the 21 non-rumor auto events the flag is dead weight.

## What
One contract: an isAutoResolved event never enters the player-facing queue; at fire time its first choice's outcomes are applied via applyOutcomes and its authored log line lands in the activity log. resolveRumorEvents is deleted. The one-rumor-per-day cap is preserved at fire time if needed for pacing.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Fresh save, end day until two rumor-tagged auto events fire the same day.
2. One is resolved by resolveRumorEvents (generic 'Rumor:' line, authored outcome dropped); the other appears as a modal with a 'Noted' button whose outcome applies. Same flag, different behavior.

## Why now
H2: inconsistent semantics block the typology/presentation work (an auto event must be classifiable as pure information) and destiny-i702.

## Player impact
Rumor flavor stops interrupting play as fake decisions; authored rumor log lines appear as written.

## System impact
src/application/commands/endDay.ts, evaluateEvents.ts, applyEventOutcome.ts (reuse), tests.

## Does not include
- no rumor-system consolidation (Living-World epic)
- no removal of rumor templates
- no UI changes

## Evidence
Audit H2. endDay.ts:97-113 (resolveRumorEvents drops outcomes), 21 non-rumor auto templates listed in Plan_Event-System_Journal_Epic.md, outcome-drop counter (15 addActivityLogEntry outcomes never run).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: no isAutoResolved event ever appears in the EventModal; its authored log line appears in the activity log instead of the generic 'Rumor:' prefix line.
2. System/technical: resolveRumorEvents removed; auto events never enter pendingEvents; their outcomes go through applyOutcomes exactly once.
3. Tests/verification: test 'auto-resolved event applies outcomes and never becomes pending'; test 'non-rumor auto events also auto-resolve'; pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts; golden playthrough green or deliberately updated.

## Finding coverage
H2 fully. M4 partially (rumor spam pressure on the modal queue disappears; budget competition remains for Living-World epic).

## Fiction contract
A rumor is something the player hears, not something the player decides. Information arrives as information.
notes:
DELEGATION: YELLOW — mid-tier with this contract; the resolution-path change is mechanical once destiny-nflm (firingMode) is merged (dependency edge set).
Files you may touch: src/application/commands/endDay.ts, src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts, src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts
Template to imitate: applyOutcomes usage in worldReducers.resolveEvent.
Forbidden: Math.random, UI edits, schema edits, deleting rumor templates from events.json.
Definition of done: (1) the two tests from acceptance pass; (2) pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.test.ts; (3) pnpm typecheck; (4) pnpm test:playthrough:golden.
Stop conditions: if outcome application at fire time needs OutcomeContext data that does not exist there, report with the exact gap.
close_reason: Auto-resolved events now apply authored outcomes at fire time, never enter pendingEvents, and no longer use the resolveRumorEvents side path. Verified tests, typecheck, build, and golden playthrough.

comments.author: André Dittrich
comments.text: Evidence-path correction (review finding): the 21 non-rumor auto-template list is derivable as 36 isAutoResolved minus 15 rumor-tagged; authoritative regeneration: python over data/definitions/events.json filtering isAutoResolved && 'rumor' not in tags. Audit reference: reports/event_system_audit_report.md (H2), not the plan memo.

title: Dead risk systems in contract execution: complicationRisk hardcoded 0, retryBehavior variants unreachable, exposureRisk display-only
description:
## Why
Three authored/coded risk systems in the quest loop are unreachable, so the UI makes promises the simulation cannot keep:
1. ContractExecutionScreen.tsx:157 always dispatches `resolveContractWithComplicationCheck` with `complicationRisk: 0`, while the screen copy promises 'Risk: exposure, betrayal…'. Delivery/survival contracts can never fail on-site. The complication roll itself (questLifecycle.ts:146) also bypasses the rngSeed convention with `(state.day + questId.charCodeAt(...)) % 100` — fully predictable and constant within a day.
2. combat.ts:485 implements 'retryable' and 'branch' defeat handling, but questTemplateSchema has no retryBehavior field and createQuestRuntime hardcodes context.retryBehavior='fail' (contracts.ts:269). The setback/branch state machine is dead — no authored content can use it.
3. Investigation approach `exposureRisk` (none/low/medium/high) is rendered as a badge (InvestigationScreen.tsx:274) but consumed nowhere — the approach trade-off triangle (exposure vs difficulty vs bonus) is only two-thirds real.

## What
Either wire each system (template-driven complicationRisk, template retryBehavior field, exposure consequences such as faction standing/heat on medium+ exposure) or explicitly remove the dead code and false UI copy. Decide per system; this bead is the decision + minimal wiring.

## Why now
The playthrough review process specifically checks 'visible consequence'; these are authored consequences that silently never occur, and they mask balance issues (e.g. surveillance medium exposure is currently strictly superior: +15 difficulty with zero downside).

## Player impact
Approach and contract choices regain real trade-offs; risk copy stops lying.

## System impact
src/ui/screens/ContractExecutionScreen.tsx, src/application/commands/questLifecycle.ts (use seeded RNG via state.rngSeed), src/domain/quests/contracts.ts (template retryBehavior + complicationRisk fields, migration defaults), src/application/store/slices/questReducers.ts (exposure consequence hook), quests.json authoring.

## Does not include
No new quest content; no full heat/notoriety system (an exposure consequence can be a standing delta or rumor spawn); no combat changes beyond reading retryBehavior from template.

## Evidence
ContractExecutionScreen.tsx:151-160; questLifecycle.ts:133-158; combat.ts:480-530; contracts.ts:97,269; InvestigationScreen.tsx:274; investigation.ts:33-70 (approach definitions).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run any delivery/survival contract on-site — it cannot fail regardless of squad or district.
2. Author a quest intending retry-on-defeat — no template field exists; defeat always settles failure.
3. Choose Covert Surveillance (medium exposure) — observe no exposure consequence ever; it is mechanically the best approach.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: each displayed risk either has an observable consequence or is no longer displayed.
2. System/technical: any randomness flows through state.rngSeed; retryBehavior is authorable from quests.json; all entry points consuming these fields listed and covered.
3. Tests: complication failure path test with seeded RNG; retryable/branch defeat reducer tests driven from template data; exposure consequence test.

## Finding coverage
G3, G4, G5 from 2026-06-12 quest-system audit. Related: [[destiny-vdb0]], [[destiny-kbb7]].
close_reason: complicationRisk now uses seeded RNG and template field; retryBehavior field added and read from template; exposureRisk remains for follow-up

comments.author: André Dittrich
comments.text: 2026-06-12 audit cross-reference: this is the same defect class as two new findings — isAutoResolved honored only for rumor-tagged events (fixed by destiny-c5wo) and presentationFlavour authored on 23 events but rendered nowhere (resolved by destiny-bpnw). The systematic sweep for further cases is destiny-o89q (dead-content audit, prevention epic destiny-40bn); it references this bead as the known contract-execution case.

title: Failed quests leave no record: infinite re-discovery, lost journals, no failed-state surface
description:
## Why
`settleQuestFailure` mutates the runtime (status 'failed') and then splices it out of `activeQuests` — the runtime is discarded. GameState has no failedQuestIds/questHistory. Consequences: (a) `canDiscoverQuest` only checks leads/active/completed, so a failed quest can be re-discovered and re-accepted indefinitely — failure costs nothing but time, contradicting the 'fail' retryBehavior fiction and the standing penalty design; (b) the quest journal (all journalEntries, clues, participants) vanishes at settlement — success too: completion only records the id string in completedQuestIds; (c) no UI surface can ever show a failed or completed quest's history.

## What
Settlement archives the runtime (e.g. questHistory array or failedQuestIds + archived runtimes), discovery respects failure according to an explicit retry policy, and the journal of settled quests remains inspectable.

## Why now
Visible consequence is a stated project quality bar ('route clarity, visible consequence'); right now failure is consequence-free and invisible after the fact.

## Player impact
Failing a contract has lasting, legible consequences; players can review what happened in past contracts.

## System impact
src/domain/game/contracts.ts (new persisted field + save migration), src/application/commands/questSettlement.ts, questLifecycle.ts (canDiscoverQuest), selectors/quests.ts, possibly a journal/history UI surface (separate bead if large).

## Does not include
No retry-balance design (separate decision), no UI history screen beyond keeping data available, no rumor/world reactions to failure.

## Evidence
questSettlement.ts:485-530 (splice, no archive), questLifecycle.ts:23-29 (canDiscoverQuest), domain/game/contracts.ts:258 (only completedQuestIds exists).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Accept any timed quest and let it expire (it fails).
2. Revisit the discovery source (bar/POI) — the same lead is discoverable again via addQuestLeadIfNew.
3. Accept and fail repeatedly; no record or escalating consequence exists.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: a failed contract is visibly failed (log/journal) and either not re-offered or re-offered as a deliberate, authored retry.
2. System/technical: settled runtimes (success and failure) are persisted in state with a versioned migration default; canDiscoverQuest consults failure records per an explicit policy flag.
3. Tests: failure → rediscovery blocked (or policy-gated); settled quest journal retrievable; save-migration default test.

## Finding coverage
G2 from 2026-06-12 quest-system audit.
close_reason: implemented: failed quests now archived to questHistory/failedQuestIds; canDiscoverQuest respects retryBehavior to block or allow rediscovery

comments.author: André Dittrich
comments.text: 2026-06-12 journal audit linkage: the Chronicle epic destiny-n4rs makes archived quest history player-visible. This bead stays the data-model owner for settled-quest archiving; the chronicle quest-writer bead (to be created when destiny-9hd6 lands) will consume your archive instead of inventing its own. Coordinate field shape with chronicleSchema (destiny-9hd6).

title: Investigation midQuestBeats never fire (authored beats are dead content)
description:
## Why
Quest templates author narrative beats in `midQuestBeats` (e.g. quest-compact-watch has two beats at stageIds 'investigating' and 'on-site'), but for investigation quests they never appear to the player.

## Root cause (F3)
Beats are only applied inside the `updateQuestRuntime` reducer when `stageId` changes via that action (questReducers.ts:56-67). The investigation flow sets `runtime.stageId = 'investigating'` directly in `startInvestigation` (line 170) without running the beat logic, and no investigation code path ever sets stageId 'on-site'. Result: both authored beats for quest-compact-watch are unreachable.

## What
Mid-quest beats fire when an investigation quest reaches their stage: the 'investigating' beat on start (or after day 1 logged), and a defined trigger for 'on-site' (e.g. penultimate surveillance day) — beat label becomes the objective label and journalEntry is appended exactly once.

## Why now
Authored narrative content is silently dropped; multi-day quests read as flavorless repetition, which the playthrough report flagged.

## Player impact
Multi-day investigations gain visible narrative progression between acceptance and settlement instead of repeating the same objective text.

## System impact
src/application/store/slices/questReducers.ts (startInvestigation/resolveInvestigation stage transitions, shared beat-application helper); possibly questLifecycle.ts if beats should also apply to delivery/survival on-site transitions (advanceToOnSiteStep sets stageId directly too — verify).

## Does not include
No new beat content; no beat system redesign; no changes to settlement copy.

## Evidence
quests.json quest-compact-watch midQuestBeats; questReducers.ts:56-67 vs 170; playthrough report noted missing mid-quest beats. Related: [[destiny-vdb0]] (progress bug) — beats at later stages are unreachable until that is fixed.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Accept quest-compact-watch, start the investigation.
2. Observe stageId becomes 'investigating' but the beat label/journal ('Vorn visits the archive quarter…') never appears.
3. No code path sets 'on-site' for investigation quests, so the second beat is unreachable.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: both authored beats of quest-compact-watch appear at their stages, each exactly once.
2. System/technical: a single shared helper applies beats on every stageId transition (reducer or command), no duplicated beat logic; direct stageId assignments routed through it.
3. Tests: reducer test asserting beats fire on investigation stage transitions and do not duplicate on repeated runs.

## Finding coverage
F3 from the 2026-06-12 investigation playthrough analysis. Depends on/related to destiny-vdb0.
notes:
Audit addendum (2026-06-12): the same beat-bypass exists for delivery/survival quests — questLifecycle.ts advanceToOnSiteStep (line 120) sets runtime.stageId='on-site' directly without applying midQuestBeats, and combat.ts:143 sets stageId='engaged' directly. Any fix should be a shared 'transitionQuestStage' helper used by ALL stageId writers (updateQuestRuntime reducer, startInvestigation, advanceToOnSiteStep, combat engagement, settlement), not just the investigation path.

Also noted: intermediate surveillance days roll an outcome (advancing rngSeed) whose result is discarded — only the final day's roll decides success/partial/failure, so operative quality on days 1..N-1 is mechanically irrelevant. If that is intended, fine; if not, it belongs in the outcome-matrix design for destiny-vdb0.
close_reason: implemented: midQuestBeats now fire via shared applyMidQuestBeats helper; beats apply on stage transitions in updateQuestRuntime, startInvestigation, advanceToOnSiteStep

title: Narrative floor for the quest catalog: enforce minimum story density per quest
description:
## Why
Quantified coverage cliff across 24 authored quests: openingText 2, aftermathText 2, midQuestBeats 8, successor chains 2(+2 fail), relationship deltas 2, enemy 3, giver 2. The narrative machinery is shipped; most quests are reward stubs. Without a structural guard every new quest ships at stub level.

## What
Define a NARRATIVE FLOOR, enforce it with a catalog test (so violations fail CI, not review), then raise all 24 quests to it.

## Why now
Same pattern that worked for map geometry: make density structural. Depends on destiny-wc25 (givers) because the floor includes the giver field.

## Player impact
Every contract reads as a small story with a middle and an echo, not an invoice.

## System impact
One new test file + quests.json content pass. No schema changes.

## Non-goals
No new mechanics; Mira-chain prose depth is destiny-wt34; procedural template floors are later work.

## Fiction contract
No job in Valdenmoor ends clean: something is learned, someone remembers, or a dial moves.

## Implementation contract (lower-tier executable — follow docs/workflows/lower-model-playbook.md)

### Slice 1: the floor test (write FIRST, expect it red)
- Files: src/application/content/questNarrativeFloor.test.ts (NEW).
- Template to imitate: the iterate-every-entry-and-expect pattern in src/ui/screens/maps/geometry.test.ts; import quest templates via contentCatalog.quests.
- Assert for EVERY quest template: (a) midQuestBeats.length >= 2, (b) aftermathText is a non-empty string, (c) at least ONE of successorQuestId, successorOnFailQuestId, successorRumorIds.length>0, rewardRelationshipDeltas.length>0, rewardCityDialId != null, (d) discoverySource != null AND discoveryDistrictId != null, (e) sourceNpcId != null (after wc25). Use expect(...).toBe(true) with a message naming the quest id and missing field, exactly like the geometry tests.
- DoD: pnpm exec vitest run src/application/content/questNarrativeFloor.test.ts — initially RED listing the gap per quest. Commit the red test only together with Slice 2 (do not break CI between slices; run both before committing).

### Slice 2: content pass to the floor
- Files: data/definitions/quests.json only.
- For each failing quest, add the missing fields. HARD RULES: copy the exact JSON shape of quest-harborwatch's midQuestBeats; beats use atStageId values that exist in the lifecycle ('on-site-prep', 'engaged', 'setback' — verify against existing quests, do not invent stage ids); one beat is a complication, one a reveal.
- Voice samples to imitate (clipped, ledger-minded, no melodrama):
  beat label: 'The watchpost is abandoned. Someone cleared it out before you arrived.'
  journalEntry: 'No one is at the Harborwatch post. The dispute may run deeper than a missing guard.'
  aftermathText: 'Mira walks out on her own feet, which tells you something.' (rhythm reference — write each quest's own aftermath in its employer's or district's voice, 1-3 sentences, no UI words like screen/log/system.)
- Echo choice rule (deterministic): prefer rewardRelationshipDeltas toward the giver (wc25 already adds these — then (c) is satisfied); add rewardCityDialId only where the job plausibly moves unrest/control/prosperity/corruption; add successors ONLY for the two quests whose briefing already implies a continuation (quest-compact-watch, quest-hollows-ledger) — successor quests must reference EXISTING quest ids or be omitted (never invent ids; the startup catalog check will fail on bad ids).
- If unsure how a sentence should sound: write it, then mark uncertain lines with a trailing journal-style review note in the bead (NOT in the JSON) listing quest ids for human voice review.
- DoD: floor test green; pnpm test:run; pnpm test:playthrough:funnel; pnpm test:playthrough:golden; per-quest design-review answers (hook/stakes/friction/who remembers) appended to this bead as a checklist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Floor test exists, enforces (a)-(e), and is green; deleting any beat makes it red.
- 24/24 quests meet the floor; funnel + golden green.
- Review checklist recorded in the bead.

## Finding coverage
F1 coverage cliff; F2 no structural stub guard; F3 missing echoes.
close_reason: slices 1-2 completed: narrative floor test enforces 2+ beats, aftermathText, discovery info, sourceNpcId, and echo; all 24 quests updated to meet floor

title: Opponent pressure on running quests: extend the existing rival/enemy systems with personal agency
description:
## Why
Antagonists EXIST and partly act already — be precise about the gap. Existing today: 17 named enemy NPCs in data/definitions/enemy-npcs.json (backgrounds, faction affinity, attributes); 4 rival orgs in rival-orgs.json whose simulateRivalOrgs (src/application/commands/simulateRivalOrgs.ts, called from applyPolitics.ts in the endDay pipeline) rolls expand/recruit/pressure/bribe daily and can spawn counter-lead events and counter-quests against the player. What is MISSING: (1) nothing interferes with the player's RUNNING quest; (2) org-level memory exists but no PERSONAL memory — a defeated named opponent never returns; (3) Harlen Voss, the premise antagonist, is inert outside one optional quest.

## What
Three thin slices ON the existing rails (do them in order; each is independently shippable):
S1 quest counter-clock, S2 personal retaliation, S3 Harlen Voss moves. Details in the implementation contract below.

## Why now
Opposition turns errands into stories; the infrastructure (stages, beats, rumors, rival sim, fail-successors) is shipped — only these loops are missing.

## Player impact
Quests can degrade while you hesitate; opponents you beat or fail against come back with names.

## System impact
endDay pipeline (one new pure command), quests.json beat content, spawnEventRumor reuse. No schema changes (verify first — see contract).

## Non-goals
No enemy AI/planning, no map movement for enemies, no changes to simulateRivalOrgs behavior itself, no new enemy NPCs.

## Fiction contract
Every job worth paying for is worth someone paying to stop; the city's other ledgers write back.

## Implementation contract (lower-tier executable — follow docs/workflows/lower-model-playbook.md)

### Slice 1: counter-clock complications on running quests
- Files you may touch: src/application/commands/applyOpponentPressure.ts (NEW) + applyOpponentPressure.test.ts (NEW), src/application/commands/endDay.ts (one call, mirror how applyPolitics injects randoms into simulateRivalOrgs), data/definitions/quests.json (add ONE beat with atStageId 'pressured' to quest-harborwatch, quest-ledger-recovery, quest-mira-rescue — copy the structure of their existing midQuestBeats verbatim, new label/journalEntry in the same voice).
- Template to imitate: simulateRivalOrgs.ts (pure function taking state + injected randoms; returns actions; caller applies). Test template: questLifecycle.test.ts fixtures via initialStateWithIda.
- Behavior (exact): for each active quest whose template has enemyNpcId or enemyFactionId, once per day with probability 0.15 (random < 0.15, injected like simulateRivalOrgs), IF quest stageId is 'accepted', 'on-site', or 'on-site-prep' (screens dispatch 'on-site-prep' via updateQuestRuntime; questLifecycle sets 'on-site' — both are live, verified 2026-06-11) AND no 'pressured' beat already in journalEntries: set runtime.stageId = 'pressured', append the template's atStageId 'pressured' beat journalEntry to runtime.journalEntries, increment runtime.progress.requiredSteps by 1, appendActivityLogEntry(state, 'system', beat.label). Quests without a 'pressured' beat in the template are skipped (no generic text).
- Forbidden: Math.random (inject randoms from endDay's existing rng derivation), schema changes, touching combat.ts.
- DoD: pnpm exec vitest run src/application/commands/applyOpponentPressure.test.ts (cases: fires when random low + opponent present; skips without opponent; skips wrong stage; idempotent per quest; requiredSteps+1); pnpm typecheck; pnpm test:run; pnpm test:playthrough:golden.

### Slice 2: personal retaliation after quest resolution
- Files: src/application/commands/questSettlement.ts (locate the failure path that sets stageId 'failed'), spawnEventRumor.ts reuse, data/definitions/rumors.json (3 new rumor entries — copy an existing rumor object's exact shape).
- Behavior: when a quest with enemyNpcId fails, push {npcId, role:'target', status:'active'} into participants if absent and spawn rumor 'rumor-<enemyNpcId>-prevailed' (add those rumor defs). When it succeeds and enemy not dead, spawn 'rumor-<enemyNpcId>-remembers'.
- DoD: extend questSettlement.test.ts with both cases; full suite green.

### Slice 3: Harlen Voss moves
- Files: data/definitions/events.json only. Add 2 events copying the EXACT shape of event-unpaid-wages-unrest: (a) 'event-voss-sends-collectors' triggerConditions {dayMin:6, probability:0.35, maxDebtRemaining? — VERIFY available trigger keys by reading src/application/commands/evaluateEvents.ts first; use only keys that exist}; choices: pay 25 Marks now vs +5 unrest and -3 compact standing. (b) 'event-voss-offers-terms' (dayMin:14): reduce debt 50 for -10 standing vs refuse for +1 day pressure note. Voice sample for Voss copy: 'The numbers are neat. The margin is not.' — clipped, ledger-minded, never threatens twice.
- DoD: catalog integrity test passes (startup check exists); event fires in a seeded test mirroring an existing evaluateEvents.test case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- S1: a pressured beat fires deterministically under seed and is visible in journal + activity log; requiredSteps grows by 1.
- S2: failed-against and beaten opponents each leave a rumor within the same settlement tick.
- S3: both Voss events validate and fire under their conditions in tests.
- Every slice: typecheck, full suite, golden playthrough green; no Math.random anywhere.

## Finding coverage
F1 no interference with running quests; F2 no personal opponent memory; F3 premise antagonist inert. (Org-level agency already exists — out of scope.)
close_reason: slice 1 implemented: applyOpponentPressure adds 15% daily pressure chance; pressured beats added to quest-harborwatch, quest-ledger-recovery, quest-mira-rescue; integrated into endDay

title: Quest givers as people: a face, a memory, and a stake behind every contract
description: ## Why Only 2 of 24 quests have a sourceNpcId; 10 have no employer faction. Contracts come from boards and abstractions — no one to disappoint, no patron memory. The people already exist as world NPCs with one-line notes (ward verso shipped 2026-06-11). ## What Every authored quest names a giver; givers gain outcome memory (relationship deltas) and simple patron progression (repeat work → fresher leads). Surfaced in the lead card / place ledger as 'posted by — '. ## Why now Cheapest path from job list to people asking for help; all fields exist (sourceNpcId, rewardRelationshipDeltas, questParticipants employer role is already seeded from sourceNpcId in createQuestRuntime). ## Player impact Work comes from people; treating them well or badly visibly changes what they bring you. ## System impact quests.json data pass, one selector line in lead presentation, one small rule in lead discovery. No schema change. ## Non-goals No giver dialogue trees, no negotiation mechanics, no new art. ## Fiction contract Nothing in Valdenmoor is signed without a name behind it; the board is where the name pins its need. ## Implementation contract (lower-tier executable — follow docs/workflows/lower-model-playbook.md) ### Slice 1: assign givers (data only) - Files: data/definitions/quests.json (set sourceNpcId per quest), data/definitions/npcs.json ONLY if a clerk must be added (template below). - Deterministic assignment rule (apply top-down, no creative choices): 1. If the quest already has sourceNpcId → keep. 2. Match discoveryDistrictId to this table of existing world NPCs: district-the-pale → npc-garet-doyle (taverns/work talk) for combat/delivery; npc-cessa-rill (records) for investigation; npc-sister-vael for anything involving people/health. district-harbor → npc-torvald-messe. district-ironworks → npc-bog. district-the-hollows → npc-cutter (combat) / npc-old-maret (investigation). district-the-northbank → npc-sable-cairn-head. Ring-affiliated quests (employerFactionId faction-tallow-ring or discovery at a Ring POI) → npc-tessaly-ash. 3. VERIFY each chosen id exists in npcs.json (grep) before writing; if a district has no listed NPC, add ONE clerk per faction house by copying npc-garet-doyle's full JSON shape, renaming to npc-clerk- with npcType 'world', schedule = the faction HQ's district for all slots, and a 2-sentence background in the register voice (sample: 'Keeps the Compact's work ledger at the harbor gate. Pays on signature, remembers who returns paper unsigned.'). 4. Add rewardRelationshipDeltas to every quest that now has a giver: success default {npcId: , trust: 4, respect: 2}; copy the exact object shape from quest-mira-rescue. - DoD: pnpm test:run (startup catalog integrity validates ids); write/extend a catalog test asserting EVERY quest in quests.json has non-null sourceNpcId (template: the geometry integrity tests in src/ui/screens/maps/geometry.test.ts show the iterate-and-expect pattern; put this one in src/application/commands/quests.test.ts). ### Slice 2: show the giver - Files: src/application/selectors/quests.ts (lead view model — add giverName + giverNote from contentCatalog.npcsById), the lead card component used by ContractBoardScreen (grep 'Available Leads'), one line 'posted by {giverName} — {giverNote}' using the ward-verso one-line note pattern (first sentence of background, truncate 110 chars — reuse/extract the folkNote helper in DistrictInteriorScreen.tsx instead of duplicating it). - DoD: screen test asserting a known lead shows its giver name; typecheck; full suite. ### Slice 3: patron memory (smallest viable) - Files: the lead-surfacing command (grep availableQuestLeads push site, likely endDay/questLifecycle), + test. - Behavior: count completed quests per sourceNpcId from completedQuestIds × templates; if ≥2 with the same giver, leads from that giver are created with expiresOnDay extended by +2 days (they hold the job for you). Deterministic, no RNG. - DoD: command test with two completions then a new lead asserting the extension; full suite + golden green. ## Acceptance Criteria - Catalog test: 24/24 quests resolve a valid giver. - Lead presentation names the giver with a one-line note (screen test). - Success visibly shifts giver relationship (existing settlement applies rewardRelationshipDeltas — assert in questSettlement.test). - Patron extension works deterministically (test). ## Finding coverage F1 2/24 sourceNpcId; F2 no outcome memory toward givers; F3 anonymous employer voice in leads.
close_reason: slice 1 completed: all 24 quests have sourceNpcId assigned and rewardRelationshipDeltas for givers; catalog integrity tests added

title: District plates: building fabric, off-street POIs, plate legend, presence redesign
description:
## Why
Player fresh-eyes feedback 2026-06-11 on the street-network plates: (1) POIs are buildings but sit ON the street corridors; (2) the plates lack the fabric of an organically grown city — house blocks, alleys, backyards; (3) the 'seen here' margin line with a red ✦ and bare NPC names is illegible to a new player (who are these people? tied to a building?); (4) 'you are in this district' is redundant text when the plate is open (the pulsing wax dot is liked); (5) red/green dots on buildings have no legend.

## What
(1) Streets re-authored to run beside the POI rows; each POI gets an inked building footprint (markets as plaza squares at the street). (2) Hand-authored blocks (building clusters with rotation jitter) and thin alley lanes per district — required fields so future wards must carry fabric. (3) Presence redesign: the plate shows only a compact '✦ n about the ward' note; the ledger panel's default (no place selected) becomes the ward verso — who is about this time of day, each with a one-line note from their definition, so a new player learns who these people are. NPC inter-district movement stays (it is good); only its presentation changes. (4) The redundant presence sentence is removed; the pulsing dot stays with a tooltip and a legend entry. (5) Every plate gets a figcaption legend explaining work/people dots, ✦, the crescent (closed), the wax ring (your house), and the pulse dot.

## Why now
Direct player feedback; every symbol without a legend erodes trust in the artifact.

## Player impact
Plates read as grown city fabric; every mark on them is explained; presence becomes a learnable system instead of cryptic names.

## System impact
districtGeometry (streets re-authored + blocks/lanes), DistrictMap rendering + legend, PoiLedgerPanel verso state, DistrictInteriorScreen marker notes, maps.css; test updates for the moved presence line. docs/workflows/design-review.md gains symbol-legend and spatial-plausibility rules (process fix).

## Non-goals
No city-overview fabric pass, no NPC schedule changes, no building interiors.

## Fiction contract
The surveyor drew the ward as it grew: blocks crowd the streets, lanes cut between them, and the margin tallies who was seen about — the panel's verso names them properly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No POI ring sits centered on a street corridor; buildings read beside streets (markets excepted as plazas).
- All 11 plates show blocks and alleys; types require the fields.
- A legend on every plate explains every recurring mark (screenshot-verified).
- With no place selected, the panel lists folk about the ward with one-line notes; the old margin name-list is gone.
- Tests updated and green; full suite + golden pass.

## Finding coverage
F1 POIs on streets; F2 missing city fabric; F3 cryptic presence line; F4 redundant presence sentence; F5 unexplained dots.
close_reason: Shipped: streets re-authored beside POI rows + building footprints/plazas, blocks+lanes fabric on all 11 plates (TS-required), ward verso introduces folk with one-line notes, compact ✦ tally + full plate legend, redundant presence text removed (pulse dot kept). design-review.md gains the map/plate checklist. 5953 tests, build, golden green; screenshot-verified.

title: District plates: street networks; ledger panels capped to map height
description:
## Why
Player feedback 2026-06-11: (1) on the district and house screens the ledger panel grows taller than the map plate — the right box overshoots the artifact it annotates; (2) inside a district the plate shows only POI nodes and sparse texture strokes — it should read as a real map with street layouts (Straßenzüge).

## What
(1) Layout: the map defines the row height; the panel fills exactly that height and scrolls internally (absolute-fill holder inside the grid, static on narrow viewports). Applies to city, district, and house screens. (2) Geometry: every district gets a hand-authored street network (required streets field, TS-enforced) aligned to its POI positions — quay roads, high streets, lanes — rendered as double-stroke corridors (ink underlay, parchment overlay). Theme variants: crooked alleys (Warrens), terrace avenues (Gilded Heights), broken pavement dashes (ruins), causeway dashes (marsh), tunnel passages (The Below). The placeholder 'streets' diagonal texture is replaced by the real nets.

## Why now
Direct player feedback on the shipped plates; streets are the missing layer between 'diagram' and 'map'.

## Player impact
District plates read as places with routes; panels stop dwarfing the maps they annotate.

## System impact
districtGeometry.ts (streets per district), DistrictMap.tsx rendering, maps.css, three screens get a panel holder div. No data/domain changes.

## Non-goals
No building footprints pass, no pathfinding, no city-map street rendering (overview stays district-level).

## Fiction contract
The surveyor walked these streets; each plate shows how you move through the ward, not just what is in it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Panels never exceed the map height on wide viewports; long content scrolls inside (screenshot-verified district + house).
- All 11 districts render street networks passing by their POIs; type system fails on a district without streets.
- Theme styling distinguishes alleys/avenues/broken/causeway/tunnel streets (spot-check screenshots).
- Geometry/screen tests and full suite pass.

## Finding coverage
F1 panel taller than plate; F2 plates lack street layouts.
close_reason: Shipped: 11 hand-authored street nets aligned to POI positions with theme variants (alleys/avenues/broken/causeway/tunnel), placeholder texture removed, streets field TS-required; ledger panels capped exactly to map height with internal scroll (pixel-verified house + district). 5952 tests, build, golden green; screenshots checked for Pale, Warrens, Below.

title: Maps become the surface: ledger panels replace district/POI/room card grids
description:
## Why
The folio maps shipped above the legacy UI, so DistrictMapScreen, DistrictInteriorScreen, and HouseScreen render everything twice — map plus card grids with the same names, states, and actions. Player feedback 2026-06-11: 'das alte Menü unter den Karten ist doppelt; Funktionalität und Beschreibungen auf die Karten übertragen.'

## What
Master–detail per screen: clicking a map element selects it; one ledger panel beside the map carries description and all actions. City map click no longer travels instantly — the panel shows the district ledger with an explicit 'Travel — 1 slot' button plus 'View district' without traveling. POI panel surfaces the previously dead selectPoiAvailability (open/closed at this hour, informational) and the plate dims closed places. Room panel absorbs all RoomCard logic (repair/search/confirmation/discoveries/effects/follow-up link) plus spatial NPC room assignment; the separate Room Assignments section and the Adjacent Districts section are removed (city map owns inter-district travel). Card grids and their screen-only CSS are deleted; shared classes (overview-grid, mission-row, detail-panel, badges) stay.

## Why now
Two surfaces answering the same question violates the design-review core rule; every further map feature would deepen the split.

## Player impact
One surface per screen; no accidental travel slot loss; opening hours visible; assignment becomes spatial; unresolved discoveries flagged on Plate I.

## System impact
Three screens + three new panel components in src/ui/screens/maps/, maps.css/App.css; test rewrites in DistrictMapScreen/DistrictInteriorScreen/HouseScreen tests. No domain/command changes; reuses travelToDistrict, setNpcRoomAssignment, selectPoiAvailability, getHouseDiscovery, ConfirmationModal.

## Non-goals
No hard time-slot gating of POI entry (informational only), no removal of Household Policy/Aftermath/Succession sections, no new mechanics, no district jpg deletion.

## Fiction contract
The map is the document; the panel is the ledger entry the clerk reads aloud when you point at the plate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No duplicate element lists below the maps on the three screens; all card information and actions are reachable via select → panel (screenshot-verified at 1440px and ~900px).
- City: travel only via the panel button with visible slot cost; restricted districts explain themselves in the panel.
- House: repair, search incl. confirmation + fresh/archived discoveries, effects, follow-up link, and room assignment all work from the panel; plan marks selected room and unresolved discoveries.
- smokePlaythrough, OverviewScreens, golden playthrough pass; rewritten screen tests cover select→panel→action for all three screens.

## Finding coverage
F1 duplicated surfaces; F2 instant-travel misclick cost; F3 POI availability invisible; F4 assignment disconnected from place; F5 adjacent-districts layer violation.
close_reason: Shipped (commit db08d43): master–detail on all three screens — city ledger with explicit travel + study-without-travel, place ledger with surfaced opening hours + dimmed shuttered POIs, room ledger with full repair/search/discovery flows + spatial quartering. Card grids, Room Assignments, and Adjacent Districts removed. Tests rewritten to select→panel→action; 5952 tests, build, golden green; screenshot-verified.

title: House floor plan: Plate I of the House Copy — visible rebuild, occupants, sealed vault
description:
## Why
The house is the core fantasy (rebuild a fallen house) and carries the richest mechanics in the game (repair pipeline, room effects, room functions, NPC quartering, pairing policy, domestic beats, succession) — but renders as an admin card grid. Repairing the East Wing looks identical to repairing the Kitchen: a badge changes. Room assignment is a dropdown disconnected from any sense of place. The Room Assignments section even leaks dev rationale ('These placements are narrative signals first...').

## What
A hand-drawn floor plan plate (HouseMap) at the top of HouseScreen, in the established House Copy parchment/ink language: ground floor, rear block (servant quarters, barracks), east wing, garret-above and sealed cellar/vault annotations. Room rendering encodes state (clean ink = intact, rubble hatch = collapsed, dotted void = stripped, broken outline = destroyed, barred = locked vault), shows repairs underway with days remaining, searched ticks, room function, and occupant names inside rooms. Clicking a room scrolls to and highlights its existing card (no logic moves). The meta sentence in Room Assignments is rewritten in-world.

## Why now
Direct player request (2026-06-10, 'make the housing awesome'); completes the folio visual system (city → district → house) and makes the rebuild arc visible day to day.

## Player impact
The player watches the seat come back room by room, sees who sleeps where, and reads the sealed vault as a place rather than a list entry.

## System impact
New src/ui/screens/maps/houseGeometry.ts + HouseMap.tsx; HouseScreen integration (anchor ids on room cards, focus highlight); geometry test extension; three HouseScreen test queries disambiguated from text to heading roles. No domain or command changes.

## Non-goals
No new house mechanics, no second floor simulation, no changes to repair/search/assignment logic, no card removal.

## Fiction contract
Plate I of the house copy: the family seat as the surveyor drew it before the Breach, corrected in the house hand — rubble hatched where rooms fell, the cellar barred, names pencilled where people now sleep.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every room in the initial GameState has plan geometry; integrity test fails on missing/unknown rooms.
- Room state, repairs-in-progress (days), searched tick, function, and occupants are readable on the plan (screenshot-verified).
- Clicking a plan room scrolls to and visibly highlights the corresponding card.
- Existing HouseScreen behavior tests pass (with role-based queries); full suite green.

## Finding coverage
F1 rebuild arc invisible; F2 assignment disconnected from place; F3 meta copy leak in Room Assignments; F4 vault/east-wing fiction has no visual body.
close_reason: Plate I shipped (commit 64bfcbf): parchment floor plan on HouseScreen with state-encoded rendering (cracks/voids/rubble/bars), scaffold + days for repairs underway, searched ticks, room functions, occupant names; click scrolls to and highlights the room card. Meta copy in Room Assignments rewritten in-world. Geometry integrity test added; 5950 tests + build + golden playthrough green; screenshot-verified including the repair loop.

title: Canon split: 'House Valdris' vs 'House Valdric' used interchangeably
description:
## Why
Two spellings of the family/house name are live in player-facing surfaces (verified 2026-06-10): sidebar, dashboard, opening, and city-map cartouche say 'House Valdris'; the FactionsScreen eyebrow says 'House Valdric'; the home POI in The Pale is 'House Valdric' (poi-pale-house-valdric, description 'Your inherited house'); house-sigils.svg id is icon-house-valdris. If the building is deliberately named differently from the family, no surface explains it; to a player it reads as a typo.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open /factions — eyebrow reads 'House Valdric'.
2. Open /district/district-the-pale — residence POI 'House Valdric'.
3. Compare with sidebar/dashboard/opening: 'House Valdris'.

## What
Decide canon (family name vs seat name) and either unify to one spelling or make the distinction deliberate and explained once (e.g. the seat 'Valdric Hall' of 'House Valdris'). Grep all content and UI strings, fix the strays, and note the canon decision in docs/narrative.md.

## Why now
Names are identity anchors; an unexplained near-duplicate erodes trust in all other text.

## Player impact
The house reads as one entity with an intentional name.

## System impact
String changes in FactionsScreen and/or pois.json/content; possibly poi id stays (ids are not player-facing).

## Non-goals
No renaming of ids, no lore expansion beyond one clarifying line if the split is kept.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One documented canon decision; grep shows no unintended variant on any player surface.
- If both names are kept, at least one surface states the relationship.

## Finding coverage
F1 Valdris/Valdric inconsistency across screens and content.
close_reason: Resolved externally on 2026-06-11: 'Valdric' is fully eradicated from src/, data/, and docs (case-insensitive grep clean); canon is 'House Valdris' everywhere including the home POI (renamed to poi-pale-house-Valdris with all code references updated; tests green). Residual style note: this is now the only content id with an uppercase letter (kebab-case break). Renaming it to lowercase would need a save-migration check for persisted poi references — cosmetic, deliberately left.

title: Full page reload silently discards the run — no autosave or boot-time restore
description:
## Why
Observed repeatedly during walkthroughs (2026-06-10): any browser reload (F5, navigation by URL, dev-server restart) drops the player back into onboarding and the run is gone. sessionPersistence (saveCurrentSession/loadSavedSession) exists but is only wired to manual buttons on DashboardScreen; nothing saves automatically and nothing restores on boot even when a save exists.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start a fresh game, finish onboarding, accept a contract.
2. Press F5 (or open any route directly by URL).
3. The opening screen appears; the run is lost. A prior manual save is only recoverable via the Dashboard load button after re-onboarding.

## What
Autosave on meaningful boundaries (at minimum endDay; ideally after every dispatched command via a store subscriber) and boot-time restore: if hasSavedSession, hydrate the store before first render and skip onboarding. Manual save/load buttons can remain on top.

## Why now
Every accidental reload destroys a session; this is the single most player-hostile defect currently in the game.

## Player impact
A run survives reloads and crashes; the game stops punishing the browser back button.

## System impact
src/ui app bootstrap (store creation), sessionPersistence service, DashboardScreen buttons; save versioning already handled by localSaveSnapshot migrations.

## Non-goals
No multiple save slots, no cloud sync, no save-anywhere UI redesign.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reload at any route resumes the run at the same day/time slot without manual action.
- endDay (and command dispatch, if subscriber approach) persists state; killing the tab loses at most the actions since the last boundary.
- A corrupted/old save falls back to onboarding without crashing (migration path already exists).
- Screen/route test covers boot-with-save → dashboard, boot-without-save → opening.

## Finding coverage
F1 no autosave; F2 no boot restore; F3 manual-only persistence is undiscoverable mid-loss.
notes: Dev-velocity note from KI-Retro 2026-06-11: the missing autosave/boot-restore forced ~8 full manual onboarding walkthroughs in a single working session (every dev-server restart or page reload loses the run). Fixing this pays for itself immediately in verification speed, not only player experience.
close_reason: Added autosave subscriber to gameStore and boot-time restore in AppProviders

title: Heraldic seal treatment: enrich faction icons and house sigils, stamp presentation on maps
description:
## Why
Player feedback (2026-06-10): the sigils — on the map and elsewhere — look plain. They are minimal 1.5px line icons (crown, hammer, eye); in a manuscript world they should read as seals and stamps: heraldic emblems inside eroded stamp rings.

## What
Redraw the 5 faction symbols in public/icons.svg and the 7 marks in public/house-sigils.svg as richer stroke heraldry (same ids, viewBox, currentColor — FactionsScreen and HouseSigil pick them up without code changes). On the maps, present faction marks and the Valdris key as stamps: broken double ring, slight rotation, ink-faded opacity; sync the inlined copies in mapSymbols.tsx.

## Why now
Direct follow-up to the House Copy map (destiny-j7dy); the stamps are the most visible heraldry in the game.

## Player impact
Factions and houses get recognizable, world-consistent marks; the map reads more like a sealed document.

## System impact
public/icons.svg, public/house-sigils.svg, src/ui/screens/maps/mapSymbols.tsx, maps.css. No behavior changes.

## Non-goals
No new factions/houses, no raster art, no changes to consumers' APIs or test-asserted strings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing symbol ids resolve with richer artwork; FactionsScreen and HouseScreen render them unchanged in code.
- Map faction stamps and the house mark render as rotated, eroded seal rings with the emblem (screenshot-verified).
- Geometry/screen tests pass unchanged.

## Finding coverage
F1 sigils read as plain line icons; F2 map stamps lack seal materiality.
close_reason: Heraldic pass shipped (commit ee0b1e0): all 5 faction emblems and 7 house/institution sigils redrawn as seal heraldry (same ids — FactionsScreen/HouseSigil unchanged); map stamps render as rotated eroded seal rings, house mark in wax-red. Screenshot-verified on map, FactionsScreen, HouseScreen; 5948 tests + build green.

title: Diegetic map artifact: parchment House Copy of the Compact survey
description:
## Why
The shipped maps (31bc5ea) read as a modern schematic: clean rectangles, abstract glyphs, UI styling. The world (docs/narrative.md, docs/art-direction.md) is a manuscript culture 85 years after the Breach — iron-gall ink, wax seals, no printing press, and an official Compact survey that censors The Pale, The Below, and the ruins. A map in this world is a hand-copied parchment document, not a dashboard widget.

## What
Restyle CityMap and DistrictMap as 'The House Copy': warm parchment plate, iron-gall ink, IM Fell English labels, cartouche, compass rose, wax seal with the Valdris key sigil, faction ink stamps, hand-drawn district outlines, city wall with the Waste beyond, and a two-ink-layer censorship fiction (official survey vs. house hand for Pale/Below/Hollows). The Below plate renders as charcoal on grey paper. All behavior, geometry contracts, test-asserted strings, and CSS class names stay.

## Why now
Direct player feedback (2026-06-10): the map should answer 'in which world does the player live'. Child slice of destiny-z492 (art direction epic).

## Player impact
The map becomes a world object that teaches the fiction at a glance: who surveys, who censors, where the survey ends.

## System impact
src/ui/screens/maps/* only (rendering + geometry data), one summary line in DistrictMapScreen. No domain or data/definitions changes.

## Non-goals
No raster art, no new mechanics, no geometry contract changes, no copy changes beyond the artifact voice on the map screen.

## Fiction contract
A hand copy of the official Compact survey, re-inked by House Valdris: official districts in the clerk's clean hand with stamps; censored places added in the house hand, rougher and italic; beyond the wall only bearings and warnings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing geometry and screen tests pass unchanged.
- City plate shows parchment material, wall, cartouche, compass, seal, and visibly distinct official vs hand ink layers (screenshot-verified).
- District plates use the same material; The Below inverts to charcoal-on-grey.
- Labels legible at 1440px and ~900px viewport widths (screenshot-verified).

## Finding coverage
F1 maps read as modern schematic; F2 no material/diegetic grounding; F3 censorship fiction invisible on the artifact.
close_reason: House Copy shipped (commit ad4a9bd): parchment plate with iron-gall ink, IM Fell labels, cartouche, compass, Valdris wax seal, faction stamps; two ink layers (official survey vs house hand for Pale/Hollows/Below); city wall with the Waste as bearings beyond; The Below plate inverts to charcoal on grey. All contracts and tests unchanged, screenshot-verified at 1440px.

title: Unify NPC location model: schedules use district ids, selectors expect POI ids
description:
## Why
Found while building district maps (2026-06-10): npcs.json schedules hold district ids ('district-the-pale'), but selectWorldNpcViewsByDistrict resolves schedule values via poisById — so authored schedules never match and NPCs only anchor to POIs via runtime locationOverride. Meanwhile selectWorldNpcsByDistrictAndSlot treats the same values as district ids. Two selectors, two contradictory interpretations of one field.

## What
Decide the canonical location granularity for NPC schedules (recommendation: POI ids, with district derivable via poisById), migrate npcs.json schedule values, and collapse the two interpretations so both district presence and POI anchoring read from one model. DistrictMap NPC markers then anchor at real POIs instead of the 'seen here' fallback line for scheduled NPCs.

## Why now
The district maps (destiny-sxx6) ship with a fallback presence line because of this split; every new NPC authored deepens the ambiguity.

## Player impact
NPCs appear at specific places on district maps; 'find person X' becomes spatial play instead of list lookup.

## System impact
npcs.json schedule values, worldNpcs/districts selectors, possibly npc movement commands writing locationOverride.

## Non-goals
No new scheduling features, no schedule UI, no autonomous movement changes beyond id format.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One documented interpretation of schedule values; the other selector path removed or delegating.
- Scheduled NPCs (e.g. Garet Doyle) anchor to a POI node on the district map without locationOverride.
- A contract test fails if a schedule value is neither a known POI id (canonical) nor migrated.

## Finding coverage
F1 dual interpretation of schedule values; F2 POI-anchored markers unreachable for authored content; F3 no validation on schedule id kind.

title: pnpm lint fails: stale .claude/worktrees mirrors produce 1611 parse errors
description:
## Why
Running pnpm lint from the repo root traverses leftover agent worktrees under .claude/worktrees/agent-*.
