Advances world NPC interactions (Yes) and meaningful choice+consequence (Partial/Yes via exploitation). Partial on agency and Mira (systemic enabler for future). Higher P1 gaps exist (do not reorder); natural P2 sibling to bond/politics cluster (destiny-dbk6, destiny-bw2h, destiny-lfxq, destiny-h3ba, destiny-rjyy).
- GAME_DESIGN_DOCUMENT.md: Sensual Presentation pillar (§16 and earlier refs) — earned via relationship depth, character-driven, restraint earns intensity, readable before explicit, never front-loaded, two-way agency. Relationships, courtship, household, and politics must affect each other.
- docs/content-policy.md (post-vvfh): 'Starkly suggestive with consequences is the default register; more direct consensual description is permitted only when earned.' Guardrails: Model aftermath not exploitation; character-driven; two-way agency preserved.
- docs/narrative.md: blackmail and favor networks as core city texture.
- docs/workflows/narrative-content.md: 'Keep reusable text modular for future scaling'; runtime truth rule (fiction must not over-promise unsupported delivery/intrigue steps).
- docs/workflows/bead-creation.md and task-contract.md: narrative-heavy beads require explicit Fiction contract.
- Existing incidental fiction: events.json (letter discovery as plot hook), pois.json (correspondence as district life).
- Specialist panel living-world audit: NPCs must act as independent agents with their own agendas; intrigue and personal comms are missing texture.
- Bond/politics architect review memories (2026-06-01): intimacy leverage as political tool; directed relationships as substrate for power dynamics.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Narrative / Player-visible (Fiction Contract & Guidelines):** The delivered Fiction Contract is authoritative, concise, and reusable. It explicitly encodes the four user requirements (NPC-to-NPC independence, policy-aligned intensity, modular generation that respects depth, exploitation as first-class fiction truth with visible consequence). Tone guidelines section contains clear definitions and at least one positive + one contrast ('bad') example for each of the three relationship types (deep consensual intimacy; power-imbalanced consensual; purely manipulative). A writer or later agent can answer 'is this letter text compliant?' without ambiguity.

2. **Authoring / Modular Approach:** The high-level generation proposal documents a practical combinatorial model using templates + textbausteine. It specifies: module categories (e.g. salutations/openings, memory/desire kernels, pressure/request cores, risk/aftermath subtext, closings/signatures); how intimacy level + relationship nature tags + writer voice filter and combine modules; how 'stark andeutend' vs 'earned direct/sinnlich' registers are enforced at assembly time; support for both fully procedural variety and seeded authored letters for key NPCs/story moments. The approach is compatible with existing content patterns (cf. event-rumor-templates.json) and the narrative-content workflow's modularity rule.

3. **Docs / Traceability:** The bead description follows the full bead-description.md + task-contract.md structure. All user-specified elements (Fiction Contract, modular approach proposal, 'direct/sinnlich' definition in-world, tone examples/guidelines for the three rel types) are present in clear, copy-paste usable form. Finding coverage explicitly maps the user's four requirements. Cross-refs to destiny-vvfh, bond P2s, rhx6, GDD, and policy are present. bd lint (if applicable) or manual review passes with no contradictions.

4. **Verification / No over-promise:** Acceptance explicitly notes that this bead records the contract only; any later impl bead must still prove runtime truth (letters do not imply unsupported mechanics such as reliable courier networks unless those are also built).

## Finding coverage

Maps the user's four requirements 1:1 (see Evidence). Also covers narrative standards gap identified in destiny-vvfh follow-up context, living-world specialist findings on NPC agency, GDD sensual/relationship cross-system requirement, and provides input to P1 fiction standards epic (rhx6) and P2 bond/politics cluster. No findings dropped.

## Fiction contract

Private correspondence in Valdenmoor is a physical, traceable, and weaponizable medium. Named NPCs write letters to one another — not only to the player — as part of pursuing their own agendas: desire, alliance, grievance, ambition, revenge, or simple need for connection in a dangerous city. These letters exist and move (or fail to move) independently of player action; the player may discover them, commission forgeries, intercept deliveries, withhold them as leverage, or become the target of their contents.

The emotional and sensual register of any letter is strictly determined by the actual directed relationship depth, history, and nature between its specific sender and recipient. It is never generic, never front-loaded, and never applied uniformly. When relationship depth and context permit, letters default to the starkly suggestive register ('stark andeutend') with visible emotional, political, relational, or mechanical consequences for the correspondents and any third party who learns the contents. More direct or sinnlich consensual language is permissible only in moments where preceding investment, specific character voice, shared history, and narrative necessity for the consequence have made the directness earned, tonally coherent with the dark fantasy setting, and necessary rather than decorative.

Every letter carries provenance (sender, recipient or intended recipient, approximate timing or triggering event, delivery vector or courier) that makes forgery, selective delivery, and blackmail first-class interactions. Withholding a letter or threatening exposure is a recognized power move within the city's information and favor economy. NPCs treat correspondence as both intimate channel and political asset; they write, read, and react to letters between third parties when they can.

The system must preserve two-way agency even in text: both parties in a consensual intimate exchange demonstrate preference, memory, and the capacity to initiate, refuse, or alter the terms. When letters are manipulative, the calculation must be legible in subtext or in the later consequence the player (or another NPC) can trigger. Letters never override character competence or primary identity; they reveal and complicate it.

This contract applies to all future authored seed letters, procedural generation rules, discovery surfaces, and exploitation mechanics. It is the diegetic truth against which all implementation and content will be measured.

## Proposed High-Level Approach for Modular Text Generation

**Core principle (from narrative-content.md + policy):** Reusable modules for variety and replayability; strict filters so sensual tone only appears at appropriate depth; writer voice and relationship context dominate selection so every letter feels like it came from these two specific people in this moment.

**Data shape (future content defs, not created here):**
- Banks of small textbausteine categorized by function and intensity ceiling: salutations/openings (neutral to charged), memory kernels (shared past events referenced with escalating intimacy), desire/need cores (longing, physical recollection, vulnerability — suggestive to direct), pressure/request cores (ask, demand, offer, veiled threat), risk/aftermath subtext (awareness of exposure, political cost, emotional stake), closings/signatures (warm, formal, possessive, desperate, coded).
- Relationship context tags per module or rule set: depthLevel (0-3 or mapped to presentationTier / pairBond stages), nature (romantic-mutual, political-seductive, bond-power-dynamic, rivalry-tension, alliance-pragmatic, etc.).
- Voice modulators: drawn from NPC traits, faction, personality quirks, current emotional state or recent events (e.g. high ruthlessness writer uses colder imagery; empathy-high writer references the recipient's feelings).

**Assembly process (high-level, pure function later):**
1. Determine effective intimacy ceiling from sender-recipient directed rel depth + recent shared history (never player projection).
2. Select candidate modules whose maxIntensity <= ceiling and whose tags match the rel nature + current context.
3. Apply writer voice filters / transformations (word choice, sentence rhythm, metaphors that fit the character's background — dockworker vs courtier vs former soldier).
4. Combine 4-7 modules into coherent structure (greeting + 1-2 body kernels + request/pressure + risk close + signature). Add light glue or small variations for flow.
5. For 'earned direct/sinnlich' moments: the generator (or authored override) may include one higher-intensity kernel only when depth >= threshold AND the narrative purpose of the letter justifies it (e.g. the letter is a pivotal confession whose exposure will have major aftermath).
6. Output includes not just the text but structured metadata: provenance, intensityUsed, key themes (for later blackmail/interception logic to reference without parsing prose).

**Authored vs procedural:** Seed 15-30 high-quality authored letters for critical NPC pairs and story moments (e.g. Marion writing to a contact, a Gilded Court rival seducing an asset, two bonded NPCs negotiating their dynamic). Use the same module system for them so they demonstrate the 'good' end of the spectrum. Procedural fills the rest for ambient and player-adjacent mail.

**Sensual support:** Modules at higher intensity levels exist in the banks but are filtered out unless the rel context passes the gate. This guarantees the policy 'never front-loaded' and 'earned through established relationship depth' without the generator needing special cases.

This approach gives replayable variety (different combinations on replay or for different NPC pairs) while keeping authorial control over voice and the hard boundary on when direct content appears.

## Definition: What 'direct/sinnlich' consensual content feels like in this world (policy-aligned)

Per content-policy.md (post-2026-06-01) and GDD §16: 'direct/sinnlich' is not generic explicit description or uniform erotic prose. It is a specific, character-voiced expression of desire, physical memory, or intimate need that:
- References concrete, private details only the two correspondents would know or recognize (a particular scar, a private nickname, the exact way one person touches the other, a shared moment in a specific location).
- Carries emotional or political weight — it is said because it changes something (deepens trust, creates leverage, acknowledges a cost, makes a request that now has stakes).
- Preserves two-way agency and aftermath modeling even in the text itself (the writer shows vulnerability or preference; the implied recipient's reaction matters; the letter anticipates consequence of discovery or of the act itself).
- Uses the dark fantasy register of the world: scarcity, danger, bodily reality, power awareness. Heat and want are present, but so is the collar, the debt, the rival listening at the door, the ledger that must still be balanced tomorrow.
- Is 'readable before explicit' in the broader sense — even the direct passages are prepared by prior letters, events, or relationship history in the player's mind.

Example feeling (not literal text): a letter that moves from 'I cannot stop thinking of your hand at the back of my neck during the council' (stark andeutend, memory + power awareness) into one earned direct sentence about a specific shared night, followed immediately by the political or emotional price the writer is willing to pay or exact because of it. The directness serves the consequence, not the other way around.

This register is reserved for fully consensual adult interactions between characters who have already demonstrated investment and mutual agency. It does not apply to, and must never bleed into, coercive, captivity, or trauma-framed contexts.

## Tone Examples and Guidelines by Relationship Type

**1. Consensual deep intimacy (high mutual depth, genuine attachment or love in a hard world):**
- Tone: Specific, warm or fiercely tender, laced with shared history and small private jokes or references. Vulnerability is present but not performative. May mix longing with pragmatic awareness of the city's dangers. Often future-oriented or anchoring ('come back to me after the Pale run').
- Voice: Fits the individuals — a pragmatic retainer like Marion might be blunt and fiercely protective; a courtier more ornate but still personal.
- Good (illustrative): 'The ink on this page smells like the cedar oil you use on your blades. I keep the letter folded inside my shirt when I walk the docks at night. The others think it is a contract. Let them. — M'
- Bad/generic (avoid): 'I miss your body. You are so beautiful. Come to me soon.' (no specificity, no character voice, no world stakes, front-loaded).
- Consequence surface: Strengthens pair bond or loyalty on receipt; discovery by rival creates jealousy or blackmail opportunity; the letter can become evidence of a real alliance the player can leverage or attack.

**2. Power-imbalanced but still consensual (e.g. bond-holder and bonded NPC with genuine mutual attraction; holder/ward dynamics that have become intimate):**
- Tone: Charged with the acknowledged dynamic. Language can eroticize the imbalance (collar, contract, protection/debt) but must show the less-powerful party's active choice, desire, and boundaries. The bonded writer may use the power difference as part of the heat while also asserting terms or reminding the holder of their obligations. Risk of corrosion is real and should be visible over a series of letters.
- Good: The bonded party writes with a mix of want and sharp negotiation — 'You hold the paper on me, but last night you asked what I wanted. I want you to remember that when the Compact assessor comes. I will wear the mark you left if you keep your word on the hearing.' (agency, specificity, stakes).
- Bad: Pure submission fantasy with no pushback or personhood ('I am yours to command, master'). This violates two-way agency and character-driven rule.
- Consequence surface: Can deepen the bond or create 'corruption' flags if the holder exploits the intimacy without reciprocity; intercepted letters become extremely potent blackmail because they prove the holder has 'gone native' or the bonded has 'chosen' their collar in a way that has social meaning.

**3. Purely manipulative (one or both parties using intimate/seductive language as a tool for gain, with little or no genuine depth):**
- Tone: On the surface seductive, confiding, or passionately phrased — but the details are slightly off, borrowed, or too perfectly calibrated to the recipient's known weaknesses. Requests serve a hidden agenda. Subtext or a second intercepted letter reveals the calculation. The writer may be a skilled operator (Gilded Court seductress type) or a desperate amateur.
- Good (for intrigue): The letter is beautifully written and moving if taken at face value. A second letter from the same sender to a different target uses almost identical 'private' memories (revealing the script). Or the 'intimate' request is for access to a ledger the player knows the writer has no personal stake in.
- Bad (for this type): The manipulation is too obvious in the first letter itself, robbing the player of the discovery pleasure. Or it uses the same generic erotic language as the 'bad' deep-intimacy example — skilled manipulators in Valdenmoor are better than that.
- Consequence surface: This is the richest vein for player exploitation. Forging or leaking such a letter can destroy a political alliance, force a bond renegotiation, or turn a lover into a vengeful enemy. The player can also be on the receiving end when an NPC forges 'intimate' correspondence in the player's name.

**Cross-cutting rules for all types:**
- Every letter must be attributable to its writer without the signature (voice is that strong).
- Sensual or intimate passages, however charged, must be in service of a clear motive or consequence (alliance cementing, favor request, emotional anchor before a dangerous action, setup for later betrayal).
- Discovery/exposure always has teeth: reputation shift, relationship delta (positive or negative), new blackmail item, faction standing change, or story branch.
- NPC-to-NPC letters the player never sees still 'happen' in the simulation and can produce secondary effects (an NPC is distracted or emboldened because they received a letter the player never found).

This completes the contract and guidelines requested for the 4th bead. Future beads will implement against it.
notes:
SESSION HANDOFF (Narrative role, 2026-06-01): Full content prepared and recorded in bead description per user request + bead-creation.md + task-contract.md. Includes: strong reusable Fiction Contract (NPC-to-NPC letters as physical/interceptable vector, earned sensual per policy vvfh, provenance for exploitation); high-level modular templates+textbausteine generation approach with categories, filters by depth/nature/voice, authored vs procedural; precise in-world definition of 'direct/sinnlich' (specific private details + consequence weight + agency + dark fantasy register, never generic/exploitative); concrete good/bad tone examples + guidelines for consensual deep intimacy, power-imbalanced consensual bonds, and purely manipulative intimate language. RPG audit (Yellow) recorded under rpg-audit-2026-06-01-private-correspondence-bead-prep key. Precedent followed: destiny-vvfh structure and quality bar. This bead is the contract; no files created, no code touched. Next: when P2 intimacy/politics work is dequeued (after P1s like rhx6 fiction standards), claim this, then spawn thin content bead (populate modules + 20-30 seeds) + impl bead (domain contracts + generate command + endDay wiring + discovery surfaces). Aligns with GDD sensual pillar, living world, blackmail networks, and directed rels. All user requirements for the 4th bead met 1:1 in the description.
Drift guard from 2026-06-02 review: preserve the contract that letters are delivery/exploitation vectors for intimacy already built elsewhere, not the main unlock route into intimacy itself. Coordinate with destiny-jed7, destiny-tr29, and destiny-68j4.
close_reason: Superseded by destiny-fnhz which has more detailed architecture proposal and implementation-ready acceptance criteria

title: Bond-holder power produces trait drift, roster friction, and moral-weather consequences
description:
## Why
Even after the bond market backend (og7t), entry wiring (lfxq), and UI panel (bw2h) exist, and even after the directed relationship + intimacy system (13si + l7zl) is live, the act of the player holding one or more bondStatus contracts produces almost no personal, roster, or world-level consequence that acknowledges the power the player is wielding.

The only current signals are:
- alongsideFreeAssignmentDays + equality notices (internal to bonded NPC)
- one activity log line in applyMonthlyBondOperationCosts: "The cost of running bonded labor settles into the house. Empathic eyes do not look away, and the wider city grows meaner around it."

There is no:
- Drift on playerCharacter.traits (ruthlessness or dominance creep from sustained exercise of ownership power)
- Friction on other roster NPCs (high-empathy retainers cooling toward the player)
- Texture in the activityLog or pending events that carries sensual/power charge (the bodily reality of owning another person's contract)
- Measurable effect on cityStability, factionStandings (moral decline), or district tension

This directly contradicts the GDD sensual pillar ("Sensual and physical presentation is an explicit product pillar — not decorative filler. It is mechanically connected..."), the explicit warning that "if sensual presentation is only decorative, it weakens the product", and the living-world requirement that the city registers the player's actual moral and power choices.

## What
Extend the existing bond effects pipeline (inside or immediately after applyBondServiceEffects in bondService.ts) with a new pure function applyBondHolderConsequences(state: GameState): GameState that runs once per day for the player as bond-holder.

Effects (all deterministic, all using existing mutation patterns):
- If player holds 1+ bonds with total contractValue > threshold or average alongsideFree low (coercive), apply small positive drift to playerCharacter.traits.ruthlessness and dominance (clamped, max +1 per day aggregate).
- For every roster NPC with traits.empathy > 60 who is not themselves bonded, applyRelationshipDelta(player, npc, 'affinity', small negative) — they register the house's use of bonded labor.
- Append 0-1 charged activityLogEntry (category 'system') per day when conditions met, using prose that names a specific bonded NPC and carries the power/sensual weight without explicit content (e.g. "The ledger of [Name]'s remaining term lies open on the table tonight. The house feels smaller for it.").
- If aggregate holder power crosses a soft threshold, increment a transient pressure on cityStability or Gilded Court standing (moral decline vector already present in applyPolitics).

The function is the only new piece; it is called from within the existing applyBondServiceEffects so endDay.ts is untouched. All effects are reversible or decay when bonds are freed.

## Why now
The mechanical ownership system is about to become player-visible via bw2h. If the only feedback when the player buys, holds, lists, or rescues a bonded person is numeric contractValue and three rescue buttons, the feature ships as pure management sim. The GDD and the user focus require that bond-holding "carry visible sensual/power consequences for the player (as bond-holder)". Adding the holder-side feedback now, while the backend is still fluid and before the UI hardens, ensures the first time a player sees the BondStatusPanel they already feel the weight in the relationship bars, the log, their own trait profile, and the city's reaction. It is the thinnest slice that makes the player the subject of the power they hold rather than only its accountant.

## Player impact
- After several days holding coercive or multiple bonds, the player will see their own ruthlessness/dominance numbers (wherever traits are displayed today or in future) have crept.
- High-empathy roster members (e.g. Marion if empathy qualifies) will show declining affinity in the Relations tab — the house is judging the player's methods.
- The activity log will occasionally surface a line that makes the ownership feel personal and charged rather than a pure balance-sheet item.
- Faction standing or cityStability may take small hits, making the "city grows meaner" log from the existing code now have a mechanical downstream (pressure, events, recruitment difficulty).
- Freeing a bond will stop the accumulation — visible relief in the same surfaces.

## System impact
- Primary file: src/application/commands/bondService.ts — add the pure applyBondHolderConsequences and call it from the end of applyBondServiceEffects (after the existing equality and cost logic).
- Reuses: playerCharacter.traits (already mutated in rosterReducers, read in applyWages/questSettlement), applyRelationshipDelta (existing), appendActivityLogEntry (existing), cityStability/factionStandings (already mutated in applyPolitics).
- No new schema fields on GameState or playerCharacter (reuses existing traits and numeric dials).
- No changes to: endDay.ts (call site already exists), any domain contracts, UI, events, content, or initial state.
- Test surface: extension of bondService.test.ts describe('applyBondServiceEffects'...

## Does not include
- Any new UI for displaying "corruption", "power level", or special holder status (UI/UX follow-up after bw2h).
- Any permanent "power corruption" counter or new trait; only the existing ruthlessness/dominance numbers move.
- Any new events, scandals, or choice moments (those can consume the state this bead produces).
- Any change to how bonds are acquired, priced, or rescued (lfxq + bw2h).
- Any work on the politics consumption side (bead 2 queries + later rjyy children).
- Any modification of the equality notice or "alongside free" mechanics.
- Any terminology rename (h3ba) or lore bonds.
- Any content authoring or explicit scenes.
- Any save migration (no new persisted fields).

## Evidence
- GDD 3.5 sensual pillar + design rules + risk callout on decorative-only sensual; "the city is shaped by ... corruption or moral decline"; "relationship choices can conflict with faction strategy".
- Existing bondService.ts:169 (applyBondServiceEffects) and the applyMonthly... log already gesture at moral cost but produce no mechanical echo.
- applyPolitics.ts:158 (debtCrisis) and 84 (faction pressure) already have patterns for city/faction response to player moral/economic choices — bond holding should feed the same system.
- PlayerCharacter.traits already exist and are read for mechanical bonuses/penalties in multiple commands; the holder experience should be allowed to move them.
- Closed l7zl fiction contract and four-failure-mode audit: power must feel like risk and weight, not neutral progression.
- User focus + prime memories on NPC agency and living world: the world (and the player's own people) must react to the player actually holding power over others.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. **Player-visible acceptance**
   - After sufficient days holding 1+ player-owned bonds (criteria documented in implementation: e.g. 3+ days with average alongsideFree < 7 or total contractValue > X), at least one of the following is observable without new UI:
     - playerCharacter.traits.ruthlessness or dominance has increased by at least 1 from baseline.
     - At least one non-bonded roster NPC with empathy > 60 has a lower affinity toward 'player' than in the control save.
     - The activity log contains at least one entry whose text names a specific bonded NPC and registers the power dynamic in the register required by the fiction contract.
   - Freeing the last bond stops further accumulation on subsequent days.

2. **System / technical acceptance**
   - All new logic is a pure function applyBondHolderConsequences(state) inside bondService.ts, invoked from the existing applyBondServiceEffects after the current equality/cost work.
   - Only existing mutation helpers are used (applyRelationshipDelta, appendActivityLogEntry, direct immutable updates to playerCharacter.traits / cityStability / factionStandings following patterns already in the file and in applyPolitics).
   - No new persisted fields, no schema change, no RNG, fully deterministic given the day's state.
   - The function is safely idempotent if called multiple times on the same day state (guards via lastFiredDay-style or day checks if needed, but prefer pure per-call delta).

3. **Tests / verification acceptance**
   - pnpm test:run green, count not below 333.
   - pnpm typecheck clean.
   - New test cases in bondService.test.ts covering the holder consequences path:
     - Holding a single combat-capture bond for N simulated days produces the expected ruthlessness creep and one log entry.
     - High-empathy non-bonded roster member receives affinity penalty only while the bond is held.
     - Freeing the bond via freeNpc (existing) halts further drift on subsequent applyBondServiceEffects calls.
     - No drift occurs when player holds zero bonds.
   - Existing bond and relationship tests unaffected.

## Finding coverage
F4 (holder consequences too thin — full for trait/roster/log/world dial slice), F6 (GDD sensual pillar + moral decline + cross-system), part of F1 (precise language in this bead uses "bondStatus" / "directed relationship edges" / "lore bonds" explicitly to avoid worsening the h3ba ambiguity).

## Fiction contract
The player who signs the contract is also signed by it. Every day the paper remains in the house ledger, something in the holder shifts — a slight hardening, a slight narrowing of the rooms — and something in the house registers the change: a retainer who once met your eyes now looks at the floor, a district that once felt open feels watched. The log lines must carry the charge of ownership without ever naming the act that the city's moral weather has already recorded. When the last bond is freed, the house should feel, for a moment, larger again. That reversal is as important as the accumulation.
notes: Bond-holder consequences (trait drift, roster friction, moral weather) should be extended or informed by private correspondence involving bonded NPCs (destiny-fnhz). Compromising letters can significantly amplify or create new holder-side consequences. See destiny-fnhz.

title: Relationship and bond leverage queries for political systems
description:
## Why
The politics epic (destiny-rjyy and children destiny-58n5 intrigue events, vy9e agenda scoring rewrite, rx5v player-initiated votes, 71m3 agenda trees) and the existing directed relationship system (intimacyStage, bondType including 'romantic', legacyIntentActive, hard/softBond on edges keyed by buildRelationshipKey from→to) plus the bondStatus labor contracts have no shared query surface.

There is no pure function, selector, or helper that answers "what political leverage does the player currently hold via intimate relationships or power-imbalanced bond holdings against this faction or NPC?"

Consequently:
- applyPolitics still picks random vote templates (even with seeded rng) and resolves with simple playerVote %.
- Leadership change events (58n5) only consider ambition + pressure, never whether the player has a romantic or high-fear bond to a rival candidate or the current leader.
- Player proposals (rx5v) have no mechanical way to spend or risk relationship capital.

This leaves the GDD-mandated cross-system identity ("Let combat, economy, politics, and relationships affect each other") and the user focus ("Using intimacy and relationships as political intrigue tools") as unrealized architecture even after the directed graph and romance arc are closed.

## What
Introduce two narrow, pure, deterministic query functions (no side effects, no UI, no events):

1. In src/domain/relationships/contracts.ts (or a new thin src/domain/relationships/politicalLeverage.ts if preferred for ownership clarity):
   - getRelationshipPoliticalCapital(state: GameState, factionId: string): { score: number; sources: Array<{npcId: string; reason: 'intimacy'|'fear-bond'|'romantic'|'lore-bond'; strength: number}> }
     Scans all directed edges where toId is a known faction leader/contact (via existing faction definitions or simple name match for thin slice) or any roster NPC whose factionAffinityId matches, and scores:
     - +high for intimacyStage === 'committed' or 'attachment' on player→npc when npc has faction ties.
     - +medium for high fear on npc→player when bondStatus.holderId==='player' and ownerType==='player' and the NPC has faction relevance.
     - +bonus for bondType==='romantic' or lore bonds of type 'ex-lover'/'creditor' etc. to faction NPCs.
     - Returns normalized 0-100 score + provenance list for later log/choice text.

2. getBondHolderLeverage(state: GameState, npcId: string): { canExert: boolean; risk: number; flavor: string }
   For a specific bonded NPC, returns whether the current power dynamic (fear delta + dominance diff + entryReason) is strong enough to be spent as "leverage" (e.g. coercion for intel or vote influence) and what exposure risk it carries.

The functions are the complete deliverable of this bead. They are called (but not yet acted upon) from one place inside applyPolitics as a no-op demonstration that the data is now reachable. No new state, no new commands, no UI, no content authoring.

## Why now
The directed graph + intimacy fields exist (closed). Bond creation is being wired (lfxq). The politics children are still early (P2/P3) and have not yet been implemented with their full agenda/ intrigue logic (vy9e, 58n5, 71m3). Adding the query layer now lets those beads consume real relationship and bond-holder power without inventing their own ad-hoc scans or duplicating trait math. It is the thinnest contract that turns "relationships as political tools" from a GDD aspiration into an importable pure function that later slices can depend on.

## Player impact
None visible in this slice. The queries produce data; no action, button, log line, or choice yet consumes them. This is intentional — the player-visible agency (spending the leverage in a vote or intrigue event, seeing the risk materialize) belongs to the consuming beads that will be built on top of this foundation.

## System impact
- New or extended file: src/domain/relationships/politicalLeverage.ts (preferred) or addition to contracts.ts + politicalLeverage.ts exporting the two functions + types for the return shapes.
- Minimal: src/application/commands/applyPolitics.ts — one import + one call to getRelationshipPoliticalCapital for the proposing faction (result unused, perhaps logged at debug level or simply present to prove reachability).
- Reuses without modification: contentCatalog (for faction defs if needed for leader lookup), npcRuntimeState (bondStatus + factionAffinityId), relationships map, buildRelationshipKey/getRelationship.
- No schema, no commands, no reducers, no selectors (pure domain functions), no tests outside the new module's co-located .test.ts.
- Zero impact on bondService, endDay call sites, UI, or existing relationship consumers.

## Does not include
- Any consumption logic, choice presentation, or mechanical effect when leverage > threshold (those are follow-on beads after this + the agenda rewrite vy9e).
- Any UI surfaces, tooltips, or "Leverage" numbers shown to player.
- Any new event outcomes or "useBondLeverage" action types.
- Any change to how votes are selected or resolved (vy9e owns that).
- Any authoring of specific intrigue events that use the queries (58n5 owns content).
- Any alteration of bond terminology (h3ba) or lore bonds array.
- Any player trait drift or new consequence logging (bead 3 in cluster).
- Any save data, defaults, or migration.

## Evidence
- GDD 3.5 and p61: explicit requirement that relationships and politics affect each other; faction ideologies should affect relationship play and vice versa; intrigue skill exists in schema.
- Closed destiny-l7zl: romance arc and intimacyStage were built precisely because heirs and legacy have political weight in the Valdris succession/Mira context.
- Open destiny-rjyy description: "agenda-driven proposal logic", "player can initiate their own agenda" — currently impossible to do with relationship power.
- destiny-58n5: leadership changes via "scandal" and "player intrigue NPC deployed" — the intrigue skill and relationships should supply the scandal/leverage vector.
- Current applyPolitics.ts: random template pick + only playerVote and standing deltas; zero relationship reads.
- Prime memories: "relationship and trait calculations" are core Systems work; NPC agency includes forming alliances and causing incidents that feed faction events.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. **Player-visible acceptance**
   - None required for this slice (queries are internal). The only "visible" proof is that typecheck and tests pass and a maintainer can import the functions.

2. **System / technical acceptance**
   - Two pure functions exported with the exact signatures and return shapes documented in the What section (or minor naming tweaks only if review requires).
   - Implementation lives in domain/relationships/* (inward dependency only; no imports from application, ui, or infrastructure).
   - Functions are deterministic given GameState + factionId/npcId; they never mutate, never call RNG, never touch localStorage or contentCatalog side effects.
   - The demonstration call in applyPolitics.ts compiles and does not change any observable behavior or log output.
   - Full round-trip: a fresh initial-game-state with known romantic bond + player-held bondStatus on a faction-aligned NPC produces non-zero capital score from the query.

3. **Tests / verification acceptance**
   - pnpm test:run passes, count >= 333, no regressions in any relationship or politics test.
   - pnpm typecheck zero errors.
   - New co-located politicalLeverage.test.ts (or relationships/contracts.test.ts extension) contains at least:
     - happy path with committed intimacyStage to a Gilded Court NPC yields score > 40.
     - player-held combat-capture bond on an Iron Compact NPC contributes positive fear-bond leverage.
     - no relevant bonds or mismatched factions yields score 0 with empty sources.
     - getBondHolderLeverage returns canExert true only when fear delta and dominance conditions documented in bead 1 are met.
   - 100% of new code covered by the added tests (pure functions make this trivial).

## Finding coverage
F3 (no integration of intimacy/bond leverage into politics — full), F5 (no query helpers for leverage — full), part of F6 (GDD cross-system requirement).

## Fiction contract
In Valdenmoor, pillow talk and contract leverage are the same currency, only the denomination changes. A romantic bond or a fear bond with a faction lieutenant is not private; it is a potential vote, a scandal vector, or a quiet veto. The query functions must return not only a number but the provenance list so that later intrigue text can name the exact source ("Marion's attachment to the Tallow Ring contact", "the bonded clerk who still flinches when you enter the room"). The player never presses a button labeled "use intimacy"; they simply discover that the relationships they have already built (or the bonds they already hold) now open or close political doors. The weight of that discovery is the fiction.
notes:
This bead (pure leverage queries for politics) should later consume data from the private correspondence system (destiny-fnhz). Intimacy-derived leverage, compromising letters, and relationship-based blackmail should become valid inputs for political capital and influence calculations. See destiny-fnhz and destiny-dbk6.
Sequencing note from 2026-06-02 review: leverage queries should consume pre-existing relationship depth, bond dynamics, and correspondence, not replace courtship/household progression as the main route to intimacy-adjacent content. Coordinate with destiny-jed7 umbrella.

title: Bond-holding applies power-dynamic deltas to directed player-NPC relationship edges
description:
## Why
Player-held bondStatus entries (the labor/captivity contract system from destiny-og7t / lfxq / bw2h cluster) and the directed relationship edges (Record keyed by buildRelationshipKey(from→to) in gameState.relationships, with axes + intimacyStage + bondType + legacyIntentActive from relationships/contracts.ts and closed destiny-13si + destiny-l7zl) are completely decoupled at runtime. 

No code path in bondService.ts (applyBondServiceEffects, applyMonthlyBondOperationCosts), applyWages, endDay, or adjustRelationship ever reads bondStatus.holderId/ entryReason / alongsideFreeAssignmentDays to mutate the corresponding directed edges for the holder ('player') and the held NPC. 

A combat-capture bonded NPC can retain high trust/affinity with the player indefinitely; a voluntary bond can produce no fear or loyalty asymmetry. The power imbalance of ownership is invisible to the romance system, social simulation, memory provenance, passive drift, and therefore to all future political intrigue consumers (applyPolitics, 58n5 leadership events, player-initiated votes in rjyy children).

Current holder "consequences" are limited to equality notices after 14+ alongsideFree days and one generic activity log line about the city growing meaner. This contradicts GDD 3.5 (sensual presentation pillar, "relationship choices can conflict with faction strategy", mechanical pair bonds and power) and the living-world principle that NPCs are agents reacting to the player's actual behavior.

## What
After completion, applyBondServiceEffects (the single existing call site in endDay.ts:234) will additionally compute and apply power-asymmetric deltas on the two directed relationship edges (player→bonded and bonded→player) for every roster entry where bondStatus?.holderId === 'player' && ownerType === 'player'.

Deltas are derived via pure calculation (no Math.random):
- Base fearDelta toward player: +4 to +12 scaled by (player.traits.dominance - npc.traits.empathy) / 20, +8 flat bonus if entryReason === 'combat-capture', +0 if 'voluntary', reduced by alongsideFreeAssignmentDays / 7.
- Affinity tension on player→npc: small negative if npc.traits.empathy > 55 or player.traits.ruthlessness > 60.
- Loyalty modulation on npc→player: positive if dominance diff favors control, negative friction otherwise.
- All deltas clamped to [-100,100] and applied via existing applyRelationshipDelta (which already writes capped npcMemory entries on both sides when |delta|>5).

The language of memory entries written for these power deltas must use the exact phrasing contract below. No new schema fields, no changes to bondStatus shape, no new public API surface beyond internal enrichment of the bond effects step.

## Why now
The relationship graph is directed and already carries intimacy/power fields (closed 13si/l7zl). The bond market backend is functionally complete and entry paths are being wired (open lfxq). The politics epic (open rjyy + 58n5 intrigue events + vy9e agenda rewrite) currently has zero inputs from personal power or intimacy. Adding the mechanical link here makes bond-holding (the "as bond-holder" focus) produce observable relationship state that later intrigue and UI slices can consume without retrofitting. It is the thinnest possible slice that makes the power dynamic real in the only two places that already model asymmetric human leverage (bond contracts + directed axes). Delaying leaves the GDD cross-system promise and the "visible sensual/power consequences" requirement as dead promises visible the moment bw2h lands.

## Player impact
- NPC Detail "Relations" bars (existing) for any bonded NPC will now drift in ways that visibly encode ownership (elevated fear, conditional loyalty/affinity) rather than remaining neutral or positive.
- When the player later views NPC memory (or it appears in logs), entries will explicitly surface the felt weight of the contract from both sides.
- Roster NPCs with high empathy will gradually cool toward a player who holds many or coercive bonds (via the deltas feeding existing proximity/passive systems).
- All of this is visible today with zero new UI; it simply makes the existing relationship surfaces tell the truth about power the player is already exercising via the bond market.

## System impact
- Primary: src/application/commands/bondService.ts — extend applyBondServiceEffects (or a new internal pure applyBondHolderPowerDynamics(state: GameState): GameState called from it) to perform the reads and delegate writes exclusively through applyRelationshipDelta + writeNpcMemory.
- Secondary (read-only): src/application/commands/adjustRelationship.ts (for the delta + memory helper).
- Integration: one additional pure call inside the existing applyBondServiceEffects body in bondService.ts; the endDay call site is untouched.
- No changes to: gameStateSchema (src/domain/game/contracts.ts), initial-game-state.json, any UI file, events/contracts.ts, applyPolitics.ts, contentCatalog, or lore npcs.json bonds array.
- Test surface: bondService.test.ts (existing describe block for applyBondServiceEffects) plus any co-located new pure tests.

## Does not include
- Any player-visible label, tooltip, or new panel section describing "power dynamic" or "sensual leverage" (that is UI/UX scope for a later polish bead after bw2h).
- Any new event templates, createBond outcome extensions, or entry reason paths (lfxq).
- Any consumption of the new deltas for council votes, faction proposals, blackmail, or intrigue success modifiers (those are separate thin slices #2 and #3 in this cluster).
- Any change to playerCharacter.traits, NPC traits, or permanent corruption counters.
- Any modification of lore "bonds" arrays on NpcDefinition (the h3ba terminology problem).
- Any save migration, new required fields, or defaulting logic.
- Any explicit erotic content, pregnancy triggers, or policy-violating framing (per the l7zl policy note and docs/content-policy.md).
- Any work on agendaAxes, leader NPCs, or vote mechanical outcomes (rjyy children).

## Evidence
- Direct code audit: bondStatus never appears in any relationship mutation site; applyBondServiceEffects (bondService.ts:169-207) only mutates alongsideFreeAssignmentDays and delegates to applyMonthlyBondOperationCosts for the single "city grows meaner" log.
- GDD: section 3.5 "Sensual presentation as a design pillar", "relationship choices can conflict with faction strategy, economics, and combat readiness", courtship loop ending in pair bond with mechanical effects, "make ... intimacy choices feed actual relationship outcomes".
- Closed beads: destiny-13si (directed graph requirement), destiny-l7zl (intimacyStage + bondType + legacyIntentActive + fiction contract on weight of power/legacy in dark register; explicit "Four failure mode audit" on coercive vs consensual).
- Open beads in cluster: destiny-bw2h (backend complete but invisible), destiny-lfxq (creation paths), destiny-rjyy + destiny-58n5 (intrigue/politics with zero personal leverage inputs), destiny-h3ba (still-unresolved three-way "bond" name collision).
- Prime memories + NPC panel: "relationship and trait calculations" are Systems responsibility; "NPCs are world agents"; directed bonds were P1 architectural fix precisely to enable asymmetric power, courtship, manipulation.
- User directive: focus on "Sensual / intimate / power-dynamic leverage in relationships and bonds", "Using intimacy and relationships as political intrigue tools", "Making bond-holding carry visible sensual/power consequences for the player (as bond-holder)".

## Acceptance Criteria
1. **Player-visible acceptance**
   - For any roster NPC with player-held bondStatus (any of the five entryReasons), after endDay the directed edges player→npc and npc→player differ from a control state without the bond in at least the fear and affinity axes by amounts consistent with the documented formulas.
   - At least one npcMemory entry (on the bonded NPC and/or player side) whose event text references the contract, ownership, or felt power imbalance in a register consistent with the fiction contract.
   - Existing relationship surfaces (NpcDetailPanel bars, any memory viewers) continue to function; no new crashes or NaN values.

2. **System / technical acceptance**
   - All power-dynamic mutation logic lives in bondService.ts and calls only the pre-existing applyRelationshipDelta (from adjustRelationship) and writeNpcMemory for side effects.
   - The enrichment is inside (or called by) the existing applyBondServiceEffects function so that the endDay.ts call site requires zero edits.
   - Calculation is pure given the input GameState snapshot (no hidden mutable state, no global RNG).
   - No gameStateSchema change; no new top-level fields; no alteration of bondStatus shape or any existing bondStatus field semantics.

3. **Tests / verification acceptance**
   - pnpm test:run completes with zero failures and total test count not below 333.
   - pnpm typecheck reports zero errors.
   - bondService.test.ts (or new pure test file co-located) contains at minimum four new cases exercising the power path:
     - combat-capture bond + high player dominance produces larger fear delta than voluntary bond.
     - bonded NPC with empathy > 60 produces negative affinity drift on the player→npc edge.
     - memory write occurs on both directed sides when |delta| > 5.
     - voluntary long-term bond with alongsideFree days > 7 produces measurably smaller fear accumulation than fresh combat-capture.
   - All pre-existing bondService and relationship tests continue to pass without modification to their expectations.

## Finding coverage
F2 (bondStatus and directed rels decoupled — full), F4 (holder consequences too thin — full for the relationship/memory slice), part of F6 (GDD cross-system sensual/power pillar — this mechanical link).

## Fiction contract
Bond-holding is never a neutral administrative fact in Valdenmoor. The contract places one body in the power of another; that asymmetry is registered first in the nervous systems of both parties and only later (if ever) in council minutes or ransom notes. The player who holds the paper feels the leverage as a private weather system — heavier some nights than others — while the city registers the same fact as a general meanness in the air. Every delta and every memory line written by this system must preserve the truth that the power is erotic in the oldest sense (charged, bodily, consequential) before it is ever strategic.
notes:
Bond-holding power dynamics (this bead) will later strongly influence the tone and blackmail potential of private correspondence (destiny-fnhz), especially when one party holds a bond on the other. Intimate or compromising letters can become extremely potent leverage in such cases.
Drift guard from 2026-06-02 review: bond power dynamics may intensify or complicate intimacy, but they should not be treated as the core player-facing courtship path. Coordinate with destiny-jed7 umbrella and especially destiny-tr29/68j4.

title: Retune overpromising combat contract copy or add specialized handlers
description:
Why
The quest authoring audit still contains combat contracts whose copy implies escort or extraction behavior that the current combat runtime does not stage. Without a real follow-up bead, those findings remain unmapped and the audit stays incomplete.

What
Review the remaining combat contracts with escort, extraction, or similar high-specificity framing and either narrow the authored copy to the current combat loop or add targeted handler follow-ups where needed.

Why now
The investigation and delivery/survival authoring cleanup should not leave combat-side fiction drift behind.

Player impact
Combat leads read honestly and stop promising escort or capture behavior the player cannot actually perform yet.

System impact
Quest content definitions, combat-adjacent contract authoring, and small follow-up tests if metadata changes.

Does not include
No full escort system. No prisoner extraction mechanic.

Acceptance
1. Every remaining combat contract flagged as overpromising in the audit is mapped to content retune or a deeper handler bead.
2. No combat briefing implies escort or extraction behavior unless runtime support exists.
3. The quest authoring audit has no dangling placeholder bead IDs after this mapping.

Finding coverage
Follow-up gap from the 2026-06-01 quest authoring alignment audit for combat-side fiction/runtime mismatch.
close_reason: Combat quest copy retuned: escort→clearance title, extraction→safehouse attention, survival briefing clarified

title: Audit and retune delivery/survival contract copy after duration-contract rollout
description:
Why
The duration-contract rollout fixed the loudest escort mismatch, but delivery and survival quests still vary widely in how strongly their copy implies multi-watch, travel, cleanup, or guard behavior.

What
Review all delivery and survival quest briefings and adjust either content or execution metadata so the current on-site loop is truthful.

Why now
This is the next smallest follow-up needed after the quest-duration fixes.

Player impact
Delivery and hold jobs read honestly and feel less arbitrary.

System impact
Quest content definitions, execution metadata, and a small test pass only.

Does not include
No combat changes. No route-map redesign.

Acceptance
1. Every delivery/survival quest is classified as one-watch, multi-watch, or copy-too-strong.
2. Any mismatch is resolved in content or mapped to a follow-up bead.
3. Work Board and on-site execution surfaces remain consistent with the final metadata.

Finding coverage
F7: remaining delivery/survival copy may still overpromise relative to the current loop.
close_reason: Delivery and survival contract copy retuned to match current execution metadata and on-site loops.

title: Economics: align existing hired merc wages with market contract rates
description:
## Why
The project already computes skill-based market wages for hire offers, but existing hired mercenary fixtures and some wage fallbacks still behave like flat status-rate labor. That leaves the economics model split between market contracts and runtime payroll.

## What
Align existing hired mercenary runtime entries with the same contract-rate logic used by recruitment offers, without rewriting every status-based wage in one pass.

## Why now
This is the safest next slice of destiny-k988 after wage-debt consequences. It reduces the largest live mismatch without forcing a full economy rebalance.

## Player impact
Hired mercenaries behave more consistently: the rate you hire them at is the rate they keep costing.

## System impact
Touches shared hire/wage calculation, hire fixtures, wage application, and end-day economy tests.

## Does not include
No full wage redesign for retainers, servants, nobles, or family.
No ledger UI redesign.

## Evidence
Epic destiny-k988, generateDistrictHireOffers skill-based wages, applyWages status fallback, and current hired-merc fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible acceptance: hired mercenary wages remain consistent between offer generation and ongoing payroll.
2. System / technical acceptance: one shared contract-rate path exists for the targeted mercenary slice.
3. Tests / verification acceptance: recruitment, wages, and endDay economy tests stay green.

## Finding coverage
k988 F2

title: Economics: unpaid wages cause real roster consequences
description:
## Why
NPCs can currently remain unpaid far too long without believable intermediate fallout, which makes wage debt feel gamey instead of social and economic.

## What
Add a thin slice of the economics epic: unpaid wages create escalating roster consequences before final departure, including assignment refusal or morale collapse, and the house receives visible warnings.

## Why now
This is the clearest exploit inside the economics epic and the safest piece to land independently.

## Player impact
The player can no longer ignore wage debt as a harmless hidden counter. Unpaid staff will visibly sour, refuse work, or leave.

## System impact
Touches wage application, end-of-day roster consequences, activity log messaging, and relevant tests.

## Does not include
No hire-rate unification. No ledger income redesign. No full economy UI pass.

## Evidence
Epic destiny-k988, current applyWages behavior, and session retro on thin-slice economics fixes.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible acceptance: unpaid wages produce visible escalating warnings and at least one consequence before final departure.
2. System / technical acceptance: roster state changes in a deterministic way based on wage arrears and NPC context.
3. Tests / verification acceptance: targeted wage/endDay tests cover the escalation path and full project gates stay green.

## Finding coverage
k988 F1

title: Let NPC households expand and repurpose world sites over time
description:
## Why
The clarified direction explicitly requires non-player households and NPCs to expand, repurpose, and improve their own buildings when they have the means. Without that, only the player meaningfully changes space and the city stops feeling alive.

## What
Add NPC and household site expansion/renovation logic.

Cover:
- when NPC households can afford expansion or repair
- how rooms are added, repurposed, or hardened
- security, amenity, and custody implications
- how new site capacity changes social and quest pressure
- what changes remain visible after the site returns to abstraction

## Why now
This is required if buildings are meant to belong to all actors, not only the player.

## Player impact
The player sees rival houses, safehouses, shrines, and faction buildings evolve over time instead of remaining static set dressing.

## System impact
- extends worldHousehold data from authored snapshot toward living institutions
- ties economy and world simulation into physical site change

## Does not include
- decorative-only map art refreshes
- full construction minigames
- player base-building overhaul by itself

## Evidence
world-sites-captivity-narrative-review-2026-05-29.md; current world households already imply rooms, debt pressure, and hidden custody but remain structurally static.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. NPC-controlled sites can gain, lose, or repurpose room capacity based on world conditions.
2. These changes affect security, custody, or social outcomes.
3. Abstracted sites still remember the structural changes when reconcretized.

## Finding coverage
F1 F3 F4
close_reason: Superseded by pre-existing destiny-v1tx (same non-player household growth scope).

title: NPC room interaction and captive-interaction rules
description:
## Why
The user wants NPCs to interact with rooms and with captives in those rooms. Occupancy alone only says where someone is; it does not define what they do there.

## What
Define and implement interaction rules for NPCs in concrete locations: dwell, work, guard, visit, question, tend, smuggle supplies, comfort, abuse, conspire, and observe. These interactions must also feed abstract state when the location collapses back.

## Why now
Without this layer, concretized locations remain visually explicit but behaviorally shallow.

## Player impact
Rooms become places where relationships and consequences are produced, not just labels.

## System impact
Builds on occupancy substrate and captivity runtime. Must integrate with relationships, events, rumors, and content policy.

## Does not include
No cinematic scene system. No explicit exploitation mechanics.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Concrete locations support meaningful room-level NPC actions.
2. Captive interactions can change condition, compliance, trust, fear, and clue state within content-policy limits.
3. Interaction outcomes can survive collapse back to abstract runtime state.

## Finding coverage
F3 F4 F5
close_reason: Implemented concrete-site room interactions: guard pressure, protective care, and observed custody clues that persist through collapse-back.

title: Author room blueprints for all enterable houses and establishments
description:
## Why
The new direction requires that all enterable houses and similar interiors have rooms. A runtime substrate alone will not guarantee authored room coverage for the actual places the player can enter.

## What
Audit enterable POIs, residences, safe houses, taverns, detention sites, and other interior locations, then author room blueprints for each relevant place. Blueprint scope should include named rooms, room roles, basic capacity, and any special security or clue tags.

## Why now
Without this content pass, the architecture can land while the world still behaves like a partial menu shell.

## Player impact
Enterable places feel spatially real and support room-specific discovery, rescue, hiding, and interaction.

## System impact
Content and schema alignment across POIs and world households.

## Does not include
No full art pass. No tactical maps.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Every enterable house or establishment has an authored room blueprint.
2. Blueprint naming and role tags are consistent with the general location runtime.
3. Key story locations such as Mira's holding site can be represented without bespoke exceptions.

## Finding coverage
F1 F2
close_reason: Authored POI room blueprints, added site tags for safehouse/holding-site coverage, and modeled the old tannery as a real location with rooms.

title: Location concretization and collapse-back lifecycle
description:
## Why
The clarified direction explicitly requires that locations may later fall back from concrete to abstract state. Without an explicit lifecycle, concretized places will either bloat permanently or lose important state when collapsed.

## What
Define and implement the lifecycle rules for when a location becomes concrete, when it may collapse back to abstract state, what transient room data is discarded, what persistent consequences must remain, and how quests, captives, occupancy, clues, and repairs survive collapse.

## Why now
This is the core mechanism that makes the hybrid model operational rather than just aspirational.

## Player impact
The world can stay broad without feeling fake or memoryless.

## System impact
Touches persistence, selectors, and any future location runtime.

## Does not include
No new authored content.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Collapse-back preserves durable consequences and references.
2. Concretization is deterministic from abstract state plus authored definition.
3. Quest and captive references do not break when locations collapse.

## Finding coverage
F5
close_reason: Added persisted siteRuntimes plus deterministic materialization, concretize/collapse lifecycle commands, reducer hooks, known-room retention, and regression coverage ensuring collapse-back preserves captivity and room knowledge.

title: NPC household growth and room expansion for non-player actors
description:
## Why
The user wants NPCs and foreign houses to expand their own buildings when they have money, and to use those rooms socially and politically. Current world households are authored static records.

## What
Add household growth rules for non-player actors: NPC or world households can invest in repairs, added rooms, or upgraded room functions; resources and wealth can change location capability; new rooms can affect security, concealment, care capacity, and social influence.

## Why now
A general location substrate without non-player household growth would keep the world reactive only for the player.

## Player impact
Rival houses and institutions evolve materially over time, not just in text.

## System impact
Builds on worldHouseholds plus new location runtime. Must integrate with faction resources and world simulation.

## Does not include
No city-scale construction economy overhaul.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Non-player households can change room capacity or function over time.
2. Growth affects later occupancy, captivity, and infiltration conditions.
3. Changes persist whether the location is abstract or concrete.

## Finding coverage
F1 F4
close_reason: Non-player households now invest in growth, add or repurpose rooms, deepen capacity over time, and persist those changes across abstract/concrete site lifecycle.

title: Abstract custody simulation: offscreen care, coercion, visits, and psychological consequences
description:
## Why
The user explicitly rejected shallow offscreen simulation. Hybrid only works if abstract locations still preserve depth.

## What
Model offscreen custody and room ecology at a deep abstract level: care or neglect, food, water, sanitation, guard pressure, movement restriction, interrogation, visits by other NPCs, relationship and trauma shifts, dependency, fear, rumor, and survival-bond development within content-policy constraints, plus escape, rescue window, illness, death, transfer, and clue generation.

## Why now
Without this, abstract locations become narrative dead zones and the hybrid model loses the project's intended depth.

## Player impact
Even unseen places continue to matter and generate consequences.

## System impact
New abstract simulation rules layered on top of captivity and location runtime. Must integrate with events, rumors, relationships, and world NPC systems.

## Does not include
No exploitation framing. Must follow content policy and consequence-first tone.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Offscreen captivity can evolve meaningfully without concrete room-by-room ticking.
2. Simulation outputs can surface as events, rumors, quest leads, and state shifts.
3. The same NPC can later be concretized without losing the meaningful abstract history.

## Finding coverage
F3 F4 F5
close_reason: Abstract custody now simulates harsh and protective offscreen regimes, emits rumors and custody alerts, and preserves those consequences when sites later become concrete.

title: Bond management UI: BondStatusPanel in NpcDetailPanel for sale listing and rescue actions
description:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BondStatusPanel renders in NpcDetailPanel when bondStatus is non-null
- [ ] Player-held view shows entry reason, contract value, term, free button, and for-sale toggle+value
- [ ] Free NPC button dispatches freeNpc and is disabled when money < contractValue
- [ ] markNpcForSale dispatches with correct npcId, marketValue, forSale
- [ ] NPC-held view shows holder, decay hint, and three rescue buttons
- [ ] Legal rescue disabled when player cannot afford ransom
- [ ] Transferred NPCs are visible in RosterScreen (not filtered out)
- [ ] NpcDetailPanel.test.tsx covers player-held and NPC-held bond panel states
- [ ] Typecheck and test:run pass
notes: Consistency audit 2026-06-11: this bead predates the Muster Roll overhaul (ed24a34). NpcDetailPanel.tsx (not .ts) was fully restructured: header band (portrait frame + identity + muster-action-row with Talk/Offer Gift/Court/Assign Title), then CharacterSection, tabs, DutySection, EquipmentSection — all in src/ui/screens/roster.css 'muster-*' classes. A BondStatusPanel should slot in as a section after EquipmentSection following the muster-section-title pattern, and sale/rescue actions belong in the muster-action-row, not a separate column. Selector names referenced here must be re-verified against src/application/selectors before implementation.

title: Wire bond entry reasons: add createBond outcome type and hook the five entry paths
description:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] createBond outcome type exists in schema, validated by Zod
- [ ] applyEventOutcome handles createBond and sets bondStatus with correct entryReason
- [ ] At minimum compact-assessment and debt-settlement paths are wired to real events/commands
- [ ] Tests cover applyEventOutcome createBond case for each wired path
- [ ] Typecheck and test:run pass
notes:
Bond entry reasons (this bead) should be able to seed initial correspondence or relationship context that later generates private letters. Certain entry paths (e.g. combat-capture, debt-settlement) have natural potential to create compromising or intimate correspondence. See destiny-fnhz.
Entry-path note: bond creation paths can seed compromised or power-heavy intimacy contexts, but they are not the replacement for explicit courtship/household progression. See destiny-jed7 umbrella.

title: Player-initiated council votes via houseWardSeats or faction sponsorship
description:
## Why this exists
House Valdric starts politically stripped — houseWardSeats = 0. Restoring political standing is a core progression arc. Once the player regains a ward seat OR builds standing >= 50 with an allied faction, they should be able to put something on the council table. This is the payoff for the political systems the player has been navigating.

## What needs to be done
Two access paths to initiate a vote:

Path A — Direct (houseWardSeats >= 1):
- Player selects a vote template from a curated list of 'house proposals' (authored separately from faction templates)
- Proposal fires immediately into activeCouncilVotes with proposingFactionId = 'house-valdric'
- No sponsorship needed

Path B — Sponsored (faction standing >= 50 with any faction):
- Player can attach their proposal to an allied faction's name
- Faction standing >= 50 required
- Allied faction's leader approves or refuses based on their traits (ambitious leader more likely to co-sponsor if it aligns with their values)
- If approved, vote fires under that faction's name

New game state field: 'houseProposalCooldown' — player can only propose once every 10 days to prevent spam.

UI: Add 'Propose a Vote' button to FactionsScreen, gated by access conditions. Show available templates, sponsorship options.

## Why now
Depends on destiny-vy9e (agenda scoring working). houseWardSeats field already exists in state. This is the player-facing payoff for the political progression arc.

## Player impact
Political agency. The council is no longer something that happens to the player.

## System impact
New gameSlice action (proposeCouncilVote), new state field (houseProposalCooldown), new house-proposals content file, FactionsScreen UI addition.

## Non-goals
- House Valdric having faction members vote on their behalf (that requires allied NPC voting blocks)
- Vote campaigning mechanics

## Acceptance Criteria
- Player with houseWardSeats >= 1 can initiate a vote
- Player with faction standing >= 50 can sponsor a vote through that faction
- Cooldown prevents more than one proposal per 10 days
- Faction leader refusal is possible and logged
- Test: proposeCouncilVote adds to activeCouncilVotes with correct proposer

title: Rewrite applyPolitics vote selection to use faction agenda scoring
description:
## Why this exists
applyPolitics currently picks a vote template at random. The Tallow Ring can propose Compact patrol expansions. This breaks faction coherence — each faction has a defined agenda but the engine ignores it. The agendaAxes data (authored in the prerequisite task) exists to drive this.

## What needs to be done
Replace Math.random() template selection in applyPolitics with a scoring function:
1. For each faction, score their eligibility to propose: activePressure + trigger conditions from agendaAxes.proposesWhen
2. Pick the faction with highest score as proposer
3. From that faction's vote templates (filtered by agendaAxes.values tag match), pick the most contextually relevant one
4. If no faction meets threshold, no vote fires that cycle (silence is also meaningful)

Pressure relief: a faction that successfully passes a vote has its activePressure reduced.

## Why now
Depends on destiny-2lg6 (agendaAxes data). This is the core logic change that makes politics feel intentional.

## Player impact
Players can anticipate what the Compact will push when unrest is high. The Gilded Court proposing licensing fees after prosperity drops makes narrative sense.

## System impact
applyPolitics.ts rewrite of step 7. No schema changes — reads from agendaAxes already in factions.json.

## Non-goals
- Faction leader traits (separate layer)
- Player-initiated votes (separate task)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A state with Compact activePressure=80 and unrest=70 reliably produces a Compact order/patrol vote
- No faction proposes a vote that contradicts their agendaAxes.values
- Test: three different world states produce three different proposing factions

title: Fix: council vote effects are text-only — mechanical outcomes never applied
description:
## Why this exists
Every council-votes.json entry has an 'effect' field describing mechanical consequences (shop prices +5%, standing deltas, district access changes). None of these are applied. applyPolitics logs the text but dispatches no state changes. Players see 'Compact Patrol Expansion passed — Tallow Ring standing decreases by 10' but nothing actually changes.

## What needs to be done
Parse the effect field into structured data (or add a separate 'mechanicalEffects' array to each vote template). On resolution, apply the effects: faction standing deltas, city dial changes, temporary district access flags, market pressure changes. Each effect type needs a handler in applyPolitics or a new applyVoteEffects command.

## Why now
Without this, council votes are purely cosmetic. The fix is the prerequisite for players ever caring about vote outcomes.

## Player impact
Vote outcomes now actually change the world. Supporting or blocking a proposal has real consequences.

## System impact
council-votes.json schema change (add structured effects), new applyVoteEffects command, applyPolitics resolution step updated.

## Non-goals
- Temp-effect duration tracking (effects can start as permanent deltas)

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 3 vote templates have structured effects applied on resolution
- Standing deltas in effect field are applied to the correct factions on pass/fail
- Existing tests extended to verify state changes after vote resolution

title: Author agendaAxes data for all 6 factions
description:
## Why this exists
factions.json has a prose 'agenda' field per faction. It's display text only. The vote selection logic in applyPolitics cannot read intent from prose. For agenda-driven vote proposals to work, each faction needs a machine-readable agendaAxes block that encodes what they value and when they act.

## What needs to be done
Add 'agendaAxes' to each faction in factions.json:
- 'values': array of policy tags matching vote template tags (e.g. ['order', 'suppression', 'trade'])
- 'proposesWhen': conditions object — cityUnrestAbove, factionPressureAbove, standingWithTargetBelow, prosperityBelow
- 'blocksWhen': conditions under which they would spend pressure to oppose a vote

Also add a 'tags' array to each council-votes.json template so the matching can work.

Faction values to author:
- Compact: order, suppression, trade, patrol
- Gilded Court: prestige, debt, elite-access, licensing
- Foundry League: labor, industrial, safety, leverage
- Tallow Ring: contraband, exemption, profit, opacity
- The Restored: abolition, welfare, community, anti-compact
- House Merrow: customs, tolls, succession, harbor

## Why now
All subsequent agenda-driven logic depends on this data existing. Pure authoring work — no code change.

## Player impact
Indirect — factions begin proposing votes that fit their stated purpose.

## System impact
factions.json and council-votes.json schema changes only.

## Non-goals
- Code to read agendaAxes (separate task)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 6 factions have agendaAxes with values and proposesWhen
- All 8 vote templates have tags array
- Domain schema (factions/contracts.ts) updated to include the new fields
- TypeScript compiles without error

title: [epic] Council Politics: Agenda-Driven Votes, Faction Leaders, and Player Proposals
description:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each faction has agendaAxes defining their proposal preferences and conditions
- [ ] Vote selection uses agenda scoring instead of random templates
- [ ] Faction leaders (world NPCs) have traits that skew proposal tendency
- [ ] Player can initiate votes via houseWardSeats >= 1 or faction sponsorship at standing >= 50
- [ ] Agenda trees implemented: multi-step vote chains with unlock conditions
- [ ] Final node in agenda tree applies world-state changes (standing, city dial, mechanics)
- [ ] FactionsScreen shows faction agendas and current proposals
- [ ] Tests verify agenda-driven vote selection and tree progression
- [ ] Typecheck and test:run pass
notes:
New P2 thin slices filed from Systems analysis of bond/politics cluster + sensual/power focus (2026-06-01): destiny-dbk6 (power-dynamic rel deltas from bondStatus), destiny-e5c6 (pure leverage queries for intrigue/politics consumers), destiny-6sx6 (holder-side trait drift + roster friction + charged log consequences). These provide the missing cross-system link between bond holding (as power) and both directed relationships + political intrigue per GDD 3.5. See each for fiction contracts and exact acceptance. Do not implement before h3ba terminology hygiene or P1 blockers.
Correspondence system (destiny-fnhz) will become a major future input for political leverage and intrigue. Private letters (especially intimate or compromising ones) between NPCs and with the player should eventually feed agenda scoring, leader intrigue, scandals, and player proposals. See destiny-fnhz + destiny-58n5.
Cluster update: destiny-fnhz (correspondence) is now connected as future input for intrigue and leverage. Related: e5c6, 6sx6, 58n5, dbk6, bw2h, lfxq, h3ba, vvfh.

title: Add selectShopPricingBreakdown selector for price transparency
description: Why: ShopsScreen.tsx:121-139 formats market pressure labels inline (boundary violation). Player sees 15% discount but cannot understand how faction standing, corridor status, and market pressure combine. What: create selectShopPricingBreakdown(shopId, itemId) returning basePrice, marketMod, factionMod, corridorMod, finalPrice. Show breakdown as tooltip or collapsed section. Acceptance: no price formatting logic in ShopsScreen component; player can see what drives price changes.

title: Unify NPC status labels and money format across all screens
description: Why: NPC assignment state is displayed differently per screen (group label Available, badge idle, filter by readiness.blocked). Money shows as X Mk, X Marks, X marks depending on location. What: (1) create ASSIGNMENT_DISPLAY_LABELS map used everywhere; (2) use formatMarks/formatMarksAbbrev helpers across all screens; (3) audit action buttons - standardise Repair cost Mk, Pay debt amount Mk, Accept contract. Acceptance: NPC status label is identical in all contexts.
notes:
Scope check: assignment labels are currently split across RosterScreen, NpcDetailPanel, MissionPrepScreen, ExpeditionPrepScreen, DashboardScreen, and quest readiness badges. Money presentation is mostly on formatMarks/formatMarksAbbrev already, but GlobalStatusBar still prints raw Mk strings and several tests/docs hard-code older variants. Next thin slice should centralize assignment-display labels first, then tighten screen-level money/button text in a separate pass if needed.
Implemented a canonical assignment display contract in src/application/content/assignmentDisplay.ts and wired it through RosterScreen, NpcDetailPanel, MissionPrepScreen, and ExpeditionPrepScreen so UI surfaces no longer leak raw runtime keys like idle/assigned_title. Also standardized visible money formatting across GlobalStatusBar, DashboardScreen, RecruitmentScreen, LedgerScreen, and HouseScreen using formatMarks/formatMarksAbbrev/formatMarksPerDay/formatMarksPerWeek. Added regression coverage in assignmentDisplay.test.ts and updated screen tests for roster, mission prep, dashboard, recruitment, house, and smoke flows.

title: Add post-combat result screen
description: Why: combat ends and player returns to previous screen with no summary. No screen shows casualties, loot, faction changes, or quest progress. What: add CombatResultScreen or modal after combat resolution showing: outcome label, squad losses and injuries, items acquired, Marks gained, faction standing changes, quest progress triggered. Acceptance: every combat encounter shows a result summary before navigation.

title: Add per-operative investigation result breakdown
description: Why: investigation resolves to success/partial/failure with no per-operative detail. Player cannot tell which skill check passed or why - results feel random. What: create selectLastInvestigationResult() returning per-NPC roll details. Show breakdown on result screen: operative name, skill used, roll value, outcome. Acceptance: investigation result screen shows per-operative contributions.
notes: 2026-06-01 Scope guard from quest duration audit: this task improves investigation result readability only. It does NOT fix investigation pacing, multi-day surveillance semantics, or same-day settlement; those are tracked in destiny-4vdq and destiny-ea80.

title: Make purchase feedback persistent via activity log
