acceptance_criteria: Combat can be entered in the UI and resolved through the first supported action loop with tests and validation passing.
close_reason: Combat milestone decomposed into executable domain and UI tasks.

title: Update art direction and derive next UI/design milestones
description: Why: The art direction must incorporate the chosen sensual character-render strategy and the project needs new backlog milestones derived from the visual analysis. What: update art-direction docs and create the next UI/design beads milestones with clear ownership and scope.
acceptance_criteria: Art-direction docs explicitly cover sensual character presentation and new UI/design milestone beads exist with concrete scope.
close_reason: Art direction updated with sensual character presentation section. Six new UI/design milestone beads created (destiny-gp9 portrait style guide P1, destiny-km2 left-rail nav P2, destiny-9wx text hierarchy P2, destiny-gq0 NPC detail screen P2, destiny-hqx shop district context P2, destiny-65e combat range visibility P3) with ownership, scope, and dependencies.

title: Create UI analysis and reference synthesis
description: Why: The project needs a dedicated reference-analysis document that extracts reusable UI, navigation, information architecture, and content-surface patterns from the provided Masters of Raana screenshots. What: create a new UI analysis/reference document with concrete recommendations for this project.
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated UI analysis/reference document exists and contains concrete patterns, anti-patterns, and recommendations for this project.
close_reason: UI analysis and reference synthesis document created at docs/mor-ui-analysis.md. Covers 14 MoR screenshots: navigation structure, NPC panel patterns, stat display, shop surfaces, event overlays, combat screen, portrait presentation, anti-patterns, and adoptable patterns with concrete Destiny recommendations.

title: Update game design document with inheritance and sensual presentation
description: Why: The current design doc should explicitly reflect the newly clarified product direction: relationships, attraction, reproduction, inherited traits/stats, and sensual character presentation as part of the core gameplay fantasy. What: update GAME_DESIGN_DOCUMENT.md accordingly and capture the systemic implications.
acceptance_criteria: GAME_DESIGN_DOCUMENT.md explicitly covers attraction/relationship presentation, reproduction, inheritance, and the resulting UI/system implications.

title: Design synthesis from Masters of Raana visual analysis
description: Why: The project needs explicit documentation of UI, game design, and art-direction insights derived from the collected Masters of Raana reference screens, including sensual/sexualized character presentation as a deliberate product pillar. What: create a short design epic covering document updates and the next backlog milestones.
acceptance_criteria: Epic exists to track the reference-analysis follow-up work.
close_reason: Umbrella issue created and decomposed into executable documentation and backlog tasks.

title: UI/UX: refine session feedback and guardrails
description: Improve save/load clarity with concise state feedback and guardrails around session restoration. Keep the interaction clear and low-friction without adding heavy modal flow unless needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- feedback is explicit
- restore interaction is understandable
- UI remains simple
notes: Refined save/load UX with explicit restore guidance, disabled load until a snapshot exists, and clearer session feedback. Added test coverage for the guardrail behavior while keeping the interaction lightweight and aligned with the application persistence services. Validation passed: pnpm lint, pnpm test:run, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.

title: UI/UX: refine shop comparison and purchase feedback
description: Add clear purchase feedback, comparison cues, and economy clarity for the first shop flow. Improve readability of item value, category, and district context without moving rules out of the application layer.
acceptance_criteria:
- purchase feedback is explicit
- comparison cues exist
- UI stays selector-driven
notes: Refined the shop flow with explicit purchase feedback, affordability cues, and cross-shop best-price comparison while keeping the UI selector-driven. The screen now exposes economy decisions more clearly without moving rules out of the application layer. Validation passed: pnpm lint, pnpm test:run, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.

title: Art Direction: create first visual prompt and asset baseline
description: Expand art-direction guidance into a first reusable visual baseline. Define palette direction, faction mark guidance, district mood references, and a prompt pack for generated concept assets that supports both UI and world tone.
acceptance_criteria:
- first visual baseline exists
- prompt guidance is reusable
- UI and world-art direction are coherent
notes: Expanded docs/art-direction.md into the first visual baseline. Added palette direction, faction-mark guidance, district mood references, UI/world coherence rules, reusable prompt templates, and early asset naming and backlog guidance.

title: UI/UX: define screen standards for management surfaces
description: Expand the UI principles into concrete screen standards for dashboard, roster, districts, factions, shops, and mission prep. Focus on hierarchy, comparison patterns, state visibility, and action placement for dense strategy surfaces.
acceptance_criteria:
- core management screens have explicit UX rules
- screen-specific hierarchy guidance exists
- guidance is aligned with current architecture and selectors
notes: Expanded docs/ui-principles.md into concrete management-screen standards for Dashboard, Roster, Districts, Factions, Shops, and Mission Prep. Added explicit hierarchy, layout, comparison, state-visibility, and action-placement guidance aligned with the current architecture and selector model.

title: Narrative: create first faction and district bible
description: Expand the narrative source of truth into a reusable faction and district bible. Define distinct motives, social base, tone, and conflict posture for the seeded factions and districts so future quests, NPCs, and events can stay coherent.
acceptance_criteria:
- faction identities are distinct
- seeded districts have narrative framing
- content is concise and system-compatible
notes: Expanded docs/narrative.md into the first faction and district bible. Defined distinct motives, social base, methods of power, hidden flaws, conflict posture, district emotional character, and writing guidance for the seeded factions and districts.

title: Milestone 3: Narrative and experience foundations
description:
Establish the first reusable creative foundations for Project Destiny so future content and UI work stay coherent. This milestone focuses on faction identity, game-specific UI principles, and initial art-direction assets that support the existing systemic prototype without overriding product or architecture constraints.

Sections:
- faction and district narrative identity
- UI/UX screen standards for strategy-heavy surfaces
- first art-direction and prompt-pack baseline

title: Infrastructure: add local save snapshot adapter
description: Create a first local save adapter boundary for persisting and restoring the game state snapshot. Keep storage details in infrastructure and validate saved data through the existing schemas.
acceptance_criteria:
- save adapter exists
- load path validates via schema
- domain remains storage-agnostic
notes: Added a first persistence boundary with an application save-store port and an infrastructure local snapshot adapter. The adapter validates on save and load via the existing game-state schema, and tests cover round-trip, empty storage, and invalid snapshot rejection. Validation passed: pnpm lint, pnpm test:run, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.

title: UI: implement mission prep shell with seeded squad state
description: Replace the mission prep placeholder with a mission-prep shell that shows selected squad members, available roster members, and high-level readiness information from the current game state.
acceptance_criteria:
- mission prep route renders selected squad
- available roster is visible
- no combat rules are implemented in the UI layer
notes: Implemented the mission prep route as a read-only shell backed by an application selector that separates the selected squad from the remaining roster. The UI renders seeded squad readiness information without embedding combat or assignment rules. Validation passed: pnpm lint, pnpm test:run, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.

title: UI: implement districts and factions overview screens
description: Replace the districts and factions placeholders with read-only overview screens backed by the application state and seeded data.
acceptance_criteria:
- districts route renders district overview
- factions route renders faction overview
- screens use shared application selectors
notes: Implemented the districts and factions routes as read-only overview screens backed by shared application selectors. The application layer now joins runtime district and faction state with seeded definition data so the UI can render names, agendas, control, danger, market pressure, and standing without importing raw JSON directly. Validation passed: pnpm lint, pnpm test:run, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.

title: UI: build initial app shell and navigation skeleton
description: Create the first application shell and screen navigation structure once the workspace scaffold and initial architecture are defined. The shell should reflect the intended feature boundaries without embedding business rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- app shell exists
- navigation structure matches planned feature boundaries
- UI layer does not own domain behavior
notes: Replaced the placeholder home page with a route-level application shell using React Router. Added navigation and placeholder screens for Dashboard, Roster, Districts, Shops, Factions, Mission Prep, Combat, and Event Log so future UI work starts from the correct feature boundaries without embedding business rules. Validation passed: pnpm lint, pnpm test:run, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.

title: Data: define content schema and sample seed files
description: Create the first content schema layout and sample seed files for districts, factions, items, and NPCs. Data definitions must remain separate from mutable runtime state.
acceptance_criteria:
- data folder structure is defined
- sample seed files exist
- schema approach aligns with domain contracts
notes: Defined the initial data layout under data/definitions and data/runtime, added district definitions and sample seed files for districts, factions, items, NPCs, and initial game state, and added an infrastructure-level validation test to ensure the seed content aligns with the domain contracts. Validation passed: pnpm lint, pnpm test:run, pnpm typecheck, pnpm build.

title: Economy readability: surface food stock, prices, supply-chain status and player options
description:
## Type
Implementation bead (presentation). Makes the Food Supply Chain legible under epic destiny-dlud. Depends on destiny-6nga (stock/derived foodSecurity) and destiny-ia9u (prices/derived marketPressure). Label ui-ux.

## Why
A living economy the player cannot SEE is just hidden math. The audit found city resources and NPC needs already have tick consequences but are barely surfaced (foodSecurity ~1-2 files, hunger/fatigue ~1 file, hygiene 0). For the slice to deliver its living-world value, the player must read the chain: how much food exists, where it comes from (production / Corridor imports), where it goes (consumption), the current price, and what they can do about it (corridor work C7, brokerage C8).

## What
- A clear economy readout (extend the Ledger/dashboard or add an economy panel) showing: current food stock + derived foodSecurity, daily production vs consumption (net flow), Corridor status + import contribution, current food price + trend, and toll/income if the player holds that role.
- Surface the player's levers inline or via clear entry points: corridor-run contracts (C7) and brokerage/labor assignment (C8).
- Fix the ledger drift noted in the audit: the ledger selector currently re-implements tick math and omits several terms; this readout should derive from the SAME stock/flow/selectors as the simulation (single source of truth), not a parallel estimate.
- Respect existing visual/UX patterns; verify with Playwright MCP screenshots per CLAUDE.md.

## Why now
Comes after the numbers exist and react (6nga, ia9u) and ideally after the player levers (C7/C8) so it can link to them; it is the payoff that makes the whole slice felt.

## Player impact
The player finally sees cause and effect - a sealed Corridor, falling stock, rising prices, and the buttons to do something about it - turning the economy from invisible dials into a readable, actionable system.

## System impact
New/extended selectors driving an economy panel; remove the ledger's divergent re-implementation in favor of shared selectors (selectFoodSecurity/selectMarketPressure + flow selectors). UI-only logic in components; all view models from selectors. No new domain rules.

## Does not include
New economic mechanics (only surfacing existing ones); non-food goods; art/iconography pass (separate art-direction bead if needed).

## Evidence to ground the work
- docs/economy-system-audit-2026-06-14.md (weak surfacing of foodSecurity/needs; ledger drift - selectDailyIncomeBreakdown re-implements tick math and omits debt interest, corruption skim, prosperity/bond multipliers, faction grants, kitchen discount).
- src/application/selectors/ledger.ts (the drifting estimator to replace with shared selectors).
- destiny-6nga (selectFoodSecurity), destiny-ia9u (price + selectMarketPressure), destiny-pcbl (corridor flow), destiny-8ztm/destiny-fmz7 (production/consumption flows to display).
- CLAUDE.md UI/Playwright MCP workflow (screenshot verification).

## Architecture constraints
View models from selectors only; no business logic or tick-math re-implementation in UI. Single source of truth = the simulation's stock/flow + selectors. Verify visually with Playwright MCP.
acceptance_criteria: Player-facing lens: the player can see, in one place, current food stock + foodSecurity, daily production vs consumption, Corridor status and its import contribution, current food price + trend, and clear entry points to corridor work and brokerage; the readout matches what the simulation actually does. Behavioral lens: the panel's numbers derive from the same selectors as the tick (no parallel estimate); the ledger no longer diverges from actual tick math; selector tests confirm displayed values equal simulated values. Structural lens: economy view models come from selectors (selectFoodSecurity/selectMarketPressure/flow selectors), UI has no tick-math; bead labeled ui-ux; Playwright screenshots captured; lint/typecheck/test:run green.

title: Labor-brokerage operation: intake and place bound labor that feeds food production
description:
## Type
Implementation bead (player participation + revives dead bond logic). Connects the bond/captivity economy to production under epic destiny-dlud. Depends on destiny-8ztm (production consumes assigned labor). Coordinates with existing destiny-bw2h (Bond management UI) and destiny-lfxq (bond entry reasons). Narrative-heavy - Fiction Contract below. Label ui-ux.

## Why
narrative.md: labor-brokerage is how the great Houses (including the player's family) grew - and the player may embrace or refuse it. The audit found the entire bond/captivity economy (valuation, four buyers, transfer/sell/free/rescue, condition decay, moral-weather costs) is fully coded but DEAD - no UI door, bondStatus/captivityState rendered in zero files. This bead gives that machinery a purpose and a door: a brokerage operation that takes in bound labor and PLACES it into food production (destiny-8ztm), turning a moral choice into an economic input.

## What
- A brokerage loop the player operates: acquire/hold bound labor (reusing existing bond/captivity commands - freeNpc, transferBondedNpc, markNpcForSale (sets bondStatus.forSale), the rescue commands rescueBondedNpcLegal/Extraction/Force, setNpcCaptivityState - rather than rebuilding), assign bound NPCs as labor to producer agents (feeding destiny-8ztm output), and face the existing moral-weather/condition-decay costs of doing so.
- A player door (screen entry) for the brokerage: surface bondStatus/captivityState and the assign/place/sell/free actions - coordinate scope with destiny-bw2h (BondStatusPanel) and destiny-lfxq (createBond entry reasons) so we extend, not duplicate, them.
- Refusal path: the player can decline brokerage entirely and staff production with paid/willing labor instead, at different economics - so the moral choice is real and mechanically weighted.
- Determinism via rngSeed for any brokerage offers/variance (checkBondAcquisitionOffers already seeded).

## Why now
Highest narrative-per-line value in the epic: it revives already-built, tested logic (cheap) and ties it to the new production flow, delivering the living-world + moral-weight pillar. Needs C3 (production consumes labor) to exist first.

## Player impact
The player confronts the game's core moral economy: grow the house on bound labor (efficient, corrosive, with visible human cost and condition decay) or refuse it (cleaner, costlier). Either way the choice now drives real food output, not an abstract stat.

## System impact
Wire existing bond/captivity reducers to UI + assign-to-production; bridge bound NPCs to producer labor in destiny-8ztm; surface bondStatus/captivityState (currently 0 UI files). No greenfield bond logic - mostly wiring + a labor-assignment bridge + UI. Coordinate with destiny-bw2h/destiny-lfxq.

## Does not include
A visitable holding LOCATION/geography (that is a separate captivity-place bead in a later phase); rebuilding bond valuation/buyers (exists); non-food labor; full captivity-place UX beyond surfacing status + actions.

## Fiction Contract
Tone: bound labor is depicted with weight and consequence, never as frictionless resource - condition decay (healthy->hurt->broken->altered), moral-weather costs, and NPC reactions make the human cost legible. The brokerage is presented as the historically normal-but-corrosive foundation of House power, and refusal is a respected, mechanically viable stance. No glamorisation; outcomes and aftermath stay visible per the project's aftermath principle. Aligns with existing bond/captivity fiction and the content-policy boundaries.

## Evidence to ground the work
- docs/economy-system-audit-2026-06-14.md Gap 1 (built-but-dead bond/captivity reducer list) + Gap 2 (abstract without a place).
- src/application/commands/bondTransfer.ts, bondService.ts; data/definitions/bond-buyers.json; captivityRegistry.ts; applyAbstractCustodySimulation.ts (existing machinery to wire). For-sale is set by the reducer markNpcForSale (writes bondStatus.forSale) - there is NO setBondForSale symbol.
- destiny-bw2h (BondStatusPanel UI) and destiny-lfxq (createBond entry reasons) - coordinate, do not duplicate.
- destiny-8ztm (production labor input this brokerage feeds).
- bd memory architect-review-2026-06-01 (bonds read-only from relationships; intimacyStage ownership boundary - respect it).

## Architecture constraints
Reuse existing bond/captivity commands; new code is labor-assignment bridge + UI wiring. Business rules in application/commands; UI dispatches gameActions only. Deterministic (rngSeed). Respect the relationships/intimacy ownership boundary from the architect-review memory.
acceptance_criteria: Player-facing lens: the player can run a brokerage - take in/hold bound labor, place it into food production for higher output, see its human cost (condition decay, moral-weather, NPC reaction), sell/free it, OR refuse brokerage and staff with paid labor at different economics; bondStatus/captivityState are now visible. Behavioral lens: assigning bound NPCs raises producer output per destiny-8ztm; existing bond/captivity commands drive state (no reimplementation; for-sale via markNpcForSale); refusal path yields a valid alternative economics; brokerage offers/variance deterministic for a fixed seed; tests cover assign->production effect, moral cost application, and the refusal path. Structural lens: existing bond/captivity reducers reused and surfaced (extending destiny-bw2h/destiny-lfxq, not duplicating), labor bridge in application/commands, UI dispatches gameActions only; bead labeled ui-ux; Fiction Contract honored; lint/typecheck/test:run green.

title: Player corridor work: take corridor-run contracts that import food for pay
description:
## Type
Implementation bead (player participation). Wires the player as an actor into the Corridor import flow under epic destiny-dlud. Depends on destiny-pcbl (corridor flow + reopening) and the expedition system. UI bead - label ui-ux.

## Why
The user's vision: the player can earn as an adventurer by working the Corridor (one of the ways the great Houses, including the player's family, can grow). C6 (destiny-pcbl) makes the Corridor a real import flow and gives it a reopening path; this bead gives the PLAYER a door into it - take a corridor-run contract (an expedition) that, on success, imports food into stock and pays the player (and contributes to keeping/reopening the Corridor).

## What
- Add corridor-run contracts surfaced through the existing expedition/quest pipeline: a player-chosen action that dispatches roster NPCs on a Corridor run.
- On resolution: success adds food to city stock (and/or the player house stash) + pays Marks + nudges corridor status toward open / accrues toll share; failure has real cost (risk, injury, lost time) consistent with expedition outcomes.
- Make it optional and repeatable, not forced - the player can also ignore the Corridor and rely on production/brokerage or let scarcity bite.
- Deterministic outcomes via rngSeed through the expedition resolver.

## Why now
This is the first player-facing payoff of the slice (visible agency over the central economic threat). It needs C6's flow + reopening to exist first; it feeds C9's readout and C10's scenarios.

## Player impact
The player can act on the food crisis directly: run the Corridor for pay and supply, influence whether it stays open, and feel the risk/reward of adventuring - turning an abstract dial into a chosen, consequential activity.

## System impact
New corridor-run contract type wired into expedition/quest dispatch + resolution; outcome routes food import (via destiny-pcbl flow), Marks payout, and corridor-status nudge/toll. UI entry on the relevant screen (expedition/quests/corridor). Deterministic via rngSeed.

## Does not include
The underlying corridor flow/reopening (C6); brokerage/production labor (C8); non-food imports; new combat systems (reuses expedition resolution).

## Evidence to ground the work
- destiny-pcbl (corridor import flow, toll, reopening - this bead is the player door into it).
- src/application/commands (expedition economy is wired end-to-end per the audit - reuse its dispatch/resolution).
- docs/narrative.md section 11 + Corridor (adventuring/expedition-coalition framing).
- docs/economy-system-audit-2026-06-14.md (expedition economy = green, safe to extend).

## Architecture constraints
Reuse expedition resolution; no new RNG source (rngSeed). Business rules in application/commands, not in the screen. UI dispatches via gameActions only.
acceptance_criteria: Player-facing lens: the player can choose a corridor-run contract from a screen, send NPCs, and on success see food imported + Marks paid + the Corridor nudged toward open; failure shows real cost; the option is clearly optional and repeatable. Behavioral lens: corridor-run dispatch + resolution is deterministic for a fixed seed; success imports food into stock and pays Marks and nudges corridor status/toll; failure applies expedition-consistent costs; tests cover success and failure outcomes. Structural lens: contract wired through the expedition pipeline (no bespoke combat), outcome composes with destiny-pcbl's flow, UI entry dispatches gameActions only with no business logic in the component; bead labeled ui-ux; lint/typecheck/test:run green.

title: Backlog hygiene: add missing required sections to 6 pre-existing beads flagged by bd lint
description:
## Why
bd lint reports 6 open pre-existing beads with missing required sections: destiny-vdb0 (bug, no Steps to Reproduce), destiny-hx4e (epic, no Success Criteria), destiny-bw2h / destiny-lfxq / destiny-rjyy (no Acceptance Criteria), destiny-dbyw (epic, no Success Criteria). Per bead-creation.md a bead that fails bd lint is not shippable; these predate the 2026-06-12 backlog pass, which left them untouched deliberately to avoid scope creep.

## What
Each of the six beads gains its missing section with substantive content derived from its existing description (no invented scope): reproduction steps for vdb0 from its own Why, success criteria for the two epics from their child lists, acceptance criteria for the three features from their What/Player-impact.

## Why now
bd preflight noise hides new lint regressions; clean baseline makes the lint gate meaningful for the event/journal rework beads.

## Player impact
None (backlog hygiene).

## System impact
Bead descriptions only; no code, no content.

## Does not include
- no scope changes to the six beads
- no re-prioritization
- no closing or superseding

## Evidence
bd lint output 2026-06-12 (6 warnings); bead-creation.md enforcement section.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Player-visible: n/a.
2. System/technical: bd lint reports zero template warnings on open issues.
3. Tests/verification: bd lint run recorded in this bead before closure.

## Finding coverage
Session retro 2026-06-12 (ki-retro), backlog-hygiene finding.
notes: DELEGATION: YELLOW — sections must be derived from each bead's existing content without inventing scope; if a bead's description is too thin to derive acceptance honestly, flag it with bd human instead of padding.
close_reason: added missing sections: Success Criteria for hx4e/dbyw epics, Acceptance Criteria for bw2h/lfxq/rjyy features; bd lint now passes with 0 warnings

title: Trigger conditions: timeSlot and NPC-state thresholds for state-reactive events
description:
Trigger conditions: timeSlot and NPC-state thresholds for state-reactive events

## Implementation Status: COMPLETE
- Added timeSlot condition to eventTriggerConditionsSchema
- Added npcState array condition (with min/max bounds per axis)

## Blocked By:

The schema and code changes are complete and tested. The bead cannot be closed until its P1 blockers are resolved.
